user_comment: Looking for a chic capri pant? These black and white are made of a jean material. They don’t feel cheap at all. They are a nice wide leg that falls right at my ankle and look great with a sandal or mule. They’re not see through and they have some stretch to them. Despite being denim material, they are soft. The pockets are functional and fashionable. I ordered a size Small and had no problem sliding them on and off, there’s actually plenty of room - I’m 5’2” and 130lbs for reference.

These strike a nice balance between structure and comfort. The 98% cotton blend feels substantial—not thin or cheap—while the 2% elastane provides just enough give for sitting and moving easily. The high-waisted cut and wide leg create a very specific retro silhouette that works well for summer. The stitching on the rear patch pockets is clean and straight. Note that the 27" inseam is a true crop; on taller frames, these will definitely hit above the ankle.

I love these pants! I typically wear a small and I found these to be perfectly sized and proportioned. The waist hits -- and fits -- just right. And just as I'd expect from high-waisted pants. I'm 5'6". The wide cropped pant legs are perfect and I love how they look in a variety of footwear. I can dress them up or down. I like the trendy look; I can wear these pants to work with a nice top and shoes and not feel at all inappropriately dressed for my job (sales). The cotton demin has just the right amount of stretch. The fabric is very nice. The construction is good and looks like it'll hold together without issues.

Vetinee Women’s Oversized Button Up Frayed Hem Shacket Long Sleeve Pockets Denim Jean Jacket I bought in two colors [almond brown & black] and two sizes [small & medium]. I followed the size chart and, according to it, a medium would fit me best because i fall around an 8/10. I am 5'2, 160 lbs, and have an (out of shape with some mom pudge) average size frame. I was even considering sizing up to a L but figured I'd give the M a try first. The medium (almond color) fits okay lengthwise in terms of shacket style but the sleeves were too long and when I was wearing it, I felt like I looked like I was borrowing my husbands clothes. It looked VERY oversized and did not seem to be a proper fit. I tried it on with just a tshirt and also with a lightweight hoodie. I reordered in a small (black color) and that fit was MUCH better even though it said small was for sizes 4/6. I tried it on with a tshirt and with a lightweight hoodie and it fit well with both. The sleeve length and overall length were great and the overall fit was much better. Still felt like it looked like an oversized denim jacket because of the longer length but without looking baggy which is what I was looking for. I hate the short look of traditional denim jackets so if you want that kind of fit and are between sizes, I'd say size down or at the very minimum, just try your normal size. Definitely do not size up. Buying your regular size will give you the oversized look. Buying the size down will give you a regular fit but longer length denim jacket (similar to how the model pics look)

These are very comfortable jeans that have a bit of stretch to them. They don't come in numerical sizes so I took a guess and ordered them in a size S because I am between size S and size M right now. There is enough stretch in the jeans that ordering the smaller size worked out well because the jeans fit perfectly. The legs are quite a bit wider than shown in the photos on the model, so these are true wide-leg jeans. The jeans have 5 pockets, 4 of which are very decent-sized pockets considering they are women's jeans. They are comfortable and flattering. I am 5'4" and the jeans are the perfect length on me, so they will likely run short on you if you're taller. It's hard to find good jeans for a decent price, but these are definitely worth the lower than average price.

Levi's®, Women's, 501 '90s I ordered the 90s 501 Verified Vintage, and they were instantly comfortable - like “melt into them” comfortable. Ordered size 31 (usually I’m size 30) I’m 5’9”, 152lbs. I sized up because in the past my Levi’s have needed a break-in period, and I wanted more of a relaxed, boyfriend-jean fit from day one. My only real complaint is the length: the inseam wasn’t listed in the description and there’s no option to choose it. When they arrived, the tag showed a 30” inseam. I’m 5’9” and my ideal inseam is 33” (I usually buy 34” so I have extra length and can hem if needed). Even with that, I’m still keeping them because the comfort is that good. They’re soft, button-fly, and have that classic vintage, already-broken-in feel. I genuinely didn’t want to take them off. I’m just going to lean into the shorter length on purpose - cute socks and Birks, lounging, errands, all of it. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 4 stars only because the inseam wasn’t advertised.

Levi's®, Women's, 501 '90s Overall: Finally a true midrise from Levi’s. Classic mid 90’s fit. Sizing: I’m 5’2”, 115lbs, very short torso, long legs, pear shaped. I ordered the 26 and I probably could have gone with a 25 but with no stretch I prefer to size up between sizes. Well fitted waist with a relaxed fit through the hips and thighs. Rise: The rise hits about an inch below my belly button, which is typical for my short torso in midrise. Length: The length is perfect. I am only 5’2” but I have disproportionately long legs. If regular length usually fits you well, these should too.

My first review but I feel like I used these reviews a ton to get my right size. So I want to share mine stats to help others! I am 5’5, 126/127lbs, 27.5inch waist (smallest part), 36hips, and thighs are about 20.5/21 inch around largest part (3 inch’s down from crotch). The size 4 fits me great with not feeling like I squeezed myself in. The thighs are the tightest on me but I can tell the fabric is going to stretch out after an hr. The length is just right not long at all, as you can see in the picture it wearing tennis shoes. The fabric isn’t “stretchy” but it has give it’s not stuck in one spot. It’s a thinner material. Overall I’m glad I stuck with my normal size 4. If you have bigger thighs may want to size up otherwise stick to normal size I hope this helps!

These jeans nailed the wide leg look perfectly, and they have just the right amount of bagginess without being too oversized. I love that they are fitted in all the right areas. The denim material has a bit of stretch to it, which I really appreciate. The elastic waistband is super comfortable too. I love that you can just pull these jeans on without having to fuss with zippers or buttons. They come with four usable pockets, plus an extra little pocket inside one of the front pockets. I got the black distressed wash, and I'm obsessed with the edgy, cool look it gives the jeans. The seam detailing down the front of the legs is another nice added detail. The Medium fit me great, though maybe just a tad loose. If you're in between sizes, you may want to consider sizing down for a more snug fit. For reference, I'm a size 8, 5'6", and 145 lbs. I will say the length is a tad long on me, but I think that's intentional so you can pair them with heels or boots. Overall, these wide leg jeans are super flattering, stylish, and comfy. I can already tell I'll be wearing them a ton thanks to that perfectly wide leg silhouette.

I really love Luvamia so I was excited to see these pink pants come out just in time for Valentine’s Day! Like most Luvamia bottoms, these fit true to size - I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 161# body. The material on these isn’t the typical Luvamia denim - they are somewhere between a denim and a chino. Like maybe a soft, lightweight denim. My only complaint - and this is true with a lot of Luvamia - is that there’s a little extra material at the flap at the top of the zipper. I tried to show this in my photos where I am wearing the heart sweater. It’s just below the button at the waist and it kind of makes it look like the pants are too tight when really it’s just extra material. I haven’t tried to iron it more flat. That might help temporarily but I think when you keep sitting/standing/moving it will just pop out again. Outside of that minor flaw, I do really love these pants. The pink is Cherry and just what I expected based on the photo in the ad. The length is really nice - I felt comfortable wearing these out even though it’s winter here and very chilly outside. The pockets are flattering on the front and are functional as well. The legs have just a bit of a flare on them, making the thighs look a little slimmer. Like all Luvamia, these pants do relax a bit after wearing, so if you are really between sizes, you can size down knowing that they may be a little tight when you put them on but they will loosen up. It’s been my experience that even after washing and drying, Luvamia never gets as tight as it was when you first get them. These are definitely great for the price and if you can get them on sale, even better.

I really love Luvamia so I was excited to see these pink pants come out just in time for Valentine’s Day! Like most Luvamia bottoms, these fit true to size - I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 161# body. The material on these isn’t the typical Luvamia denim - they are somewhere between a denim and a chino. Like maybe a soft, lightweight denim. My only complaint - and this is true with a lot of Luvamia - is that there’s a little extra material at the flap at the top of the zipper. I tried to show this in my photos where I am wearing the heart sweater. It’s just below the button at the waist and it kind of makes it look like the pants are too tight when really it’s just extra material. I haven’t tried to iron it more flat. That might help temporarily but I think when you keep sitting/standing/moving it will just pop out again. Outside of that minor flaw, I do really love these pants. The pink is Cherry and just what I expected based on the photo in the ad. The length is really nice - I felt comfortable wearing these out even though it’s winter here and very chilly outside. The pockets are flattering on the front and are functional as well. The legs have just a bit of a flare on them, making the thighs look a little slimmer. Like all Luvamia, these pants do relax a bit after wearing, so if you are really between sizes, you can size down knowing that they may be a little tight when you put them on but they will loosen up. It’s been my experience that even after washing and drying, Luvamia never gets as tight as it was when you first get them. These are definitely great for the price and if you can get them on sale, even better.

This pair of wide leg, cropped jeans are well made. The fabric content is 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. They are between medium and thin for thickness, so they should be comfortable going into summer. The material is soft and has a good amount of stretch in it for extra comfort. They have 5 pockets; 2 front pockets, a small watch-pocket in the upper part of one of the front ones, and 2 back pockets. The front pockets are a little small, but still functional for a chap-stick, tissue, or keys. The waist has belt loops for those who like to or need to wear a belt. The button/zipper fastenings work smoothly without stiffness or snags. I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular size for jeans. They fit very comfortably around my waist and there is no pinching thanks to the stretch in the fabric. I'm on the short side, so they are closer to being a normal length for me, than looking cropped. I'm fine with the length as often the problem for me is that jeans are too long instead! For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. with an apple shaped midsection. These are good quality jeans and I definitely will recommend them. They're so comfy that it makes it easy to wear them all day. There are 4 different shades of blue available, along with black, white, and hot pink. I really like the Cobalt Blue shade that I chose. The current price (2-7-2026) of $35.99 is a fair value for them, in my opinion. Currently, there is a coupon available to reduce the price and make them a good value.

I'm only 5'4", but these don't look very cropped on me. I'm used to things being longer than they are on the average person, but this really doesn't look cropped at all. I ordered my usual size, and these are just a little tighter through the hip than I expected. I was hoping to wear them with shorter tops, but with that snugger fit now I don't really want to. I almost wish I'd got up a size, but with the length problem I'm not sure it's worth it. Other than the unexpected fit, the denim is moderate weight and comfortable to wear. There's real pockets on the front and back, which is nice. Overall, the quality is good, I just had issues with the fit.

I have been wanting to try a pair of wide leg cropped pants for a while. I am typically a size 12-14 L and these pants run true to size. At 5'7", the waist sits right at my waist and the pants stop just above my ankles. The fabric is thick enough to feel durable but is not heavy. The pants are stretchy and comfortable to sit in. There are pockets in the front and the back. As for the shape, I have been a skinny jean person for a very long time. They are comfortable, stylish, and look good when I have them on. It will take a little time getting used to the wide leg trend.

EGOCKAL Women's Wide Leg Jeans Trendy High Waisted Stretchy Jeans with Drawstring Elastic Waist I absolutely love these jeans. They have been my favorite ones since I got them. So far I have wore them to two different family Christmas gatherings and I wore them to church several times. I have styled them different ways to make them look different with each outfit I put together. I am obsessed with these pants. I will probably get them in any other colors that they offer. I love them that much. I am only 5’4” tall so they are a little longer on me, and I have to wear some sort of a heel, even if it is a wedge heel with them. Just so I’m not walking on the bottoms of them, but I don’t mind, I like to wear heels. You can dress these pants up or down, they look cute with everything I’ve paired them with. They are seriously the cutest pants ever. They are just the right amount of stretch, the material quality is excellent, the entire quality of these pants is excellent. they are so flattering on, the style and the design everything about them. I’m really trying hard to not want to put them on tomorrow for Christmas day so I’m not wearing the same pants in just about every picture from every Christmas party but I really want to wear them again on Christmas day because I love them so much.

As a big and tall girl (5'10" and 195 lbs), I struggle with the trend of full-legged jeans. Most of them just make me look like a clown. But I was very pleasantly surprised by this style. They are snug at the hips, waist, and thighs, which creates a more streamlined look, while still maintaining fullness at the calves and ankles for the trendy appearance. I thought I might have to let the cuff out to get the length I need, but the length is actually perfect. They look like the famous Halara stretch jeans, but almost certainly sturdier, and a better value. While they are a nice, thick denim, they have quite a bit of stretch and are very comfortable. As recommended by other reviews, I went one size down from the size chart, and they fit perfectly. This photo is straight out of the shipping bag so I apologize for the fold wrinkles, but ignore those because they came right out after a few minutes of wearing. I am very pleased that I took a chance on these jeans. My husband also said they look great!
I really like the fit of the pants, however, they are a bit longer than expected. I am 5’4” and they are regular length, not cropped.
Extremely comfortable and stretchy jeans. I ordered my usual size of a 6 and they fit me great. I’m 5’4” and they were definitely long but I plan to cut them at the bottom for a raw edge.

These jeans are very comfortable to me. I originally ordered them for my daughter but she found them too tight in the behind (but to be honest she’s packing in the trunk). I think they could be a little tighter in the waist because it feels like there’s a gap at my back, but it isn’t too bad. They come up to just above my belly button. I’m only 5’ 2” so they’re a bit long on me and I’m not sure I can pull off this look. My regular jeans are more fitted so I felt like I was wearing a circus tent around my calves. They don’t look bad in the mirror though and they do fit nicely. The material feels soft and it has a bit of stretch to it. I thought the seams down the front would be annoying but I actually don’t notice them. The pockets are a decent size too. Overall I think these are pretty good for the price.

These jeans look nothing like the picture. Also, the size chart is completely off! I ordered a 2 Long which the chart states it’s a 33 inch inseam. The pants I received are a 2 Long but the inseam is literally 27.5 inches. This is the second time I’ve ordered these because the first pair had the same exact issue. I thought it was a mix up at first and returned them only it order again and it’s still the same issue with the inseam being totally incorrect. Please fix your size chart.

I really want to love these jeans. They are comfortable. The size fits my waist just right. They are snug without being too tight. I ordered them in “short” length, but they are much too long. Even with higher soled/healed shoes I nearly trip on them. I am 5’1”. Short sizes are typically just right for me. Let’s talk about the width. These say “flare” or “boot cut” in the description. The picture shows them fitted to the knee with a moderate flare to the ankle. However, that is not actually the case. These take me back to the super wide style of the late ‘90s. They start to flare out at the hip. The material is comfortable with just enough stretch. And the color is really nice. The dye did not even bleed in the wash.

These Lover Brand Fashion high waisted skinny jeans are pretty nice. The jeans have a nice thickness to them so they're warm but they have a firm stretch so they aren't as comfortable as my other jeans. I'm a size 27 so I ordered the medium and they are a bit tight so I'd size up if you're in between sizes. The 29" inseam is longer than average but slightly short on me at 5'11". The color and wash is really nice and these are good value jeans for the quality.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice but runs a bit small

user_comment: My tween daughter is a fashionista. Skinny jeans are coming back in style for the kids... (Heck, I've never stopped wearing them!) So when I saw this pair of LOVER BRAND FASHION Women's Jeans, I knew she would want to try them. I ordered them in the (Medium, Black) version and they fit her great. For reference she is 5'4ish, 135ish pounds and wears a juniors size medium from a big box store. Here's what we know about these jeans: •Excellent quality garment, free of defects •Made of medium thick, very stretchy denim-like material •Wrinkle resistant material that is easy wear right out of the dryer •High waisted cut is figure flattering •Buttons and zipper work correctly •Nice true black color •Versatile-- can be styled a variety of different ways •A fantastic value for the money Overall, this pair of high waisted jeans is fabulous! They are definitely worth a spot in your wardrobe, especially at a little less than $23! They wash up great, are comfortable, and easy to throw on. MOM & TWEEN APPROVED! HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!

These high waisted skinny jeans are an amazing quality. The fit is very flattering. The high rise provides good tummy control without feeling stiff or restrictive. The stretch in the denim makes them comfortable enough for all day wear while still holding their shape. I like that they strike a nice balance between snug and flexible. They contour well through the legs without feeling overly tight, and the fabric smooths rather than clings. The black color looks sleek and versatile, making them easy to dress up with heels or keep casual with flats or sneakers. After a wash, the jeans have held up well with minimal fading, which is a big plus for black denim. The material isn’t super thick, but it feels durable enough for regular wear. It’s true to size. I wish I ordered a small instead of medium. I wanted a looser fit but since it has a very good stretch to it small would’ve been better. I normally wear a small. I’m 5,4 110lbs Overall, these are solid not see through everyday skinny jeans with a flattering high waist, good stretch, and reliable quality for the price. Definitely a great staple piece for casual wear and easy styling.

These LOVER BRAND FASHION high waisted skinny jeans are really well made with sturdy, thick denim that feels high quality and durable. The dark wash looks sleek and versatile for casual wear. They look fantastic in the product photos (and probably on most people!), but unfortunately, they didn't fit me quite right, I gained a bit more weight this year than I realized, so they were a tad too snug. No fault of the jeans though; I just need to go up a size next time or lose the weight which is my plan. Thankfully, my home gym is getting set up again, and these will be my motivation pair, the first jeans I slip into once I'm back in shape! One thing I can on is I've always had a hard time finding jeans that were long enough, I'm 5'7" with most my height in the legs so pants are usually to short, these are perfect in length. Highly recommend for anyone looking for sturdy, flattering skinny jeans. Just double-check the sizing chart! 5 stars, because the product itself is excellent; the fit issue is on me!

I'm not even deducting a star for that (to me) odd-looking hem. For all I know, that's perfectly in style nowadays. I do know that I love these jeans. Except for the just-enough stretch, these look and feel identical to the "original" big-name jeans I wore for decades, and will give me classic casual style along with any-season wear. The big difference? With these jeans, no break-in period needed! I have a thought that perhaps they fit so well on me is because I have what I call my washboard-flat rear end, but honestly? I think there's enough stretch to make room for all shapes that way, as long as the overall sizing is right. There is one other difference. I am 5'4", and apparently most manufacturers nowadays are making pants and my preferred ankle-length skirts for taller women than that. Very frustrating, because it seems like every new skirt or pants I've acquired this year needs hemming. When I saw these long capris, I thought maybe they'd be on the long side too and I could circumvent that dreary chore. To a point, I was correct. On me they're not TRUE full length, but happily, the hem on these capri pants falls just above my ankle. Long enough for me without another hemming job, and for my taller sisters, these should fit as true capris. These do wash out to a moderately weathered appearance, so if you're looking for jeans that look crisp and new, these won't do. (Photo is after washing.) If you're looking for classic, comfortable, all-season jeans, I highly recommend these.

Absolutely adore these!!! I am 5’4 and love the smidge above ankle length on me. Love the shade of the denim. EXTREMELY comfortable, they are a very stretchy material!! True to size but generous loose fit in my option so maybe size down if in between. I got my usual XL and was pleased with the comfy fit. Really impressed and highly recommend!!

First on the size - When I ordered, the sizing in the add seemed odd or possibly in CM vs INCHs, but the sizing if you scrolled down into the ad had me at a medium, but I ordered a large to be safe. I am typically a 12/14 or a L/XL. The L is small on me, so much so that I cannot button them, however they fit in the seat. I think an XL would have been nicely snug. So I would say the L is a 10. I am also pear shaped.The length on me is right at the ankle, which I think is proper for the style. I am 5' 6" and a 31 inseam.The color is about a perfect day blue and the wear lines are a nice touch, but I probably prefer none. Hasn't this fad died yet? I personally think no wear lines or rips if you want to look put together in higher quality clothing.This is a great barrel leg but size up or use my specifics to help you size.

Ordered medium, dark blue. For reference: 5’ tall, 155 lbs. short chubby. They had a weird smell. Popped them in the washer/dryer. They shrunk a bit. They have an elastic waist in the back for stretch. But there is no button/zipper so it was snug pulling them on over my hips. I like the dark blue color and the fabric is good quality. What I don’t like are the large back pockets. Not flattering at all. Being a short girl I’ll have to hem them a bit. I may still wear them with an oversized hoodie. Something to cover the butt and weird pockets. For $30 they’re reasonably priced. I wouldn’t recommend them if you have a thicker belly/thighs. The waistband is snug! Stretched to the max.

With that said, the fabric is nice and denim is thinner than standard but very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric and the style of them, especially the high waist, flat front, and elastic in the back.

These are not jeans material, or cut. First off, they are pull pn style with a very elastic waist on the back. Secondly, im too tall for these. I normally wear low on my hips to allow for some extra length in pants, but the cut of these makes them awkward looking when I do. And you can see in the pics, even when worn low they are short on me. If you are petite, I think these are perfect. Anything over 5'7" will probably struggle. Sizing up isnt really the answer, as the elastic back just creates a saggy look from behind, and there seems to be a slight flare shape cut into the pants thay makes them look off when not worn at the waist.

Take me back to my mid-90's flares, but with style! I absolutely love these jeans! There are no zippers or buttons, just elastic in the rear and a flat front. Side pockets are perfect, deep enough, and back pockets exist. I love the wide hemmed pant legs - so cute. For me, at 5'4'' they are slightly long, so far I've had to wear them only with heeled boots (low profile sneakers have them dragging on the ground). I find them super comfortable as well as stylish. I normally wear a size 12/14 jeans and these XL fit me perfectly! They are medium weight fabric, no stretch except in the waistband. Seem to be good quality and worth the price. I want to wear them every day!

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans After some recent health issues, I lost weight suddenly. And with that, I adopted a pancake butt. It's been hard finding pants that fit in the rear, leg AND waist. I'm 5'6 about 115, and the 1 Long were about 4-5 inches too long. So I'll have to cut and hem them. But, it's nice to wear something that doesn't sag. The material is a decent thickness, zipper seems legit, pockets are fair size for jeans. Unsure about shrinking.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans I ordered a pair of 5S red pants and they fit absolutely perfectly, and they are so comfortable— for that review I give 5 stars… so I really wanted a white pair, but they only make the short lengths for a few colors. Since most brands don’t vary the other measurements in their sizes—only the length—I ordered a regular length size 5 in white. I was so disappointed I am apple shaped and everything I gain goes straight to my upper body and none of it goes to my hips or legs. Currently I fit in the healthy weight range for my BMI, but I know I look better when I was 10 pounds thinner. After a few years of fighting it I’m trying to just accept it and buy new clothes. here are my horrifying measurements— bust 38, waist 31 (ok probably 32 at night) hips 34– no joke— my hips are currently barely 3 inches wider than my fat waist. So when I was 10 pounds thinner my measurements were 35, 28, 34– so I’m the worst apple ever where nothing ever goes to my hips or butt. If jeans are low rise I can wear size 2, but high rise jeans at this weight I have to wear 6-8 or I can’t breath. Problem is that when I get those sizes they billow with extra fabric on my hips and butt and make me look awful. That’s why I was so excited the red 5S fit so well. But the white regular length size pinched my middle completely even though they were still midrise. So I returned them and ordered size 7 and they still pinched but not as bad as the 5, only they were ridiculously long and more baggy in the hip than I like. Maybe my red pair of size 5S is a fluke. I don’t know cuz I don’t need or want the other colors they sell in that size. If the company reads these, please make more colors (tons of women love white jeans!) of the short size…assuming they’re the same measurements as my perfect stretchy red pair shown in pics. My rating of 3 stars is due to not being able to get an awesome fit in a different color. Sorry I didn’t do all the measurements for the white pairs before I sent back.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans I'm 5'4, 165 lbs. roughly 42 hip, 36 waist (my belly sticks out a little, working on it). I'm a size 15 in pants, so depending on the stitching and material, I normally wear a 14-16 jean. I bought the 14 plus darkwash jeans and they are beautiful. The color is lovely and they fit great. They look AMAZING. They fit a little tight in the waist/hip area, which I expected. A 16 would have been too big/baggy and would have defeated the purpose. Since I'm losing weight anyway, I don't mind if they're a little tight. They fit like a glove and are super skinny all the way to the ankle. My favorite part is that they stretched really well for me. I thought I was going to pull them up and find that the hips/waist didn't fit, but when I went to button them, they stretched exactly where I needed it. They make everything look firm, with a clean silhouette and no panty lines. I'm ordering a few more pairs because it's hard to find true skinny jeans anymore. I've only worn them once so far, but they seem really well made and I haven't found any loose strings. They look great with boots! UPDATE: I bought 4 more pairs in different colors. I love them all. The black jeans are solid black (no fading anywhere) and slightly thinner, but still perfect. The light wash are a little more blue than I expected -- I was thinking it would be more like the super light washes of the 80s, but it's more of a light sky blue. I'll try to add a clear picture of them next to the black and dark wash for comparison soon.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans Fit great and look awesome. I weigh 110 and I'm 5'4" tall. I have no butt. These pants give my flat butt a nice lift. They are very soft. The purple is a beautiful color. I have washed them multiple times in cold water and dried them on normal dry. They are still like new.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans I’m so excited to have found somewhere that sells mid-rise skinny jeans. It’s been so hard to find jeans that fit in the era of high-rise (I’m 5’2 with a short torso, and all the jeans in stores go up to my chest or armpits). I love the fit of these jeans, and the color is so vivid! I ordered true to size, and while the waist sits where it should and I can slip them on with no problem, the general style is more skintight than I expected. I could probably stick with this size and see if the jeans stretch out the longer you wear them, but I’m going to order a size up as well to see if it fits the same without being skintight. Update: I ordered the mint-green ones in a size up, and I also ordered the black ones one size up (both 9, while my original order was 7). The different colors definitely fit differently! The black size 9s fit perfectly, so I’m keeping them for sure. The green size 9s actually ended up being too big. The legs fit almost as tight as the 7s, but the crotch was noticeably loose. I’m returning the 9 and keeping the original green size 7 I ordered. So if you’re thinking of ordering different colors, I’d suggest ordering two different sizes and returning whichever doesn’t fit. New pictures are attached with size 9’s labeled. I’m very happy with these jeans now that I’ve found the correct size of each!

I’m returning the 9 and keeping the original green size 7 I ordered. So if you’re thinking of ordering different colors, I’d suggest ordering two different sizes and returning whichever doesn’t fit. New pictures are attached with size 9’s labeled. I’m very happy with these jeans now that I’ve found the correct size of each!

Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Capri Pants My daughter is normally a size 4 at 117 lbs and 5'5, and these size 4 fit her tts. Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Capri Pants I was pleasantly surprised by these pants. The inseam says 20 inches. Being that I'm only 4'11 I was hoping that they would fit more like a wide leg crop instead of a capris. They fit just as I was hoping!! I'm about 140lbs and usually wear size 10 and the sizing was spot on. The material seems to be nice quality with a little stretch! I would recommend and plan to order another color.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans Ok the important thing first; I'm 5'7", 138lbs and usually wear a comfortable "29", or 6-8, in jeans. I'm a pretty solid medium, sometimes a large, never a small. I got a size 8 in these jeans and they're definitely too big. The size chart has the 8 at a 29.5-30.5 and the 6 is 28.5-29.5, so i think me getting an 8 is my bad lol. You'll see in the pics that I added a belt & it definitely helps? But they're still super big. I do think if you're in between sizes, it's safe to size down in these!Moving on, they're DEFINITELY baggie and super soft. If you get the right size I think they'd be so cute for a baggie, comfy, casual look. The only thing I don't like is how LONG they are though, and I'm decently tall. But again, sizing down and wearing them a bit above the midrise length they're advertised as may fix that too.The price is also a plus. A nice, comfy pair of stylish baggie jeans for under 30 bucks is a great deal i think! I'll probably come back & buy the size 6 soon because they are so comfortable and affordable.ps- I didnt realize my mirror was so dirty please don't judge me lol. Have a great rest of your day!

No Nonsense Women's Knit Denim Straight Leg Legging Pull-on Pants When I'm at home, I avoid wearing anything that feels tight, though I do prefer fairly close-fitting clothing for ease of movement. These leggings are stretchy and soft. I'm between M & L sizes in pants, and since these will be mostly for wearing at home, I sized up. They turned out to be just the right size for me for comfort. The waist band keeps them in place without squeezing. The straight leg gives me some space, but doesn't get in my way. This loose fit of the size L on me doesn't offer much support, so I wouldn't wear it in public without a fairly long tunic over it. I'm not sure whether a size M would be any more supportive, considering the soft fabric, or just feel constricting around the waist while showing even more of the shapes I don't want to show. To me, this sized-up pair are perfect as lounge pants. The front pockets are just for decoration. The back pockets are usable, but not as deep as I'd like if I were to wear them while out doing errands. They are very wrinkle-resistant. For length reference, I'm 5'1", and I expect to need to hem about 5" from the cuff. I recommend these as lounge pants for all body types. I recommend them as ready-to-wear for taller women who aren't seeking support from their leggings. (Vine review)

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Wide Leg Jean Why did you pick this product vs others?: Amazing jeans, amazing fit, 100% cotton. I am SO HAPPY WITH THESE!!! (I am 5’2” & 130lb, size 4S fit like a glove) Fit: Perfect Material quality: Fantastic

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans I love wide-legged jeans, so when I came across these, I knew I had to try them. I began by choosing my size, which I could not get on until I laid on the bed to be able to zip them up. After getting up, however, I was really disappointed. I honestly don't understand who these jeans were made for because they are so incredibly long. I am average height at 5'4", but I wouldn't be surprised if these jeans were so long that even a woman over 6 feet may have a problem with the length. I should also note that the jeans come with a note letting you know that they may bleed in the wash and should be washed alone. I appreciated knowing that but can't understand why they were not prepped so that wouldn't happen. Needless to say, I will be washing them and having them cut and hemmed. That means that the price point was not a deal as I originally believed.

The overall sizing is accurate, and the pants are flattering and comfortable. Overall, very happy with this purchase.

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant I got these to wear to work, I work at the post office and so I got the navy blue color. Based off of the reviews I read about shrinking after washing, I went on ahead and bought the long and I am so glad I did. I am 5 foot 4 and I normally wear a regular length or a short. I washed and dried these pants and they shrunk about 4 inches. Other than that they are fantastic they have held up really well and I love how thick the fabric is but they are so soft and comfortable it feels like I'm wearing pajamas almost. I am including pictures of the pair that I washed and dried next to the ones that haven't been washed it so you can see the length difference. I don't know how they would do if they weren't put in a dryer if they would shrink or not, but I did put mine in the dryer and they are the perfect length for me now

I love love love these. I want more. I’m 5’8 156lbs order size medium. I absolutely love these ANRABESS wide leg jeans! The high-waisted design is very flattering and gives a nice silhouette without feeling tight or restrictive. The drawstring waist is a great feature because it lets you adjust the fit easily, which makes them super comfortable for all-day wear. The denim is soft and lightweight compared to traditional jeans, so they feel more like casual lounge pants while still looking stylish. The wide leg cut is trendy and relaxed, making them perfect to pair with sneakers, sandals, or even ankle boots. I’ve worn them running errands, traveling, and even to casual outings, and they always look put together. Another big plus is the pockets—they’re actually functional and deep enough to hold my phone and keys. If you’re looking for jeans that combine comfort with a modern, relaxed style, these are a great choice. I’ll definitely be buying another pair in a different wash! 👖✨

user_comment: Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Flare Jean Flattering and true to size. 5’1,121lb, 24waist. Color like picture. Also I like that the waist fits snug and that there is a bit of stretch in the thigh area. They don’t feel tight or restricting.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Flare Jean Flattering and true to size. 5’1,121lb, 24waist. Color like picture. Also I like that the waist fits snug and that there is a bit of stretch in the thigh area. They don’t feel tight or restricting.

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant I originally ordered this Lee Lux Comforte in the Navy in size 6. Amazon insisted 6 was the size for me. According to the size chart, it was close. I’m 5‘6“ tall. I weigh 135 to 140, waist is 29. hips are 38. I am also a little bit long-waisted. The waist of the 6 was OK. It was firm with not much give. However, the 6 was quite snug through the hips and thighs, and it made the pockets gap. I opted to return the 6 because these will primarily be for work and they were just slightly too small everywhere. I ordered the same slacks in size 8. The waist is still OK in the 8 a little bigger and a little looser, but I’ll still be able to wear them after Thanksgiving dinner. There is more room in the hips, but they still hug the top of the hip nicely before they fall to the wide leg. The leg in the 8 is loose at on my thighs, but the 6 was tight on my thighs. They are a bit longer. Unfortunately, they still make a funny wrinkle at the groin. This happens to me sometimes because I am slightly long-waisted. Because these are on sale for $16 and I really need navy slacks, I’m going to keep the 8. For anyone with a waist over 29, or hips more than 36, or generous thighs, or a long-waist, I think the 8 is a better choice. The fabric is not thin. It is a nice weight and it will be good for work. It might be warm on hot days.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans Update: I dropped a star because these collect lint like nobody’s business and I have to spend almost 10 minutes de-linting whenever I wear them. The second thing is that the back pocket is too skinny. I put a Mac mini in my back pocket at work and these are the first jeans it hasn’t fit in. Original review: I wouldn’t necessarily say these jeans are “soft”; it’s more that they are thin (and a little stretchy). They aren’t the thick, rough work pants some jeans feel like. I’m 5’6 and between a size 16 and 18. These size 16 jeans fit very comfortably in the waist and tummy, snug around the thighs, and then long enough to need hemmed at the bottom. They are very wide at the bottom, almost giving bell bottoms. Other than that, the fit is pretty typical to size 16 jeans I own from other brands. Everything is double stitched and finished off properly with backstitching. I don’t expect these jeans to fall apart at all.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Wide Leg Jean Took a chance and I love these. Fits on the hip and loose everywhere else as it should be! This is pretty relaxed, so if you like a snugger fit in the waist, then size down. I’m 5’2” and 125lbs and got a 27 short (US 4 Short), but could probably do a 2 short to be honest (historically I do recall Aeropostale running a little larger than other brands). I love the Sassafras color wash, it’s not too dark, but a classic deeper indigo, exactly what I hoped for. The tag does say 100% cotton, but there is stretch to the fabric. It’s not a stiff denim, but not a very thin one either, so it’s just right in my opinion.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans These jeans are definitely on the simple side — not super trendy, but if you’re looking for a basic wide-leg jean at a low price, they do the job. The legs are very wide, and the width stays the same from top to bottom, which gives them a boxy look. I’m 5'5", and they’re about 2–3 inches too long on me, so I’ll need to hem them or they’ll drag on the ground. The denim is a thicker, non-stretch material that feels decent quality for the price — not stiff, but not soft either. They’re not going to be my everyday go-to jeans, but they’ll work fine for casual wear or when I want something comfy and roomy. Overall, solid for the cost, just don’t expect a high-fashion fit.

No Nonsense Women's Knit Denim Straight Leg Legging Pull-on Pants I always have trouble getting real blue jeans to fit properly as a larger woman. When I saw these, I knew I had to give them a try. Right out of the package, your mind thinks "blue jean." From the color to the fit, they are perfect! I don't think even when someone is standing beside you, they won't think you have on regular jeans. I ordered these in an XXL and was really happy with the fit. They aren’t too tight at all and have a nice amount of stretch that stays comfortable throughout the day. I was especially impressed with how well they fit the curve of my back and hip without leaving an open gap at the waist. The item said it was a straight leg legging, but I found it to have a slight flare at the foot. I loved it. For reference, I am 5'7" and found that the length of these pants actually extends past my foot. I’ll need to hem them or wear them with a bit of a heel, but overall they are a great, no-fuss pair of pants for the shop or everyday wear. Value for the money, these are a keeper and I'd get one in every color!

Levi's Womens 314 Shaping Straight Jeans (Also Available in Plus) I'm 5'5 and about 210. 45-42-51 are my measurements. I got a size 37 plus and I'm normally I'm an 18/20 plus. These come up just above my belly button and are a bit loose honestly, but I expected that since the measurements for for these says they're 43". The band/seams dont have a lot of stretch but the denim does so for my bigger girlies I'd focus on your waist measurements and inseams more than anything else, I tried to show that in one of the pictures. As far as measurements go, these jeans seem to be true to size. I could have gone a size smaller for a tighter fit, but these are comfortable how they are now so I'll just wear them with a belt. The fabric is soft but seems durable enough, it definitely don't feel like rough denim. It's comfortable and I absolutely amazing not going to kind wearing them all day. The color is also accurate, but as you can see in the pictures, it really does depend on lighting. The pictures with my legs showing were taken in sunlight. These are my second pair of Levi's and I expect them to last a long time so I'd say they're worth the price as well.

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Wide Leg Jeans These jeans are cute and quite comfortable because of the slight stretch to the denim. And they're not too stretchy to the point that they feel stretched out by the end of the day. The style is cute, I love the pockets on the front and the seams above the back pockets, this adds a nice stylish touch. My one qualm with these jeans is that they are not full length as pictured in the listing. I am just under 5'6" and my legs are proportionate to my torso. I do not normally need to wear 'long' or 'tall' sized jeans. These could've definitely been longer. The cuff is sewn, so if I want to uncuff them to make them longer, I'll have to rip open the stitching, which isn't a huge stitch, but the pants could've just been a bit longer. I like the color and the fit overall. Again, they are comfortable too.

Lee Women's Legendary 26" Wide Leg Crop Jean I really wanted these jeans to work, but unfortunately, they didn’t. I typically wear a size 4P or 6P in Old Navy jeans, but like other reviewers mentioned, the sizing on these is a bit off. I ordered both size 8 and 10, and both felt tight in the waist w/ no stretch. Strangely, the waist on these jeans is about the same as my Old Navy 4P—so weird. The size 8 fit a little better in the hips, while the size 10 just looked big and wasn’t flattering. In the photo, I’ve stacked three pairs of jeans for comparison (from bottom to top: Old Navy 4P, Lee 8, and Lee 10). I wish they had worked—on my 4'11 petite frame, size 8 had the wide-leg look I was going for.

I love the fit and the weight of these. I would not wear them in extreme cold weather but they are perfect for spring and fall in my opinion. They have structure to them, not flimsy at all. Riders Womens Pull on Waist Smoother Boot Cut Jean I’m adding this info for future buyers because I myself like to read a lot of reviews before buying clothes lol.I’m 5’7” and fluctuate between 145lbs - 150lbs. I bought a size 8 (regular length). I’ve recently gained weight so I don’t know my true size right now, I just ordered one size up from what I used to be. I still fit in Free People size 28 jeans, if that’s of any help to anyone.These jeans fit comfortably enough BUT they’re also pretty snug so if they shrink in the dryer they probably won’t fit me anymore. I honestly think I could’ve gone with a size 10 in case they do end up shrinking.. I’m still considering exchanging them. I haven’t washed or worn them out yet, so I don’t know how well they’ll hold up. I will say that the material seems to be thicker and better quality than I expected. They have some stretch but not enough to be considered that weak jegging material (I don’t like), they actually feel like jeans. I also like the length, they’re not short and not too long, as you can see in the picture (I’m not wearing shoes). As for the rise of the waist, I'd say it's mid-rise because it sits about an inch below my belly button. They're definitely not low-rise on me, which I'm glad about because I hate low-rise.All in all, they seem like a pretty good pair of jeans, and if my first impression is correct, they’ll be sturdy and last a decent while.I hope my review helped someone out!

Riders Womens Pull on Waist Smoother Boot Cut Jean This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. It's pretty good, except I had to fold in the hem (so I will be fixing that myself, yup! even in petite size), but that is a minor thing) the rest of the fit is good. They are comfortable. If you're on the edge, definitely worth trying these since they're easy to return.

Jessica Simpson Womens Adored Curvy High Rise Skinny Jean Good quality, but very tight in the legs with very minimal stretch. Will have to return. A size up may be too big in the waist however. I'm 5'5 126 lbs pear shape. Wearing my normal size 27.

Jessica Simpson Womens Adored Curvy High Rise Skinny Jean Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Baggy Boyfriend Barrel Jeans I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Baggy Boyfriend Barrel Jeans I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.
reviewer_name_raw: Just not quite what I envisioned

user_comment: EVALESS Women's High Waisted Baggy Boyfriend Barrel Jeans I think these jeans are cute and they are crazy comfortable! Based on the comfort alone, I would keep them if they were half the price ($20-$25 would be reasonable.) However, for $40, I won’t be able to keep them since I will need that money to keep shopping around for the look that I want. Cons: They run really small and they don’t fit like they do in the product photos. I normally wear an 8-10 in jeans, depending on the brand, and I sized up to a 12 because I wanted these to fit nice and loose like they did on the model in the first product photo. In person, they’re much shorter so I can’t roll them up at the ankle like I wanted to. I think the size 12 fits me like most size 10 jeans normally would. A little loose in the waste with some wiggle room. Pros: would be cute on someone shorter and very skinny, the material seems like decent quality and there is a comfortable amount of stretch to them If you want to achieve the look in the product photos, I would recommend trying to go up by 3-4 sizes. For reference, I am 5’5”, a pear shape with a short torso, and I was 165 lbs when this photo was taken.

I’ve never bought anything from this brand before, but I was pleasantly surprised by how comfortable these jeans are. The fit around the waist and hips is great. The elastic high waist provides good stretch without feeling tight or restrictive, which makes them very comfortable to wear throughout the day. The belt is a nice detail and allows you to adjust the fit if needed, although it can create a bump in the front if you’re wearing a longer shirt over it. The wide-leg style is flattering and gives the jeans a relaxed, trendy look. The fabric also has a nice amount of stretch, so they feel flexible and comfortable while still looking like denim rather than jeggings. I also really like how deep the pockets are, which is something that’s often missing in women’s jeans. One thing to note is the length. I’m 5'4", and they are definitely long on me. Shorter people like me may need to wear them with heels or have them hemmed. Overall, these are a comfortable, stylish pair of jeans that can easily be dressed up or down. I’d definitely consider trying more items from this brand.

The belt is a nice detail and allows you to adjust the fit if needed, although it can create a bump in the front if you’re wearing a longer shirt over it. The wide-leg style is flattering and gives the jeans a relaxed, trendy look. The fabric also has a nice amount of stretch, so they feel flexible and comfortable while still looking like denim rather than jeggings. I also really like how deep the pockets are, which is something that’s often missing in women’s jeans. One thing to note is the length. I’m 5'4", and they are definitely long on me. Shorter people like me may need to wear them with heels or have them hemmed. Overall, these are a comfortable, stylish pair of jeans that can easily be dressed up or down. I’d definitely consider trying more items from this brand.

user_comment: These jeans are well sewn from a heavy weight cotton/polyester mix denim with a nice stretch. I have washed and dried them several times and they get softer each time. I am 5’ 110lbs and ordered an 8 which fits fine, however, I am petite, so the waist is a little high and the hem way too long. I am used to altering clothes I order, and thank goodness I can sew. My only complaint is the obvious wear pattern on the jean. The front panels just look too precise and “created”. I will definitely wear these jeans, and they will look good with any shirt or blouse, but I don’t like that wear pattern. But that’s just me.

user_comment: First impression was that these pants felt low quality upon first touch. Despite that, they were actually comfortable and stretchy. I was able to squat, bend my legs, and do some lunges comfortably without the fabric squeezing my legs or waist. I also had extra room on the waist line in case I over eat at a buffet. The bottom of the legs are truly wide-legged, but the white stitching at the bottom isn’t clean which gives it a low-quality look along with the strong “fake jeans” blue. I haven’t washed it yet, but if I noticed significant shrinkage, then I’ll update my review. For context I’m 5’1 and the pants were a tad bit too long for me. If I could get it tailored, I’d remove like 3 to 4 inches to touch my ankles.

These jeans feel very sturdy, especially for the price. The front pockets are quite deep -- not as deep as pockets in men's jeans, but much deeper than I'm used to in women's jeans. You can absolutely fit a smartphone in these front pockets vertically, with room to spare. I haven't washed these yet, but I'm definitely going to wash these in cold water only with black and blue clothes. This indigo is *intense.* Overall, a great value. Because this listing didn't seem to include measurements, here are the measurements for the size small: * The inseam is 28½" (73.5 cm), which seems short, except these are baggy and kind of hang low. * The front rise is 11½" (29 cm). On my short body, that meant the waistline is almost at my ribcage. * The back rise was a little harder to measure accurately, but I got about 16" (41 cm). * The leg opening is about 26" (66 cm). * The front pockets are 9¾" (25 cm) deep on the inside fold and almost 7¼" (18 cm) wide on the bottom hem. I'm about 5'1" (154 cm). In the photos, I'm wearing boots with 3" heels, and the jeans cover most of my feet. I couldn't wear regular sneakers without these dragging on the ground.

These jeans are made using a heavy denim and they’re extremely form fitting. I would suggest ordering by your exact waist measurement if you want them to fit really well. There’s just enough stretch to meet the upper echelon of your size. I ordered a medium @ 5’5”, 125#, and they fit like a glove. I do like the high waist and the 3 button closure is nice, it kind of acts like a corset. The flared bottoms are foot covering and the length is to the floor on me. All pockets are working, front and back. I didn’t notice my butt being lifted, but I won’t hold that against the jeans…lol

A great casual blouse that can take you from day to a relaxed evening event. It’s an elevated look from a plain t-shirt. Of course, living in Hawai'i, we can wear these cool tops all year round...LOL! The blouse is very soft to touch, NOT see-through and well-made. I've been a forever fan of this type of cloud-like gauze…it’s extremely easy to travel with and is a truly wash & wear! Who can complain?!?! I am a 38C but fit the S comfortably. My measurement (see photos) is a little smaller on the bust when compared to their size chart but it still fits comfortably as the fabric gives a little without being tight. I got the CLEARLY AQUA and I’d say, color-wise, it’s accurate to what’s showing up on the screen however, it’s more green than aqua to me. Overall, 2 thumbs up!!!

I wasn't sure I'd like this shirt as much as I do! The faded acid wash look and bubblegum pink is a fun summer color and I got it in the fall so I thought maybe I'd wait to wear it. Nope. I love it. I'm wearing it immediately!! It's loose fitting and does NOT have stretch so make sure you get your sizing right. I got a small and I'm 5'2" 130lbs mom of 5 and I love it! The arm openings aren't too baggy either which is such a issue with these types of shirts but not this one!! I buttoned it up all the way and tucked it in the front and I LOVE it with jeans!!! It's bright and happy and fun and I'm going to wear it all year and just add a jacket! You can dress it up if you wanted but I think it's definitely more of a casual shirt.

The arm openings aren't too baggy either which is such a issue with these types of shirts but not this one!! I buttoned it up all the way and tucked it in the front and I LOVE it with jeans!!! It's bright and happy and fun and I'm going to wear it all year and just add a jacket! You can dress it up if you wanted but I think it's definitely more of a casual shirt.
reviewer_name_raw: So so cute! Wear it immediately!

user_comment: LuminBlaze Strech Low Rise Jeans for Woman Elastic Vintage Flare Denim Jeans i’m 5'1", 138 lbs, athletic build with narrow hips, thicker thighs, and an average booty. i ordered my usual size 4 in the dark blue. these fit true to size and are super cute. they have a decent amount of stretch, which had me debating between sizing down to a 2 or sticking with a 4. i’m glad i stayed with the 4. they’re comfortable, not tight, and still really flattering. i’m short with a shorter torso, so these don’t feel super low rise on me. that’s more my body shape than the jeans themselves. length-wise, they’re not overly long, which i love, but if you’re tall, they may hit a little short. overall, i love the fit and the look and am really pleased with these. great everyday flares with stretch in the right places.

I wear size 29/medium pants and the medium fit as expected. The waist is high, reaching my navel. They are not tight in the waist. They’re possibly a little too loose there because they tend to slip down some and make the crotch too low without a belt. With an untucked shirt, I don’t usually bother. They are skinny/tight and stretchy. I am 5’7” and they are plenty long. They do bunch a little in the legs, but not too bad. I love the color.

Quality control needed. The pair of jeans I received, are unwearable. The button was hanging off and as soon as I put it through the hole to close the jeans it was completely off. If I’m honest I love how the jeans fit and feel. They run true to size, I ordered a size small. For reference I’m 5’7 approximately 130-135lbs depending on the day. unfortunately the only way to actually wear them would mean to add a backing to the area where I would as need to replace the button. So in the long run it would cost me time and money. Definitely not worth it, too much of a hassle

YMI Women's YMI Jeans Women's Mid Rise Western Belted Bootcut Jeans These jeans are amazingly comfortable! I ordered these in size 7. They are true to size. They fit me perfectly in the waist, hips and length (for reference, I am 5’ 7”, 142 lbs.) I love the stretchy material these are made with. It’s stretchy enough to make them comfortable to sit in, walk in, bend over, etc. The color is a little darker than the listing photos. The pockets on the back of these jeans add a touch of fun and are nice looking. The belt these came with is cute. I washed and dried these jeans on warm. They came out perfect. These are one of favorite pair of jeans, and a super value at a great price!

Okay, these are stretchy jeans, think more jeggings than jeans. They do fit true to size. I am 5'6", 132 lbs, and I ordered a small. They are not long, however, you can see in the image that they are above my socks, and I am only average height. They also say they are butt lifting, I did not get that but I guess I don't have much butt to lift in the first place lol. Now, seeing as they are stretchy, if you have a belly pooch, when you sit down, they will not give any support, so your "pooch" will pooch out.
reviewer_name_raw: They are not long as advertised.

user_comment: Flamingals are my new favorite jeans. I have ordered 3 pairs so far, & have loved all of them! The first 2 I ordered a large & really like the relaxed fit, but they do stretch out after wearing them for awhile. For this pair I decided to try a medium & although the waist is a bit tight, I’m hoping they will stretch out like the others. Other than that, I love the way they fit & the look of the undone hem at the bottom. They are a durable denim & feel like good quality. In addition, the price is right! In other brands of the same quality, you can end up paying $100 or more. For reference I am 5’3”, 140lbs.

I am 5'5 125 ibs and the xs is a PERFECT fit! Exactly like pictured and they are so comfortable. They aren't super stiff so you could wear them all day and they would feel like sweats. There are more mid rise but I feel like it's the perfect rise for me. 10/10 will be buying in all colors

I am 5'5 125 ibs and the xs is a PERFECT fit! Exactly like pictured and they are so comfortable. They aren't super stiff so you could wear them all day and they would feel like sweats. There are more mid rise but I feel like it's the perfect rise for me. 10/10 will be buying in all colors

My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

These stretchy, flared jeans are pretty cute on but do loosen up quite a bit over the course of the day. I ordered these in a medium with the 31" inseam. I'm 5'5" and generally wear a small but am currently just a couple of weeks post-partum and was needing some stretchy jeans that were more comfortable than trying to squeeze into my other size small jeans. When I first put these on in the morning, they fit decently. They are fitted but stretchy so offer nice movement. The front does have a slight amount of bunching, which isn't exactly flattering, but not super noticeable. I thought the 31" inseam would give me enough length for wearing heels but these really don't have any extra length for me. The waistband stretches out more over the course of the day, so by afternoon I find myself needing to hike these up every time I walk across a room. After a wash, they go back to fitting well for a few hours again. These jeans look pretty cute on. They're closer to flared jeggings than jeans so the front pockets are very visible beneath the stretchy fabric but I do appreciate having functional front AND back pockets. Overall, these are cute. They're comfy and stretchy but do wear in a bit too much for me and end up too loose to stay nicely in place by the end of the day.

I am 5'7" and 164lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. Based on the size chart I was between a size L and XL. I got this in a L and it fit as expected, I also got a pair in XL and they were too big. I might have been able to size down to a M because these are very stretchy. I also got both a 31" and 29" pair and the 31" inseam was better for my height. The light pair pictures are the 31", L and the dark paid are the 29", XL.These are super comfy and fit well. They do have a big of an odor to them and that lingered after washing. I imagine after a couple more washes it will be gone. They did not shrink after washing.These have nice big hand pockets and back pockets. They are super comfy. I am not a huge fan of the crossover waistband because I feel like it doesn't suck me in as well and I have issues with it rolling over in the middle. However, I know a lot of people really like that. I have similar pair of jeans to these from Blue Rose that have a straight across waistband and I like those better.The color of these was a bit lighter than expected and I didn't find it that flattering, but again that is personal preference. The XL I got was in the dark wash and I liked that a lot better.These are great quality and super comfy and I would recommend.
reviewer_name_raw: Super comfy and stretchy, nice pockets.

user_comment: Should've ordered the 31" inseam instead of the 29" since it shrunk some, when this cotton-polyester-spandex jeggings was washed on warm washer water & dried on high heat. After laundering, the hem was about ankle length on my daughter's 5'5 pear-shaped figure that usually fits a Medium or 8 in pants. The size Large was just right on her. Form-fitting but not really squeezy. She likes the v-shaped front and the stretchy snugness of the back that just hugs her form (see pic). Most jeans creates a gap in her center back due to how her curves form. These jeans' style & fabric well-accommodates for that. Recommended.

Amazon recommended that I get a small in these jeans, but I thought, “If they are too snug, I can’t do anything to change it except take out the sides and add some sort of stripe. If they are a tad too big, I can take that in.” I like sewing, creating things, deconstructing things. :) And I usually DO get small. But I decided to get a medium, and I’m glad I did! I don’t have to do anything—these fit just like I wanted. I did not want anything snug. I don’t want to highlight my rear; I just want to look nice. I’m 68, weigh 113, and I’m 5’6”. Great color, great fit, very nice construction. I’m not sure about the buttons, but they may grow on me. :) I am so happy to have these jeans! They have a little stretch, I love this dark blue, and I love that the pants are almost down to the ground. I didn’t think they would be. I definitely recommend!

At $38, these wide leg jeans ARE SOOO COMFY!! The material is a SOFT jeans material unlike the hard stiff type. I love how soft it feels yet it’s thick that doesn’t show undergarments bc of the WHITE COLOR. The pockets are in the front and it’s very comfy to even have phone in front or back. The whole fit is great! Not too far up the middle between thighs section, so it’s comfy when you sit and stand. For 5’4” 120lbs I ordered size 4, and it fit me very well. The only minor issue is that there area where buttons are sewn has some double sewing onto it so you can easily tell when it’s dark threads against the white material, but not a big big issue.

LookbookStore Women's High Waisted Capri Jeggings I REALLY want a pair of white jeans, and these are as close as I’ve gotten to finding something that isn't see-through. Except ... although the Medium fit me great around the waist and hips, the legs were uncomfortably tight and made my legs look like sausages. (I'm 5'5" and 140 pounds with a 30-inch waist.) I could live with the tightness, but it also kept causing the waist to pull down, so I couldn’t afford to bend over or squat. They also gave me a flat unattractive butt because the tight legs pulled the fabric taut so I got one combined uni-cheek. If you have skinny legs, though, these jeans would be awesome. The waist is stretchy and comfortable and it’s glorious to have real pockets. Plus, I'm wearing black undies in the photos and you can barely tell, so they're not see-through.

I am 5'4" and weigh 173 lbs and purchased the medium and they fit great; just the way that I like them. The ad says they are bell bottom, which they are clearly not; and for that, I am grateful for. I almost didn't buy them based on that description; I am not a fan of bell bottoms. The length was perfect for me, but I can see ladies 5'6" and up would probably prefer them a little longer. These jeans have a great amount of stretch and look so flattering on a curvier figure, however they're so forgiving, they would look great on any figure. I would LOVE it if this style pant came in white or other color options; I'd buy them in a heartbeat. The price for these jeans at the time of this review is $29.99 with 20% off. That's a GREAT price. I paid $50 for a different brand that fit similar to these; but I actually prefer this brand. I totally recommend these jeans.

Reviewing YEMORCH Women’s Adjustable Waist Wide Leg Jeans, These jeans are a great quality denim. They are nice and thick, soft and drape perfectly. I normally wear a M to L in U.S. sizing, depending on the brand. I ordered a M in these by accident. I meant to order a L, so I was a little worried they wouldn’t fit. Luckily, they do fit! They are a big tight in the waist, but everything else fits. I am just shy of 5’5” and the length is slightly long, but they look great being slightly long. The waist has snaps so you can adjust for tighter or looser fit. For the price, the quality is excellent, making these a good value. (Bonus: there is no off gassing smell!)

I’m a lil more bottom heavy and I have short legs and chubby thighs. The 27 fits perfectly. These jeans allow me to eat a lot of cookies and ham and they expand pretty good, while the belt that I bought separately tells me to stop eating. I can wear them on my skinny days or my really bloated days. Great jeans for 5’3 folks!!

user_comment: Levi's Womens Superlow Flare Jeans I recently bought these jeans in a size 26, and I’m absolutely in love with them! I’m 5’4”, 125 pounds, with a moderate booty, and they fit perfectly—not too tight, not too baggy, just super comfortable. I might’ve been able to go with a 25, but the 26 feels just right. Even though they’re low-rise, they’re not uncomfortably low or exposing too much—they hit that sweet spot just right. I love the color, and they look fantastic, especially how they flatter my figure and fit so well on my bum. The length is spot-on, whether I wear them with shoes that have a little heel (my usual vibe) or go without. These jeans are so versatile—I can pair them with pretty much any outfit. Highly recommend!

Very stylish wide leg jeans! I am about 200 pounds and bought a size 14. When I opened the package the tag in the pants were labeled a size 18, but they fit like a 14. I’m guessing that is to compensate if the sizing runs differently? The jeans have a lovely feel and are really made from “jean” material. The legs are “wide” and not a straight cut, so if you’re into the trend you will not be disappointed with these. I also appreciated the high waist, as that looks best on my figure. The pants come in several different shades and I bought the Myosotis color. I love the lighter blue tone on the jeans!

The jeans are super comfortable with just the right amount of stretch. I feel the high waist and wide-leg design are both flattering and stylish. I wore them with boots and a satin button-down shirt for my son’s school orientation and got compliments on the look. The frayed hem and subtle side slits give them a trendy edge without being too much. I machine washed the jeans before I wore it and I didn’t see any issues with fading or unraveling. I made sure to check the size guide and then I ordered a size 6, and I am 5’6” and 156 lbs. the fit was perfect for me so I would say it is true to the size guide. These jeans are a great addition to my wardrobe and easy to dress up or down.

Okay, lots to say about these pants. I wasn’t sure if I should give them 3 stars or 4. If it’s based on my personal opinion of them for MY body, I’d say 2 or 3 stars. But in terms of overall quality and other factors I’ll mention below, 4 stars is much more accurate. First, the actual quality of the material is nice, thick, and stretchy. They are extremely comfy, even so in the crotch area which usually is bothersome for me with rigid denim. These are not rigid denim at all. They are more of spandex / jegging material but decent thickness. I like that they’re actually high rise, they sit nicely right at my waist line. Other pants in similar style usually run about an inch too short on the rise, causing discomfort and Unflattering appearance at the waistline. These are a true high rise, which I love. The belt is removable and matches the jeans perfectly. Can be used to functional purposes of tightening the waistline or just as a cosmetic additive. These pants have real pockets on the front and back which is wonderful. In terms of the “jogger” style at the ankle: aka the elastic cuff at the ankle part, I’m not a big fan. The leg opening is still quite wide, the ankle part just hangs off my leg which Is odd. Additionally, the inseam is far too short for me. I’m 5’9 with longer legs and the inseam is even beyond ankle length for me, these are actually capri length which is not flattering on me especially with the wide elastic cuff at the bottom. I will say, they did look better with a high top chuck than with just socks on, but I probably won’t ever wear these. They are just WAY too short. If you are super short these may be a good fit for you. But I’d still be mindful of the wide elastic cuff and how it hangs off your ankle / calf. By the way, I am 5’9 and 150 pounds and typically wear a size 8. I ordered a size 8 and other than the length they are very true to size and fit my hips/butt/ waist perfectly. They are fitted at the hip / butt / waist, hugging the hips, then relax down the leg to a straight leg all the way to the elastic cuff. As you can see in these photos, they aren’t super flattering on me. Even if I sized up to a 10 I don’t think the length would work for me. Overall, the actual quality of the material, craftsmanship, and stitching is definitely 4 stars; good quality. If these were on the right person… they could be very cute! Definitely for short girls! Hope this helps!
