Okay, lots to say about these pants. I wasn’t sure if I should give them 3 stars or 4. If it’s based on my personal opinion of them for MY body, I’d say 2 or 3 stars. But in terms of overall quality and other factors I’ll mention below, 4 stars is much more accurate. First, the actual quality of the material is nice, thick, and stretchy. They are extremely comfy, even so in the crotch area which usually is bothersome for me with rigid denim. These are not rigid denim at all. They are more of spandex / jegging material but decent thickness. I like that they’re actually high rise, they sit nicely right at my waist line. Other pants in similar style usually run about an inch too short on the rise, causing discomfort and Unflattering appearance at the waistline. These are a true high rise, which I love. The belt is removable and matches the jeans perfectly. Can be used to functional purposes of tightening the waistline or just as a cosmetic additive. These pants have real pockets on the front and back which is wonderful. In terms of the “jogger” style at the ankle: aka the elastic cuff at the ankle part, I’m not a big fan. The leg opening is still quite wide, the ankle part just hangs off my leg which Is odd. Additionally, the inseam is far too short for me. I’m 5’9 with longer legs and the inseam is even beyond ankle length for me, these are actually capri length which is not flattering on me especially with the wide elastic cuff at the bottom. I will say, they did look better with a high top chuck than with just socks on, but I probably won’t ever wear these. They are just WAY too short. If you are super short these may be a good fit for you. But I’d still be mindful of the wide elastic cuff and how it hangs off your ankle / calf. By the way, I am 5’9 and 150 pounds and typically wear a size 8. I ordered a size 8 and other than the length they are very true to size and fit my hips/butt/ waist perfectly. They are fitted at the hip / butt / waist, hugging the hips, then relax down the leg to a straight leg all the way to the elastic cuff. As you can see in these photos, they aren’t super flattering on me. Even if I sized up to a 10 I don’t think the length would work for me. Overall, the actual quality of the material, craftsmanship, and stitching is definitely 4 stars; good quality. If these were on the right person… they could be very cute! Definitely for short girls! Hope this helps!

I feel like in this day and age, zero stretch in jeans should be in the product description. These jeans can support my entire torso when I lean forward they're that stiff and I just don't see the point in absolute ZERO stretch regular smegular jeans when material can be made more comfortable. Even a small percentage spandex would've made all the difference. Also the color is not classic wash as the picture but a solid, uniform blue (-1 star). I'm 5'4" 134lbs, 27" waist and got a size small. My body type is curvy and overall they're very cute but the rear is a little gappy when I sit so I prefer them with a belt. Still, I only wear these doing something where walking is the maximum activity required.

I love these jeans. I’m 73 years old and still like to look fashionable, but comfort is more important than when I was younger. These jeans are very cute and comfortable. They have stretch and easily pull on. I’m 5’5” with long legs and wear a size medium. The length is perfect. Also, they wash great with no shrinkage. They had a bit of a chemical smell, which improved after washing. I’m going to order another pair.

I love the stretch and comfort of these jeans. They’re form fitting but the material is soft and extremely flexible. They’re the jeggings with the 70s flare/bell bottom look. The material is thin however, so even though they have a lot of stretch, I can imagine they can easily wear down after so many machine washes. It sits high on the waist which can be firm flattering for curvier figures. I like that it has pockets that have a good amount of depth on both the sides and back. I ordered a size XS as I’m 5 ft 108lbs. It fits more like a small and the hem runs past my feet so I’ll likely have to tailor them or wear higher heels. $41 dollars is a bit steep for the thinness of the material but wearing comfortable jeans with this amount of stretch could be a flex.

NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Denim Jeans I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? I don't look it. I'm 5 7 @ 127 lbs - 29" waist and 39" hips and I bought the size 4 with 32" inseam which is More than long enough. I sew so non issue. My problem area is heinous muffin top if jeans not "just right" and many times I wear a Spanx cami to compensate for this. Not comfy in the south east summers lol. No Spanx needed with these. I'd always thought if I avoid my waist all together I'd avoid muffin top hence low waist line pants, I was wrong! These fit excellent in crotch which can also be another issue with me. The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... Came home and went on Amazon, ordered Joe jeans at big $ and ordered low at this price. Ecstatic to Find these on my door step this morning and put them on to find my happy place in my fitting world. I can't believe the price of these and am ordering another because coffee guzzling me and white anything means I always order two if I love it lol.

user_comment: NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Denim Jeans This denim is like butter. So comfy. Fits great. But i really need a 32" inseam. These are only 30". Ill give them a try in the wash but if they shrink at all I wont be able to wear them.

Comfortable and affordable. I ordered one size larger than I typically do and they fit me well in the waist and in the length without being too tight. They are a heavier jean material, so great for the upcoming colder seasons. I like the color and style of the flared legs. Stretchy without feeling like they will stretch too much and lose their shape. The back pockets don’t make you look wide like some jeans tend to do. Body shape may make a difference in how these fit on you. The only negative about these is how they fit on the behind/seat area. They wrinkle and looked a bit odd when I was initially trying them on. I doubt this will change once I wash them. Overall, I like them. For reference, I’m 5’8” and 175. Usually I take size 14 or 16 misses or XL in pants.

There is plenty of room in the stomache area for us moms. I am 5'4 and these are just a little long, but that's not unusual so it's not anything that bothers me. The quality is decent for the price: I am in the process of losing some wright right now, and I expect these to still look great when I'm ready to go down a size.

OK so I am 5' 11" and about 260lbs..measurements 46' 41' 55 hips..and I have been wanting a Vintage denim jacket that actually fits good since forever.. I looked at this for like a week..it's been in the cart but kept ignoring cause the 'XXL sizing was worrying me because a XXL and a 2xl are different sizes a lot of people don't realize.. so I was skeptical..omg it arrived way before it's arrival time of 10 pm next day..got it today and opened the bag.. when I pulled this jacket out.. it smelled vintage lol.. like old denim..that's a plus cause then I know it's not chambray it's real jeans material with no stretch.. and then I opened up the jacket and it was big big.. I am never this lucky. I live in nyc and we go through like 6 seasons in one fall day so some jackets are too hot. Plus I have a bunch of patches that's begging for a new home on a piece of clothing..the price at $43 bucks was on point and actually the best 43 bucks I spent in a while. For reference, I am 5'9", 133 lbs, normally size 4-6 in pants or size 27-28 when sized that way. This review is for the white jeans in size medium.I ordered the size medium, which is what I typically order in these types of pants when these size ranges are offered (and it's usually a bad sign when the jeans come in this size range rather than by waist size or the numerical sizing). The model in the advertised photos has a slim body, as opposed to a more curvy body, and the pants fit her well, seemingly. My body type is more like the model's, but these do not fit that way at all. They are too loose in the thighs. I do not like the way the look at all. I'd suggest, if you did decide to take a risk and order these, that you only do so if you have a thiccer body type. Or perhaps size down, but that is a risk that I cannot recommend, as I have not tried the smaller size. I just know that these are a NO for me.The material is also super thin. Which isn't a big deal for $25 pants, if only the fit were better (for me). They lack a size chart, which is one of my biggest issues with this, as you really are taking a risk when ordering these, especially with the leggings-type sizing.

It does read a bit more orange than caramel but I already thought that when looking at the photos so I'd still say it's true to the listing. I am just under 5'6", 34B, 125 lbs, and very long waisted. I ordered the small based on the model pics and it fits excellent in the shoulders and chest and is a nice length even for my waist. The sleeves and overall length are a tad shorter on my than on the models but not abnormally short. I suspect it would have been too long had I sized up. It's not super thick which is good since we tend to have warm falls in SoCal, but it's not see through either so that's perfect. It will look cute layered with a jacket, jeans and boots for a cozier fall/winter vibe or just french tucked into jeans for fall. I'm super happy to add this pop of fall to my wardrobe and the current price ($20) AND a current promo code make it a great value.

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

I'm 5'3" about 138lbs and purchased a regular medium and the length and waist are otherwise perfect.

user_comment: Value Rating: 5/5 stars – At around $40, these are priced fairly for the quality. Cheaper versions of this Y2K cargo style often feel stiff or paper-thin, and these land in the sweet spot: thick, soft, and well-stitched without being heavy. Specifications: • Fabric: 68% cotton, 29% polyester, 2% viscose, 1% elastane • Closure: Button/zip fly • Rise: Mid-to-high waist • Fit: Wide-leg, baggy cargo style with utility pockets • Color: Black with fade wash Key Points: These fit exactly the way they should for a cargo jean—baggy, relaxed, and casual, while still flattering at the waist. I’m between 120–125 lbs, usually a 26 in jeans, and the size small fit perfectly at the waist. They are meant to be roomy through the legs, and they are, but not in a sloppy way. I wouldn’t mind if they shrink just a little after a wash, but I’m still happy with them as-is. The fabric is what surprised me most—it’s thick enough to feel durable, but soft enough that you don’t get that stiff, board-like denim feel. The fade is definitely noticeable in person, so expect that vintage wash look. To me, it reads intentional and trendy, not cheap or garish. Final Thoughts: If you’re looking for a baggy cargo style with comfort and structure, these are a win. No complaints on fit, fabric, or style.

This top was thinner than I expected it to be, but still not see through. The material is not Cotton, it is a polyester blend however it is very soft and does not appear to show any wrinkles after wear. I like the loose but not frumpy fit. I have lost about 60 pounds over the last year and a half, however I still do have larger breasts (36 DD) and I am always conscious of how shirts lay across my chest, or if they cling to arms, back or torso. This Medium size fits me well, and I would think a larger size would have also fit in a flattering way when I was a bit heavier. For reference I am 5’5 and 140 lbs. I think it is good purchase for the price, as long as you are not expecting a more expensive fabric.

I love these jeans! The material is thick, soft, and super stretchy, which is exactly what I like. It doesn’t feel like regular denim at all. It’s more like wearing soft leggings that still look put together. I got the navy blue, and it’s a rich, dark shade that goes with everything. They’re very high waisted, which I prefer, and they hold everything in nicely without being tight or uncomfortable. The back pockets are real and deep enough to fit my phone, which is always a bonus. The only issue for me is the length. I’m 5 feet tall, so like with most pants, these are a little long. I just tuck the ends under for now until I can get them hemmed. Not a big deal, but worth noting if you’re on the shorter side. They are a bit pricier than other jeggings I’ve bought, but the quality is great. If they go on sale, I’ll definitely be ordering more.

These jeggings are comfortable and well-fitting. They are true to size having ordered an L on my 5’5” 178# 32” inseam body. I ordered them in the darker blue color and am happy with the depth of the color. The waist is high which is nice to help hide a muffin top. The waistband is generous and doesn’t bind as I’m showing in one of my photos. I also demonstrate the stretch in the fabric where I pull away the fabric at my thigh. There are two functional pockets in the back and none in the front. The length comes just to my ankles and the legs are reasonably snug all the way down which I like. Moving, bending, and just living feels good in these. They don’t pull down in the back when you squat or bend over. All in all, I’m very happy with these and know they will be a continue to be my “go to” item all fall and winter.
reviewer_name_raw: Well-fitting jeans, good stretch, nice color

I ordered these Xineicy Women's Jeans Y2K Wide Leg Baggy Jeans High Waist Jeans for Women Loose Vintage 90s Boyfriend Denim Pants in a Size Small for my 10 year old daughter and knew by looking at the size chart that they would be on the large side. They remind me of the old JNCO jeans from my childhood... and my experimental fashionista kiddo has been wanting to try them for ages! These jeans are high quality denim. The seams are straight and secure. The button and zipper are both fully functional. The cut of these jeans is exactly what my daughter was looking for-- a super big barrel leg. They are a little on the long side and are a smidge big in the waist, but she will grow into them in no time! Plus, we really like the dusty/rustic color of them. When paired with a cute long sleeve graphic tee, a belt and a pair of sneakers-- this outfit gets her tons of compliments! If you are looking for the big old early 2000's jeans-- these are the ones to buy!!!

I first purchased the brown version of this tank top, and have worn it a lot over the summer. It has a slight razorback cut (see photo) and is unforgiving in terms of the slightest bulge. For reference, I'm 5'4" tall and weigh 106 pounds and you can see the unflattering fit of the grey blue, which was narrower. Both were a Size XS, but the brown version both fit much better and complemented my warm coloring. Returned the grey blue. Like most clothing, you almost have to try it on before deciding...and even then there can be a lot of variation between sizes/colors. Overall, the top is okay, it washes up well (no shrinkage, but definitely dry flat), and the price point is great. The trick is finding a color that both fits and flatters.

Very nice heavy wide leg white pant. I would say they are a little on the extra wide side through the leg and that would be ok. The only problem with these pants is that they run really long. I am 5’7” and without a heal the cover my feet and then some. With a heal they are still long, but definitely do able. I would recommend these pants for someone over 5’8”.

I love denim jackets, this is my 4th and finally got black! My review includes the following: -usually take an XL (am busty) but noted that the jacket has a slim, tapered fit, took a chance and ordered XXL. -jacket arrived, tried it on and the fit is perfect. The taper and slim fit hugged my body, flattering and accentuates my figure. There is a bit of extra room to add additional layers for more warmth. -as with my other denim jackets, the “look” is vintage. Can be paired with skirts, dresses and pants. -fabric is 79% cotton, 20% recycled cotton and 1% elastane. Also fabric is mid-weight, with low stretchability. -can be washed, however, recommend cold and hang to dry. -the black color is intensely saturated with slightly dark, brass shank buttons, including the chest pocket flaps. -garment length: 22.5” for XXL What I really like: - medium weight denim, saturated black, stunning -jacket construction inside and out is impeccable. -perfect fit, right out of the Amazon package -brass shank buttons add a striking contrast, with an elegant look -great value for a high quality jacket Consideration’s: -your body type will tell you if you need to adjust the size ordered. I am 5’9” wt. 168 and busty. -this is a tapered, slim cut jacket that can highlight your figure. -lastly, you may want to add layers, to wear in cold weather. Negatives: None Would I recommend: Absolutely!

This jacket is absolutely wonderful 😍 I love it!!! For reference, I’m 5'3", 169 lbs, and a 38C. I purchased a size large and it fit perfectly 👌—even the sleeve length! I usually struggle with sleeves since I’m a little vertically challenged 😆 lol, but this one was just right.👌 The fit is true to size and nicely tapered. I’ve been searching for a white jean jacket, and this one did not disappoint. The quality is amazing for the price, and it’s such a versatile layering piece to have in your closet. You can roll up the sleeves or wear them down, and either way, it looks absolutely gorgeous.💕 I’ve already received so many compliments! I also love that I can wear it to school with my summer jumpers that normally aren’t school-appropriate because of the sleeveless style—this jacket makes them wearable again. Don’t hesitate to grab this jean jacket—it fits great, looks beautiful, and the quality is excellent for the price. I highly recommend it! 👌

This jean jacket will be perfect for my morning walk when the outside temperature drops into the 50s. It has pockets on both sides to keep my hands warm and/or hold my keys. Surprisingly, it also has 2 pockets on the inside of the jacket (see photo). I typically take a Medium for any clothing - this Medium jacket fits perfectly - I am 5'4" tall. Per the tag, the jacket is 99% cotton and 1% elastane (i.e., spandex). The only quirk I found is that the stitching for the 'Size' tag pokes through to the outside back of the jacket (see photo). I initially thought one of my cats had put 2 claws into it already.

First glance - this looks like a standard denim jacket. First touch - feels like thick durable denim, does not feel like spandex or thin stretching material that looks like denim. Despite original concerns with sizing - I am usually a size L, but depending on brand I may take an XL. I have 5' 5.5", 165 pounds, 34DDD bust. This jacket not only was a perfect fit across the shoulders but also fully closed/buttoned at the bust line. Material is 99% cotton, 1% elastane. Simple silver buttons inscribed "essential basics". Two functional pockets on the chest, two functional pockets and the waist and two pockets in the jacket at each side. Bottom waist area has buttons to adjust to create a smaller waist; was not personally necessary in my situation but is an option. Fit surprisingly and exceptionally well! Very comfortable.

I’m 5’3” and 159 lbs. I bought these due to someone saying these were a Halara dupe. These are 4 way stretch material that looks exactly like a Jean material. They are soft and stretch around the waist and crotch making it comfortable when sitting . (I find today’s jeans are short in the crotch giving a wedgy when sitting) . I bought large but was worried that I should have gotten XL due to my menopause belly. The large fit fine. A little snug at first but relaxed without falling down as the day went on. They are a bit long so will have to hem. Not a true Jean but no one can tell by looking at them. Gave it 4 stars because I do not yet know how well they will hold up after washing and wearing multiple times.

I really wanted to love this Dokotoo babydoll denim dress because the design itself is very cute and flattering in theory. I ordered a small and I’m 5’3”, about 120 lbs, and the fit is quite roomy; very oversized even for a babydoll style. Length hits a few inches above the knee. The fabric is airy and breathable, but noticeably stiff, which affects how the dress drapes. What adds to the bulk is the midway folded seam on the skirt (the bottom part of the dress) and on the sleeves; that structured fold combined with the stiff fabric makes the dress puff out instead of flowing. The waist seam is gathered as expected for a babydoll dress, but with a softer, more fluid fabric, that gathering paired with the hem detail would have looked much more flattering and drapey. Fabric labeling is also confusing: one tag mentions cotton/linen/denim, while the sewn-in label says 85% cotton and 15% polyester. There’s no stretch. Sewing quality is solid, with clean seams, a flattering V-neck, subtle puff sleeves, and a back tie to help keep the shoulders in place. Overall, it’s a pretty dress with good construction, but the stiffness of the fabric and the oversized cut make it look bulkier than expected. With a softer, flowier fabric, this would easily be a 5-star piece. My head says this is a 3-star dress, but my heart says it’s a 5-star dress.

These arrived today. I tried them on and left them on the rest of the day! They are so very comfortable and good looking as well. I highly recommend that you use the sizing chart and measure because I would normally buy size medium, but the chart recommended size large and was right on. I’m glad I didn’t buy them smaller. The fabric is soft and stretchy, but also smooth and crisp enough to look good. I can’t say how well they wash - yet. They are a great length for me - I’m 5 foot 5 and they come nicely over my shoes but don’t drag. One minor thing is that the dye is not consistent throughout the pants, there are stripes of white on the legs. I don’t know if that’s on purpose or not. The pockets are nice and large and easy to get hands in and out of because of the stretch, but my phone won’t be falling out like some other pants. I am overall very pleased with these pants.

I love the jeans they fit and feel so good they are comfortable I received my item in 1 day I can't wait to wear them the length is perfect for my height I'm 5'3 I normally wear 14-18 I purchased a XL and they fit great the quality is on point the distress is my liking I'm going to enjoy wearing the jeans

My photo really does not do justice to the gray purple of this sweater. It is a lovely color. This is a very thin and light sweater, suitable for chilly days. It is 53% viscose, 21% nylon and 26% polyester. Its light weight makes it perfect for me here in southern Arizona. I am 5'10" tall and 128 pounds and the M fits me just right. I think this is a great sweater to wear with jeans or dress pants.

Sweater came in very quickly and first thing I notice was how soft it was, and the red was a great color. I got this for my wife who is 5' 9" and ~160lbs so we got the large size per the chart. When she tried it on, it was good on sleeve length but very snug around the chest and belly. The instructions do say if you want a loose fit to size up, listen to them! My wife is still on the fence if she will wear it but for others it may be very nice. I will say that it's a quality but very thin knit sweater. Probably good in warmer weather, but please know that if stretched, it gets see through. I've added some photos to show what I mean.

These jeans are SUPER stretchy denim. They look like regular jeans but have quite a bit of softness and stretch to them. There is also a bit of elasticized bits in the waistline in the back of the jeans. The fit was true to size. I am 5 foot 4 inches tall and these are just right at ankle length on me. Normally my jeans are a little longer. If you are any taller and they become something like culottes. They are very comfortable to wear, every bit like joggers or leggings. Because I am short this style isn't super flattering, even though wide legged jeans are in style. Pockets in front are a little oddly placed in my opinion, but functional none the less.

Exactly what I wanted just put another t-shirt under it because it's see-through.fits nice and tight sleeves are the only problem for me I like my sleeves to my thumb but I have long arms. Went and ordered more. I'm 110lbs so I got the XS no problem, just see through. Happy shopping.

I'm not sure what compelled me to order these jeans but I'm so glad I did! I kind of love these! They're so weird and out of my comfort zone, but sometimes you just need to try something new. They are such a cute, different style. They hit at the ankle so they actually look cute with both flats and with heels. They are jean material but very stretchy and comfortable. The zipper on the side seems pretty sturdy. The white is a little bit see through, but the pockets in the back make that not a huge issue. They fit pretty well and are flattering on the butt. I'm 5'3", 125lbs and ordered a 4. They're great, but I honestly could have sized down to a size 2 and the waist would probably fit better (not quite sure that size chart is accurate because I certainly do NOT have a 25 inch waist). But I still like the way that the 4 looks. Can't wait to wear these and try to style them in different ways for the spring/summer! .....Plot twist, I wrote this whole review and then realized I was wearing them backwards the whole time. Tried them on the correct way (with the pockets in the front) and they're still cute. But I honestly prefer them the other way. So good news, these pants are apparently reversible

These were $49 when I ordered them and it looks like now they are almost half that with a coupon. For the cheaper price, I would maybe recommend these. For the $49 price, I definitely would not. For $27 you might be getting what you pay for. I am 5‘4“ and 165 pounds and ordered the size 14 and they’re a little loose, I probably could’ve gone for the 12 but didn’t want to be squeezed too much. They’re comfortable and fairly cute, but I don’t know if I just got a bad pair, but one of the legs is shorter than the other. So I don’t know if this is a quality control issue, but I would hope would just be a one off because I’ve gotten stuff from this brand before and liked it, and would return them if I could, to try a different pair. If it was the same length on each leg, then I would really like these pants. The little ruffle at the bottom is cute and the top with the little front pockets is comfy and looks nice. The zipper doesn’t quite zip all the way to the top, which is a little bit annoying. So again, if these have a coupon and you’re able to return them if you get a wonky pair like mine, I would probably recommend it. If these are full price, or you can return them, I would say pass it and find something else of better quality. This is not a good value for the full price. I only took off two stars since I would hope mine is just a one off mistake. :/

My dimensions are 5'3", 130 lbs, 28 - 29" waist and I ordered the size 6 in the black. I recommend reviewing the size chart and then also taking into account the materials composition: 72%Cotton, 26%Polyester, 2%Elastane. Usually, this blend tends to get loose during wear, so I find that I have to add waist cinching pins; I took a picture of what I currently use. The Elastane provides the stretch for all types of movement: sitting, walking, stretching or relaxing. The pants are not restrictive or form fitting except for the high waist which offers a flattering and slimming silhouette. The material is a medium weight and breathable due to the 72% cotton. The stitching and cut wash well as I have washed them twice which caused them to shrink a little bit. The color is as shown in the advertisement and pairs nicely with other blacks and greys. The zipper is hidden within the side-seam and does not add any weight or visual distraction to your waist. The ruffled hem pairs nicely with heels, tennis shoes or flip flops. I have shorter legs hence they are closer to my ankles then up towards my calf. I have been very pleased with the quality and durability of these jeans.

These high-waisted jean shorts are a must-have for summer! I got them in medium blue, size medium (for reference: 5'5", 149 lbs, 39" hips, 33" waist), and they fit perfectly true to size. They’re very comfortable with just the right amount of stretch. The drawstring elastic waist makes them easy to adjust, and the 4" inseam is a flattering length for various age groups. I got these jeans because I have been losing pregnancy weight. I have been in a size 6 for a few years, and as my waistbands have been getting a bit small I figured I would be back in a 4 soon, so I wanted to be prepared. I was surprised when these fit me as is, so I'm not sure if I have hit 4 sooner than I thought or if they are a little on the larger side. They might be a tiny bit tight, but not uncomfortably so, and less tight than my size 6 jeans are loose. The jeans do go just past my feet in some places. I'm 5'4" but I'm pretty sure my legs are a little shorter than many people my height. I usually buy jeans that are labeled as having a shorter inseam. The raw edge hem on mine looks just like battlements. I doubt that's the case with every pair and it's probably just coincidence for mine, but to me it looks odd. I don't mind a jagged edge, but it looks like it was done in a pattern on purpose, so it's kind of weird. I will either cut off some of the longer ends to keep the ragged look or I will hem it straight. I haven't decided which yet, but it needs to be a little shorter on me anyways. I'm not taking stars off for it because I knew it didn't have a shorter inseam to begin with. The fabric is on the comfortable side of average for jeans. It's not too stiff, but not thin and very stretchy either. The fabric is thick enough that it seems durable, and as long as the jagged edge doesn't get caught on anything, I expect them to last for a good decade. (I don't wear jeans terribly often though.) I wouldn't buy these if you ride a bicycle often unless you are rolling the leg up. A bike would eat these up. These are high waisted as listed, on the shorter side of high waisted if that makes sense. They aren't super high, but they are still high. I like them like that. These are currently listed at approximately $40, which seems about average for a decent pair of jeans, which these are. If you are looking for a pair of jeans and don't mind the rough edge, I think the price is reasonable.

These are the second pair of wide leg jeans from Trendy Queen that I have gotten. I absolutely LOVE the fit on my frame. I am 5'9", 150#, 29" waist and 38" hips. I have about a 33" inseam. I usually wear a size 8 in most clothes, but in Trendy Queen jeans I wear a size 6. These jeans are very flattering on an hourglass shape like mine. Their denim has just the right amount of stretch. It does not get loose after wearing for several hours and holds its shape while remaining comfortable. Now - the things that are a bit off about the jeans. Both this pair and my other pair of jeans from Trendy Queen had a VERY strong chemical/plastic odor, which took several washings to get rid of. But once it was gone, they were fine. My other pair are a regular wide leg raw hem jean (now discontinued?), but this pair has an irregular hem. I didn't think it would be quite so square-cut irregular, but it looks really weird, at least to me. I think I will be further cutting these jeans to a wide cropped length raw hem and wearing them with chunky boots instead. Because I do love them, I just don't love the weird hem.

I love the Trendy Queen brand - they consistently deliver quality, style, and great value - and these jeans are no exception. The fit is perfect and true to size according to their size chart. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 120 pounds, and the size 4 fits me exactly right. The high-rise waist is flattering without feeling restrictive, and the wide-leg straight style looks like something you’d find in a high-end boutique. The denim has just the right amount of stretch - soft and comfortable, but structured enough to hold its shape all day. No sagging, no baggy knees by the afternoon. They move with you while still looking polished. They’re easy to dress up or down and pair well with everything from fitted tanks to cozy sweaters. Stylish, comfortable, and well-made - another win from Trendy Queen!

They fit as expected with a bit of extra length-but most jeans and pants are long on me as I’m only 5’4. They have a high waistline, double button & zipper front with an elastic band in the back-helps with gapping. These are great for cooler weather-not a lightweight jean. Very nice-very in style-the wide flowing leg straight from the knee onward.

Levi Strauss Signature Gold Women's Mid-Rise Bermuda Shorts UPDATE 2: third year in a row buying these shorts for summer they are my favorite! I wanted to give an update on sizing because I’ve been working out and lost weight. I am now 5’4” 121lbs and size 2 fits perfectly! Although my shorts from the last 2 years are still in great shape they don’t fit anymore. These shorts last for a long time. :) I am buying 8 new pairs for this summer in size 2 because they are literally all I wear lol! I used to hate shorts but with these they cover my cellulite and give me confidence! No more long pants in the summer. Yay! I also started to order my step daughter these because her school dress code requires girls to wear longer shorts and she likes them as well. She’s 5’3” 135-140 with a straight figure and she likes the way size 4 fits. UPDATE 1: I ordered these last summer I think? I still am loving these shorts! I am back to order more for upcoming warm weather here in Texas. Black is finally back in stock- yay! I ordered a black pair and love them. They fit the same as the dark wash jean ones I have. I actually think I like the black ones even more than the dark denim, I didn’t think that was possible! Needless to say I definitely recommend these. I’m ordering now because when warm weather comes these sell out like hot cakes! Get yours now ladies! They have become mostly the only shorts I wear besides Nike athletic shorts for working out. I need to order multiples because I wear them constantly. Not having to pull your shorts down from riding up between your legs all day is amazing. Curvy ladies will know what I mean! I’ve tried so many other shorts and none of them compare to these. The only Bermuda shorts I’ve ever found that are flattering on me and don’t make my short legs look ridiculously frumpy! I wish they had even more washes for me to order because white and light wash aren’t my thing, not flattering on my big thighs. I only wear medium, dark or black wash denim. Although I’m tempted to go outside my comfort zone and possibly try the light wash.... ****Original review: I’m 5’4” 135ish and I ordered size 6. I range between a size 4-6 or 27-28. I’m normally a 4 in jeans. Size 6 is comfortable, ever so slightly loose. The size 4 fits as well (I tried both) but I just preferred the size 6 because it had a little more wiggle room. The pictures of me posted in the shorts are of the size 6. A lot of the reviews said to go up a size and I can see why because the size 4 was pretty fitted on me. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

Levi Strauss Signature Gold Women's Mid-Rise Bermuda Shorts I have come back twice for these shorts already this year, and so long as Levis doesn't change them, I will keep coming back! These shorts are incredibly comfortable and quite flattering. They have just enough stretch and don't loosen with wear. I am 5'8"/5'9"ish and sit between a 12 and 14 so I ordered 14 to keep it comfy and they are perfect! I could probably get away with a 12, but I didn't want to chance a muffin top look. With my rear end, I usually end up with a gap at the back of a lot of jeans/shorts, but these stay flat across my lower back.I DO cut the stitches with a seam ripper and unroll the hem to make them a bit longer. The serged hemline looks great and doesn't show any signs of coming loose. It takes a little work so you don't pick at the wrong seams, but the trouble is totally worth it!All in all, these are excellent shorts, they always seems to be well priced (when in stock!), and I highly recommend them for girls with curves!

EMMIOL Women Denim Shirts Oversized Long Sleeve Button Down Blouse Distresse Jean Jacket Fall Outfits 2026 Lightweight denim with inconsistent quality stitching on buttonholes, though most seams were ok. This is pretty lightweight fabric for denim, and not heavy enough to stand up to any kind of work (like gardening) but comfortable for casual wear. The edges of the pockets have minimal backing so might tear easily if subjected to pulling or stress. This shirt is a very generous cut (as expected for oversized) and even at that, it runs large. I ordered a small (5'2" / 118 lbs) but the sleeves are WAY too long (see photo) but ok with the cuff folded back one time. Also, the sleeves have a sewn-in button tab to help keep the sleeve rolled up to the elbow. I washed the shirt as directed (warm water, tumble dry) and didn't notice much appreciable shrinkage. Overall, this is a lightweight but comfortable generously-cut shirt appropriate for casual wear, but just a little too big to be comfortable for me. YMMV

ANRABESS Women Short Sleeve Tops Scoop Neck Ribbed Knit Slim Fitted Basic Tee Shirt Casual Summer Clothes 2026 Spring Outfits This is a great, basic, ribbed scoop neck top. ANRABESS Women Short Sleeve Tops Scoop Neck Ribbed Knit Slim Fitted Basic Tee Shirt Casual Summer Clothes 2026 Spring Outfits I have this shirt in Navy, Pink & the darker purple! They are my go to!! I needed something breastfeeding friendly but didn’t want it to look obvious and this shirt was perfect. I wear them on constant rotation. When I originally ordered them I was about 4-5 months PP and now I’m 9 months PP. I ordered a medium because I did not want something too tight fitting so I like that the medium has more give to it. The small did fit but I returned it because the medium felt better for me. Size reference I am 122lbs and the girls (used to be) a size 34DD - I also ordered a medium when I was closer to 130-135 and the girls were still perkier. So if you’re in between and not sure what size, hopefully my range helps you! They are super soft and comfortable, the pink one definitely has a different feel to it, maybe because of the different dye to make the shirt? So the blue and purple are softer but I really like all of them! My navy one is beading up a bit, but truthfully, I wear them constantly. Definitely a must have for the stretchiness, design and price!!

Joe's Womens Charlie Ankle Exposed Button Fly Is it possible that a pair of jeans can move a woman to tears? It seems ridiculous, but yes, almost. As soon as I pulled these Joe's out of the bag I immediately felt how luxurious the denim was. It's soft, yet sturdy, and they feel positively plush on the skin. Yep, never had a pair of Joe's before but I'm familiar with the brand and had been planning on trying at pair at Nordie's and purchasing if I was pleased. Well, these make me more than pleased. I'm ecstatic. First, the color of 'Stephaney' is somewhat darker than the product picture shows, but the Icon styling and wash pattern are the same. The stitching is very well done and meets the brand's claim of meticulous attention to detail. There are leather logo details on the inside back waist and on one rear pocket, and logoed button, zipper and rivets. The cotton has a soft brushed feel and the 2% spandex gives flex where needing and hugging where it flatters. It's woven and cut in a way that feels it will hold its shape better than other jeans that sag during the course of the day. The fabric has some substantial weight but isn't too thick or heavy. These don't feel like they'll thin out and lose their shape at all. I'm relatively curvy with a 31" waist and 41" hips. But hey, these size 29 jeans unexpectedly lay perfectly on my waist without creating a muffin top, and fit around my heavyish thighs. The waistband is sewn without topstitching which reduces bulk and creates a smoother profile. And I really love how my bum looks in these too! The pockets are well placed and the stretch gives a bit of a lift and a cradle. Best of all, they're incredibly comfortable. The inseam measures in at 28-3/4" and perhaps a scant long for my 5'2" frame for an ankle jean, but they work without needing to hem them — still perfect to wear inside a pair of boots or in warmer weather with a pair of flat sandals. So Joe, you and me, same time next year for another pair in the new season. Or maybe sooner for a different style for spring/summer ;D

Im 5 foot and a petite teenager. My waist is a 32 to 33 and I bought these jeans in a size XS! I will say the waist is very nice for that baggy fit, however in size length, the pants are too long for me. They go a tiny bit over my feet with shoes on, and completely cover them without shoes. If youre a shorter girl, take note that these would fit better with platformed shoes. Other than that, i love these jeans. They are comfy and i will definitely be buying more!

I ordered the washed black/gray color. I really liked the color. I also really like that they're true to size as well as the fact that they're super high-rise. They have a 12-in rise, which I discovered I really like for wearing with crop tops. For me personally, I like that I expose less skin that way. Anyhow, I decided to return these because I wasn't completely in love with how they look on me. As others have mentioned, the material is a tiny bit on the stiffer side which means they don't sit in as flattering of a way as it does in the pictures. I wish they draped more loosely down the legs rather than stiff edges. I'm going to try a pair in a different color with distressing to see if it fits any more to my liking. It may also be that I don't wear jeans like this and I'm just not used to how it looks on me. I do wish they were more fitted in the butt so it was more flattering on the booty. These look good with platform sneakers, but I didn't care for how they looked with ankle boots. They're very comfortable, but I kinda wish they were in inch or two longer. I'm 5'7", 123lbs, and 27" waist. The size small fit perfectly without being too tight. The waist was 13.5" across on the size small. UPDATE: I ordered a pair in the sky blue color with distressing. The material is much softer and sits better. More like the stock picture. I like the feel and look of this pair better than the washed gray jeans, but I ultimately decided to return them because I like the gray color better and would be more inclined to wear that color with my wardrobe. I also think I'm just not used to wearing this style of jeans and feel a little bit self-conscious about how they look on me. Which is a bummer because they're very comfortable and I still like how they look with crop tops. My only concern with the sky blue pair is since the material is softer and more flexible, I'm not sure the durability/longevity of it. But since they won't stretch out like skinny jeans do, I think it would be just fine. Also, I found the sizing between the two colors to be fairly consistent. 12" rise, 14" waist, 31" inseam, and 10.5" ankle opening.

user_comment: First, I love these jeans! Ordering was confusing because I couldn’t tell the difference between “light blue” and “light blue denim.” I ordered “light blue,” in two sizes (my current size + one size larger) hoping one would fit. I like extra-baggy jeans, but it’s been years since I had any, and it’s always tough to tell how the waist and hips will fit without trying them on. My plan was to return a pair, but I love both. I’m leaning towards the XL pair in terms of fit because they have enough room in the waist to be super comfortable, but not so much that they look frumpy. The L pair fit great, too, but may not be as comfy and probably won’t play well with food babies or bloating. They were snugly comfortable during a quick try-on. My Old Navy jeans size is currently 12, for reference. I’m 5’3” and these touch the ground, but nothing a pair of platforms or a hem couldn’t fix. I love how roomy the legs are. They take me straight back to the 90s. I have always loved this style of denim. I just feel like myself. I can’t wait to wear these with some crop tees. The only downside to these jeans is that I think they are likely inconsistent in color. See my photos. Both pairs are supposedly the same color, but I strongly prefer the pair on the left with a more subtle distressing. The pair on the right has more dark edges and contrast between the worn part and the less worn part. Oh, the most important part for a pair of jeans: my butt looks great in them. Lol. Winners!

I have not yet washed and dried them but the length right out of the package is great. Though I’ll be glad if they shrink a bit shorter. They also loosen up through the day. My ONLY complaint is that I felt they didn’t look quite as barrel in real life as the photo BUT I still like them and the fit and will probably get a pair in a different color.

Just received these pants today and I truly DO love them!! I gave 4 stars ONLY because the quality is not top notch. That said, for the price I would 100% recommend these pants. If you're expecting FP quality, you're going to pay FP price! These pants are constructed with a thinner fabric that doesn't really feel like a true denim, but that doesn't bother me. They're comfortable with a normal amount of stretch to them. There were a few loose threads, but no holes anywhere and everything is intact, so not a big deal. The button feels like it may need to be reinforced, but again, not a big deal. With all that said, these pants are ADORABLE and I know I'll get a lot of wear out of them! For reference, I'm 5'6" 170 lbs and I ordered a size L, which fit me PERFECTLY. Not too tight in the waist, and length hits me right at the ankle. If you're thinking of ordering a pair of cheetah barrel jeans, GET THEM, for real.

These jeans are warm and cozy as advertised, almost too warm to wear indoors if you’re also wearing a warm top. They feel durable. But they are definitely not the flattering jeggings I am used to wearing. The petite size 6 hits my 5’2” 115 lb frame just above the navel at the top and at the top of my foot at the bottom. But with a boxy waistline they are not flattering at all. They make my mom tummy look worse than it is. I’m torn between keeping them because they are so comfortable or returning them because they aren’t attractive on my frame. Maybe the size 4 would be better.
I’m 5’10 and rather curvy and these jeans fit perfectly! So comfortable and cute can’t wait to wear them to the rodeo

I deliberated quite a bit before ordering these jeans, because based on the other styles that I saw pictured by customers, I wasn't sure if they had seams down the front and patches in the back with no pockets. I finally crossed my fingers and hoped they would look just like the product pictures -- and hallelujah, they do! The style I am wearing is Vintage-blue05 Straight, which is vastly different from some of the other styles. Those other styles aren't for me -- I was looking for something reminiscent of the boyfriend jean trend in the late 90s, and that's exactly what I got! But with cute leopard star patches on the back pockets! These jeans are pleasantly faded, have a nice thick quality, and definitely outrank my 90s jeans because they have great stretch to them, too. They are SO COMFORTABLE, it's ridiculous. I could sit cross-legged in these with no problem. They are going to be so comfortable to wear all day. They can be dressed up with a pair of booties or kitten heels, or dressed down with sneakers or Birkenstocks (or other slides). I can wear a t-shirt and oversized blazer with them, or a cropped sweater or sweatshirt, or a tank top on the weekends -- they are very versatile, especially with that cute leopard print touch on the back pockets. And the back pockets are real pockets, with the patches sewn over them (I know in another style a reviewer noted their patches were sown directly over the seat of the pants with no fabric underneath -- that is NOT the case with this style). The sizing is perfect. I'm 5'6" and 130 lbs and the small is perfect. They don't arrive with a ton of wrinkles like most of the clothes you get online, and they have no weird smells. They're well sewn and seem durable and high-quality. Overall, these rival any jeans I could have picked up at a department store or a higher-end boutique for a lot more, so I rate them an OUTSTANDING value at this price point, and highly recommend them if you're looking for a super-comfy, Y2K throwback pair of slouchy boyfriend jeans that you can wear with anything. I would give these 10 stars if I could!

These are really comparable to a softer and warmer version of Pajama Jeans. I wear these to work and I feel like I’m wearing soft cozy fleece sweats. I normally wear a size XL (US 16) and the XL fits me really well, but they’re a tiny bit too long for my 5’6 frame. They did leak some dye when I first washed them, so I recommend only washing with very dark clothing on a cold setting. The pockets are large and they feel well made. I hope they come out with a version of these suitable for hot summers!

I love these so much! They are super warm, stretchy, and so flattering! I agree with others about sizing up! I got a size 8– I am 5’2”, 135lb, and normally wear a 4 in other jeans, or a S/M in sweats or leggings. I also usually have an issue with muffin top bc most of my weight is in my stomach/butt area. These are a tiny bit snug on my waist but I have that issue with pretty much all pants, and these are stretchy enough that it isn’t uncomfortable/digging in. They fit like a glove everywhere else and make my butt look amazing lol! The last picture is how they looked with a slightly cropped sweater. I originally bought them for a trip to a very cold destination and they indeed kept me super warm. They don’t look any different than regular jeans, though! Like they’re not weirdly thick looking or anything. I’ve gotten so many compliments and sent this link to a bunch of people lol. Plan on buying the other colors for the cold east coast winter! Highly recommend!

True Religion Women's Stella Low Rise Skinny Jean Ok, first of all, these are authentic. All pockets are real pockets, and they are extremely well made. I'm 5'0", 108 lbs, and I bought the 27s. The fit is PERFECT, but I will definitely need to have them hemmed. For now I've been folding the excess pant leg up and under, and it works just fine, because the fit is slim enough that it stays in place all day without budging.I can't gush enough about how thrilled I am to have found these jeans. The high rise pant trend needs to die. It's not really flattering on anyone, but it is murder on us short girls. At only 5'0", even "low rise" jeans come up so high on me that the waist band always covers my belly button. Some "low rise" jeans come up so high on my torso I can practically tuck my tits into them. Not only is it crazy unflattering, but it's incredibly uncomfortable. The jeans always dug into my stomach when I sat down. For years I have had to rearrange the waistband of my jeans every time I sat. I so missed the 90's style, "sit so low on your hips you flash your crack every time you sit" jeans. They were so much more comfortable and flattering on me! I've been searching for years, but couldn't find anyone that still makes a true "sits on the hips" jean. And then I found these! I couldn't believe it when I put them on. After trying on hundreds of pairs of "low rise" jeans that came up to above my belly button, these actually sit right at my hip bone, right where they should. And unlike the 90's low rise jeans, these fit so well they don't have that weird gap in the back of the waistband when you sit, and they don't flash your undies. They stay put. They are AMAZING. So flattering and comfortable.The only slight drawback is that the back pockets do sit a pretty low, so they're not the most flattering butt jeans ever. And if you're someone who is constantly shoving your phone in your back pocket like I am, the pocket flap is not the most convenient thing ever. But those are minor issues for me, considering how overall thrilled I am with these. After the first pair arrived and I saw how well they fit I immediately ordered two more pairs, and they are equally amazing. Love 'em.

True Religion Women's Stella Low Rise Skinny Jean I’m obsessed w jeans that have back flap pockets. Mainly bc I have no booty and back flap pockets gives the illusion of a little more in the trunk. They are a darker blue Jean w a red stitch just like picture and has a cute tiny pocket in front (see pic). These jeans are low rise but manageable enough due to the decent stretch material not to give u much muffin top. They are stretchy but not jeggings and do not sag after wearing all day which is a huge thing for me. ! Material is comfortable and soft. It’s not a heavy Jean but not a thin one either. I have two pairs of these Stella jeans (another in black) both low rise. I was nervous about size but I went with my usual 26 and they fit perfectly. The rhinestone jeans in dark blue are a nice alternative to my usual black skinny jeans. The fabric is very soft and not stiff like some other jeans fabrics and is stretchy. The color of the jeans is not exactly dark blue, is more of a lighter blue with some distressed marks that look pretty. The rhinestones are a nice feminine detail and make the jeans stand out from the traditional jeans. I am 5'2" tall, 135lbs, 29" waist, 41" hips and size 14 fits well from the hips, which is the most difficult part for me to find a good fit. The jeans are medium rise and not completely high rise, with medium rise the jeans fit well from the waist but if I wear them as high rise then they are big from the waist. The jeans form some creases below the zipper and to the sides but wearing them medium rise helps. The jeans are a little long on me and if I wear them with booties, they are ok but if I wear them with flat shoes then they feel long. The fit is straight and they are not wide leg. I wash the jeans inside out, using a cold, delicate cycle and allow them to air dry to protect the rhinestones. They take long time to dry but I want to preserve them as much as possible. I enjoy wearing them with cute tops.
reviewer_name_raw: Feminine Jeans with a Straight Fit

user_comment: I ordered these Sidefeel Women's Straight Leg Jeans in the (8, Light Blue) version... and I LIKE THEM!!! They're so cute, comfy and they fit me well. For reference, i am 5'5ish, 148ish pounds, 34D and 27" waist. Here's what I like about these pants: •Correctly made garment, free of defects •Stretchy midweight denim material that doesn't lose its shape throughout the day •Super fun crystal applique detail •Cute cut that isn't too baggy •Light blue color is fun and summery •Can be dressed up or down, depending on your shirt, shoes and accessories •Wash up nicely, I hung them to dry •Fully functioning zipper, button and pockets Overall, these jeans are fun! Would I wear them more than a couple of times a month? Probably not... But, they're cute for a special event or dress up party. At the time of ordering these jeans were a little less than $40-- which is a good value for the money, especially considering how cute they are. RECOMMEND!!!

These jeans are really cute. I love the wide leg and the rope belt at the waist. The elastic waist is wide, and the style of these jeans is casual. I currently wear a size 14, and I ordered the size 12 because I am actively losing weight right now. The size 12 fits nicely, though they fit me as fitted jeans, as opposed to loose jeans. I'd say, follow the sizing chart because these fit true to size. I am short, 5ft 2", and the jeans are perfect full length on me. Something that I don't have to hem! The fabric is NOT stretchy. The waistband has nice stretch, and the denim is loose in that there is extra fabric gathered into the waistband, but the actual denim is traditional cotton, non-stretchy denim. I ordered the blue color, and it looks like the color on the product page. I am really happy with these jeans. I wore them out this morning, and they were comfortable and cute. They pair well with a fitted top tucked in, or a tank top with a light open cardigan.

user_comment: The style is not at all as pictured, like the pictures on the listing can't even be real. These are NOT low-rise boyfriend jeans, and the high-waisted straight front fit is not flattering at all. The dark blue is a medium blue. These are not distressed, the material is a thick and stiff, but if you want heavy duty working jeans these might be for you. These have NO stretch at all and the section of elastic on the back waistband might as well not even be there, it only adds a trivial amount of stretch and looks a little tacky. I'm 5'3" 160 lbs, usually a large and sometimes a medium in pants, but these size large are too small for me. The length is perfect and the fit through the legs is good, but the fit through the hip and waist is very narrow with no give at all.

The style is not at all as pictured, like the pictures on the listing can't even be real. These are NOT low-rise boyfriend jeans, and the high-waisted straight front fit is not flattering at all. The dark blue is a medium blue. These are not distressed, the material is a thick and stiff, but if you want heavy duty working jeans these might be for you. These have NO stretch at all and the section of elastic on the back waistband might as well not even be there, it only adds a trivial amount of stretch and looks a little tacky. I was really hoping these jeans would work, but the sizing is way off. I’m a size 15/16 in bottoms and 5'8" tall, and these wouldn’t even come close to buttoning, they run small, at least two sizes smaller than expected. My daughter, who is a size 10/12, tried them on, and while they fit her waist, they were super baggy from the hips down. The length was also very long on her (she’s 5'3"), so maybe heels would help, but overall the proportions feel inconsistent. The price is fair if you can find the right fit, because the denim quality itself is actually good. Unfortunately, the sizing and fit issues make it frustrating to recommend without trying them on first and I got the largest size they had.

I like these jeans. I did not like the elastic waistband at all at first, but it actually helps them fit at the right place and feel more comfortable. As long as you're wearing a longer shirt, no one will see it. the legs are wide. I like them wider but they match the pics. There are a few pics where the color isn't accurate. I wasn’t sure what to expect with these jeans since I haven’t worn elastic-waist denim since I was a toddler, but I was pleasantly surprised! The cut is modern with a wide leg, and the elastic waistband doesn’t have a ton of stretch, which actually works in its favor—it lays flat without bunching and looks more structured than I expected. The denim feels sturdy, the seams are clean and tight, and the pockets are nicely sized. For reference: I’m 5'3", 126 lbs, with a 30" waist, 40" hips, and a 27" inseam. I ordered my usual size M. I could get them over my hips and button them, but they’re definitely snug. A size L would likely fit better in the hips and waist, but would also be too long on me. Even though these are advertised as low rise, they fit more like a high rise on my frame—the waistband sits about an inch above my navel. Key takeaways: If you’re curvy or carry more in the hips, consider sizing up. If you prefer the jeans to sit lower on your hips (as “low rise” suggests), you’ll likely want to size up as well. Overall, these are well-made, stylish jeans—I just wish the rise matched the description a bit better.

I ordered these Sidefeel Women's High Waisted Straight Leg Jeans in the (6, Iceland Blue) version... and I LIKE them!!! They fit me well. For reference, I am 5'5ish, 148ish pounds, 34D, 27" waist, and an hourglass figure. These jeans are definitely MOM jeans. Here's what I like about them: •Correctly made garment, free of defects •Medium thick, very stretchy, super soft material •Material doesn't lose its shape throughout the day •Figure flattering cut overall, even the throwback reminiscent v-detail in the front •Not too baggy •Has functional pockets and a nice zipper •A pretty light blue color that is on trend for summer and early fall •Wash up nicely and only shrink incrementally in the dryer Overall, these jeans are a great, comfortable pair to have in your closet! At the time of ordering they were a little less than $32-- and because of all of the above, I feel like they are a great value for the money! MOM APPROVED!! RECOMMEND!!! These jeans are SO COMFORTABLE and really flattering! I love the cut of the front, it makes your waist look smaller. The fabric is so soft! I ordered a size 10 and I would say they are fairly true to size although a little long on me as I am only 5'4". I folded the bottom twice and it gave them the cuffed look which I think is cute! Due to the stretch, I think I could have gone down to an 8 comfortably. I love that they have multiple colors, I can't wait to order them in a darker wash! I did wash and dry them, they did not shrink and held up very well.

Old school Rockie jeans vibes without the tight denim! These hands down just became my new favorite pair of jeans. And I’m a hard core wrangler girl every day of the week. These have that iconic Rockies type stitching in the front. But these actually have back pockets. And there is so much comfort and stretch in these jeans. I’m normally a 10/12 and ordered a 10 and fairly certain an 8 would be just right so I’m going back for a size down! I normally wear a 34 in length to stack on my boots and these were great at right at a 33 inch inseam if I had to guess. They fit over my Ariat cruiser slip ons and didn’t drag the floor but didn’t flood when I sat either. Super happy with these jeans. And at under $50 these are a win win!

The jeans are nice. I would say a medium thickness, they have a nice stretch. They actually feel like denim. The color is the same as it appeared on Amazon. The detailing around the button is nice and fashionable. The quality overall is pretty good. My only complaint is that they are a little too short for me, 5 foot 7. I would only be able to wear them with flats and I think after I wash them once they might shrink up a little bit. So I would not advise longer legged women to purchase these unless they come in long length. They are very true to size. Many times clothing looks different online and when it arrives, it is not what was expected. These are very true to photos and descriptions.

I ordered these for my wife who is a big fan of wide leg jeans that do not flair and these are exactly what she wanted. The fit is true to size and surprisingly these almost fit "short girl" length. The inseam is approximately 31" and while my wife's is 28" (she's 5'2) these come a lot closer to fitting than most. The stitching is similar to nsme brand jeans and they have 4(not 5) pockets. Nice "worn" wash feel gives a broken in feel and appearance. Quality craftsmanship at the snap and zipper should make for long lasting wear. Overall a great bargain find !

I decided to order the Lesore Womens High Waist Wash Denim Wide Leg Straight Leg Jeans Pants with Cuffed Hem, as am in my major jean rehaul era. For reference, I'm 5 ft tall, 120 lbs and normally wear a small, 4-6 in most clothing, and can range anywhere between a 26-28 in jeans. Unlike some black rinse jeans, this doesn't have an unpleasant smell. Interestingly enough, the fabric is a blend of 61% Cotton, 31% Polyester, 7% lyocell and 1% Spandex. Here, I decided to order a size up to a 6, as I wasn't sure how the elasticity would be in general. Thankfully, this is a light to medium weight denim with more stretch than I expected and the size 6 fit just right with plenty of room to breathe. I honestly would've been just fine with a 4, as it sits a little bit low, but doesn't fall off my body. Construction all around is mostly solid, but I'm not too crazy about the plastic feeling buttons, which makes me wonder how long those will actually last. Since I'm a bit shorter, it falls just at my ankles which I expected and am totally fine with. Overall, the color is on point, they're nice enough for their price point and are super comfy in general.

Here, I decided to order a size up to a 6, as I wasn't sure how the elasticity would be in general. Thankfully, this is a light to medium weight denim with more stretch than I expected and the size 6 fit just right with plenty of room to breathe. I honestly would've been just fine with a 4, as it sits a little bit low, but doesn't fall off my body. Construction all around is mostly solid, but I'm not too crazy about the plastic feeling buttons, which makes me wonder how long those will actually last. Since I'm a bit shorter, it falls just at my ankles which I expected and am totally fine with. Overall, the color is on point, they're nice enough for their price point and are super comfy in general.

I wasn’t sure if I was going to like the extra wide cuff on the leg bottoms, so I ordered the black color. The hem looks less inconspicuous in black than the other colors. Ends up, that I really do like these jeans, hem & all! The color has a slightly faded, worn in look. I like that. The jeans are definitely high waisted, almost too high for my height (I’m 5’3”). The length, on me is “normal”, not ankle length as shown in the photos. Of course, that’s also because of my height and I’m not complaining. I like the longer length on me, to be honest. The fabric has a nice stretch to it, which is much appreciated. The fit is more relaxed than expected. Not a problem, but does have a slight gap at the back waist area on me. These are quite comfortable to wear. Overall, I do really like them. Just expect them to look differently, if you’re on the shorter side, like me.

Meh, I am on the fence with these. I don’t think they are worth the current price of $36. I am 5’4” and generally wear a 14 or 16 depending on the cut. I usually err on the side of caution when ordering online and size up. The color is true to the website photos. Material is your standard Jean material with a little stretch. So for the overall fit they are okay. Waist fits fine, no CT, thighs and calves fit as expected, however where I run into an issue is the inseam length. These are just a tad too long to wear with anything other than something with at least a two inch heel. They are sewn cuffed so it would be a pain to alter. For my body type I prefer ankle length on wide or straight leg pants as shown on the model in the website photos. I think these would look better on someone taller in a size 16.

These are actually a really nice pair of jeans. They’re soft and stretchy. The color is really pretty. They fit true to size, I got a 16 and they fit good! The only thing I don’t like is that I’d like them to be a little shorter (like shown in the photo). I’m 5’8 and they’re a bit like high waters on me.

I’m 5’4”, around 215 lbs, and postpartum, so I’ve been in the process of updating my fall wardrobe to fit my new body and hopefully find pieces that make me feel good again. I ordered these jeans in a size 16, and I was pleasantly surprised with how comfortable they are. They have a nice amount of stretch without feeling too thin or flimsy, and the high waist gives me some support in the midsection, which is something I really appreciate right now. The overall fit is flattering, and I like that the wide-leg cut is on trend — it makes them feel current without being too “young” for me to wear confidently. What threw me off at first was the cuffed hem. I wasn’t sure if it was supposed to be a trend I hadn’t heard about, so I even asked my teenage daughters. They said it’s not really a thing, at least not around here, and I’d agree. I ended up unrolling the cuffs, and once I did, I loved how the jeans draped — they have a nice wide-leg/flare look that is very stylish right now. That said, my biggest complaint is the hem itself. The jeans come with a raw edge at the bottom, which makes them look unfinished and honestly a little cheap. Even if they were designed to be cuffed, I think a proper sewn hem would make them look much more polished and higher quality. For the price, I’d still say they’re decent, but I’ll probably have someone sew a real hem onto them so I can get more wear out of them without worrying about fraying. Overall, these jeans are comfortable, flattering, and affordable, but just be prepared that the raw hem might not be to your liking unless you’re into that look or willing to get them hemmed properly.

This style of jeans is something new for me! The thighs are a bit more loose than skinny jeans and the high waist is a new aspect. Having a high waist is definitely flattering on my post-pardum body, especially my belly area. I am 5’5” and weigh 145. I ordered the size 8 and it fits pretty great.The material has some stretch to it but it is not on the extremely stretchy side. You can comfortably sit. The pockets are real and have depth to them. I enjoy the big cuffs at the bottom and the length is just right for my height to wear with flats. The instructions say that you can use a warm iron, so I will do some pressing to the cuff area. The fraying on the cuff is cute and obviously intentional.The sewing is good and it seems to be made with quality. These feel good to put on and I’m excited to try out this newer style!

This dress is great in person. I gave it 4 stars due to the length. I ordered both a medium and large for me to try on. I’m petite (4’11”) and a little busty (C cup) but am a bit heavier than normal right now (160lbs.). The medium fits a little snug on my chest - I could wear it and make it work and I love the length which hit right above the knee. The large was more roomy and comfortable on the chest but a little longer than I’d like (falls below the knee). I decided to go with the large mainly bc we hope to get pregnant again soon and I wanted some room for a growing belly and bust lol. The fabric is a soft, lightweight denim and very comfortable! Not thick which is great for south Alabama. The buttons are functional too which is great for breastfeeding later. I would say it fits true to size.

FIT: 36 C/D with a little room. I have a 40in overbust measurement. I am wearing a 36D Vanity Fair Beauty Back Bra which i belive is a minimizer. -I am 5'3" with a short torso so this is knee length on me. 156lbs. SIZE/COLOR: LARGE. ROADNIGHT BLUE. Cotton poly blend, medium weight denim. PROS: Seems to be well sewn. Has extra button sewn to side tag. -acid washed that doesn't look too dated. CONS: Very boxy. -bust button gaps. I am wearing a white tank to lessen the boxy look so this is a non issue. -medium weight denim so a little warm for our coastal summer. -finding shoes that don't make the wash look dated is tough. I ordered this in a size 6 color blue for my daughter. For reference she's 5'4'' and about 120 to 125lbs. She has a slim athletic build. I probably should or could have ordered a size 4 for her. The size 6 is a little roomy and wears baggy like I guess she wanted it to fit. They do have some stretch to them. They have a cuffed hem and adjustable shoulder straps and multiple pockets, two in front, two in back on pockets and one in the center. The denim overalls are made with a multi fabric blend consisting of cotton, polyester, and viscose which makes them feel soft and thick; they don't feel cheap and thin. They are well made; the threading is intact in the seams. They are short overalls; they fit high up on the thigh; not sure how many inches they are. They aren't too short though. Overall I think these are super cute retro overalls.

I got a size 6, which is my actual size (I'm 5' 7"). The fit is weird. The overall fit is correct, I wouldn't get a larger or smaller one. However, while the hips are an exact fit, the waist is very wide on me due to the straight cut. If I wear a crop top, you can easily see my undergarments. The shoulder straps are the correct length. The flap in front is very small. I wouldn't call the legs wide legged, they are straight and long. On me, the legs are about 6 inches too long. I understand the fashion of long legged pants, but these are ridiculously long on me. I try to tuck them into my socks but there is too much excess fabric here. I would have preferred that the flare the pants at the bottom instead of making them long. The material is exactly as expected. It's a nice black denim, the same thickness as my average pair of jeans. They are a cotton poly blend, but they feel like traditional cotton denim to me. It feels durable to me. I feel no stretch to the fabric. It is comfortable to wear, the extra space mean that these move easily with my body. However the extra long legs are always something I need to be weary of, so I don't trip over them. I will need to get them hemmed. The stitching is well done throughout. My other problem with the styling is the huge patch that says "Fashion" on the bib pocket. It seems tacky to me, I'm embarrassed to wear these without covering the patch. It is labeled machine wash cold. I laundered them just once, and they held up fine, with no fading or shrinking. However, I dried them on low heat tumble, since I didn't know if they were pre-shrunk. Overall I think the value is just mid on these. The quality of the denim and stitching is great, but there are fit and style issues.
reviewer_name_raw: Weird fit, feels like mens pants

I'm 5'2" and they were plenty long enough for somebody taller than me. Other than the straps the closure is a zipper and a button on the side of the waist. They really feel like putting on jeans. The fabric itself is denim. I like that because the fabric doesn't stretch out while wearing it all day.

Sidefeel Women's Skinny High Waisted Jeans They are stretchy but thick! Not see through or cheaply made. I have a big natural butt, hips, and a small waist. Based on the reviews, I sized down to a 10 instead of a 12. So glad I did! These are a must buy if you want quality white jeans!!! I’m 5’1”, curvy at 170 pounds. My weight is distributed in my lower areas. Hips, butt, thighs. They are soft enough to wear on my belly button incisions since I just had two surgeries in 6 weeks while still having a skinny Jean appearance. Can’t wait to wear them tomorrow!!!
