I got these in the light blue color with the elastic waistband and drawstring because I already have several dark blue pairs of regular jeans and have been wanting a pair of baggy jeans for awhile. For reference I am 6’ tall, currently weigh 165lbs, and have a 32” waist. I usually wear a size M-L but I’ve learned from previous experience to size up when buying clothing off here. Since I wanted a baggy fit I ordered the XXL and it worked perfectly! It fits more like an XL like I knew it would and gives me that perfect 80s baggyness all us Gen X loved so much. As far as the length goes, these go past my ankles in the XXL size so if you are a tall woman then you are definitely safe with the XL and XXL sizes. Possibly even Large but I can’t be sure since there isn’t a size chart. These jeans are honestly made way better than I expected. They are also really comfortable as well. They are stitched and serged well meaning they should hold up to washing and wear for quite awhile. They are what I would call a medium thickness and have four pockets that are all pretty deep. So far they’ve come in pretty handy when my hands have been full. I’m really happy I got these and may end up repeatedly washing and living in them at this point.

The quality of these cargo jeans is great—the material feels durable and the construction is solid. I like the classic look of the DarkBlue color. However, the sizing is a major issue. I ordered a large based on the recommendation (size chart not offered) and while the fit was decent through the hips and legs, the waist was a huge disappointment. It has zero stretch and is disproportionately small, making it uncomfortably tight on my 32 inch waist. The only "baggy" part of these jeans was the saggy backside, which was not the look I was going for. These jeans could be a fantastic buy if the sizing was more consistent or if you have a specific body type with a much smaller waist-to-hip ratio. For most people, you'll likely need to size up for the waist and be prepared to deal with a baggy fit elsewhere.

HDE Women's Plus Size Skinny Jeans with Pockets These are basic looking pull up jeans. The material is relaxed and not stiff. They’re really stretchy and comfortable. They have working front and back pockets. The front pockets are pretty big. They don’t look like fake jeans like other pull up jeans like this. I’ve ordered a few pairs of other pull up jeans and returned them because they looked like fake jeans. You can’t tell that these are not regular jeans. Where the zipper would be is sewn as if there is a zipper. The only thing that gives them away is the solid band around the waist and there is no button. As long as your shirt covers the waist, you can’t tell that these aren’t regular ol jeans. The legs are pretty long. They’re too long for me and they bunch up around my ankles and throughout my legs. I’m 5’2” but my legs are kind of short. I ordered the 2X and they are too big. They are too baggy every where and I definitely need a belt with them. I’ve gained a lot of weight and my waist and stomach are where most of my weight is so I was surprised that they were loose even at the waist. I might buy a pair in the next size down & hope they fit better because I really like them & they’re priced good. I’m just not sure about the long length of the legs. Overall I really like these jeans. If you’re looking for some pull up, stretchy, comfy jeans & you don’t have short legs then I would recommend them! 4 stars- because they are way too long for me.

HDE Women's Plus Size Skinny Jeans with Pockets Okay, the first thing any woman wants to know about a pair of jeans is: how are the pockets? These jeans have real pockets! Not just real pockets, but FIVE real pockets (the standard for jeans)! They're not as deep as men's jeans' pockets, but I can fit my entire hand in the front pocket, something I can't say about most of the women's jeans I've tried on. My large phone (iPhone 12 Pro Max) fits comfortably in the front pocket too. That said, these are technically jeggings and not true jeans, as they have no fly or waist opening (not a major issue for women, but don't you dare take away a man's fly), but beyond that, they feel like regular jeans, and will probably wear like regular stretch jeans. They're not for the most rugged work, but then, stretch jeans generally aren't. The fit is good, but I would recommend sizing down if you want a tight fit. I sized by the measurements, and mine are a little baggy. I'm happy with the inseam length—usually I find pants to be on the long side, with my 27" inseam, but these are just about right, with only a small amount of bunching at the ankle.
reviewer_name_raw: Real pockets! But maybe size down

I am always weary of buying pants online. The fit can be questionable, but these are spot on. I am a typical size 4/27 waist and I went with the sizing chart and ordered accordingly, and these pants are amazing! They have a great denim weight to them, and have a little bit of stretch in the waist and thigh and butt to make them hug in the right places and balance out with a wider leg at the bottom for a flared but stylish look. They have not stretched while wearing them, so they have been staying on my waist and they are comfortable and just provide a great fit and look. I'm super pleased with this particular pair of denim, and they look great with casual or dress tops and both sneakers/sandals and boots.

Very cozy jeans! These were ordered in a size 16 for my model that is about 5'3 or so. In the product photos, they look like they are kind of short, and expose a good amount of ankle- but on a shorter lady in this size, they pretty much reach the floor, yet won't get caught under shoes. The waistband is both belted and stretchable. Comfortable and versatile, they look better in person, but I thought the photos, a size, and a height description would give a better idea how they fit on the shorter ladies. My wife likes them, and they'll get a lot of service. Great jeans for the price! Recommended.

I rolled the dice hoping I would receive the skinny jeans that so many other reviewers had received, but my gamble didn’t pay off. I received the wide legged, high rise jeans, and they fit and look just as advertised. The denim is durable and heavy, and the color is spot on - lavender. They don’t offer that much stretch, imo, I’m 5’5”, 125#, and they’re form fitting. And the jeans are shorter than I prefer, hitting just above the floor on me. They’re comfy enough, and I think they’re offered at a decent price ($22.99 @ the time of this review).

This is my second pair. I was so in love with my first pair I needed another. I am 5’1” and 140lbs, 16 weeks pregnant, and they work great for my body! I love the fact they don’t have a belly band! A bit long for a short girl bit still cute if you do one roll on the bottom!

Very nice maternity jeans, pretty color , thick material and fit well. This is my fourth pair of Flamingals jeans & my favorite pair so far! They are so comfortable! They hug nicely in all the right places but are stretchy, so I would recommend ordering a size smaller. I love the color & the fit & the length is perfect. Not too short or too long. My only complaint is that one of the front pockets was folded & sewn into the seam (one star off for that) but you can’t tell from the outside & I don’t use the pockets anyway. For reference I am 5’3”, 145lbs & ordered a size Medium.

Wrangler Women's Bespoke Lily High Rise Flare Jeans Denim Only buy these because they're somewhat cheap and they fit good. Worst “jeans,” that I have ever laid my hands on. Pockets are crooked with one higher than the other, jeans are NOT LOW RISE, they are mid rise, and the cut is bootcut. Im 5’9 128lbs, hourglass build. These are literally disgusting. Dont buy them.

I wanted some comfortable jeans to wear around the house. I was a huge fan of oversized pants in the early '00s like Caffeine, Tripp, and JNCO. Unfortunately, I tossed mine out in one move or another. Pros: The length on these is great, the denim is made of real cotton so it is breathable, the aged/vintage color is nice, the inserts up the sides are cute, they can be ultra low rise. Cons: I had to take up the waist band by two full inches on each side (total of 4 in) so they sit comfortably on my low hips without falling off, I think I need to use some elastic or something to make that a bit more comfortable. The denim is very light-weight, it is not comparable to heavy denim that I remember in JNCO jeans. Sadly, it came with a small hole. For reference, I am 5'8" and 120 lb, I bought size Large.

This is a pair of ladies hi rise jeans with straight legs in a size 8. I ordered a size larger because I was afraid they might be too tight. I should’ve ordered my normal size 6 because it would have been a perfect fit so they are true to size. The size 8 is a little big around my waist but I will still wear them. I am 5 foot nine so the jeans are a little too short for me. I will roll the legs up and wear them as capris. They are a faded blue in color. They have a stretchy fit to them so they do bend and move with you. I recommend if you order these, the only thing that you be aware of is the land and make sure they will fit you correctly. Other than length, they fit great and it looks great.

I ordered these jeans in my usual size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. There’s a lot of stretch to them so hopefully they will keep their shape and not get baggy or loose on me after some time. I’ll update if that’s the case. I really like this vintage color as it goes with so many tops. I’m 5’4” for reference on length. I weigh 132 lbs. These jeans feel thick and substantial, like a good quality jeans that will last a long time. I like that they’re more of a mid rise since I have short torso. They’re more flattering on me than super high waisted pants. These are cropped and will look cute with sandals or boots. The rise is 10” and the inseam is 27”. If you are petite, these will probably work too. They’ll just be regular length on you. I definitely recommend these flattering jeans. I think they are nice on the booty as well.

I recently purchased the Kensie Ella High-Rise Straight Leg jeans in a size 14, which is the largest size offered. For reference, I am 5'3" and weigh 195 lbs. I usually wear a size 16, but these jeans have a lot of stretch, so the size 14 fit me quite well. The fabric is soft and stretchy, which made them comfortable to wear. While I'm getting used to the straight-leg style, I do find the jeans to be flattering overall. However, I noticed some minor issues with the front, where the fit isn't as smooth as I would like. Additionally, the price is a bit on the higher side for what you get, which is something to keep in mind. All in all, these jeans are a nice addition to my wardrobe, especially if you're looking for a comfortable and stretchy option. Just be aware of the sizing and the fit in the front before purchasing.

These Utcoco High-Waisted Bootcut Jeans are amazing. I haven't worn jeans in a long while, since gaining a lot of weight and my jeans no longer fit. I was skeptical about getting these, but after trying them on, I'm so glad I did. These jeans are lightweight and extremely comfortable. The amount of stretch is remarkable. The smocked high waist controlled my muffin top, so there's no need for shapewear (unless I choose to tame my lower belly pooch). The stitching looks solid throughout and I'm particularly fond of the jean-covered buttons. These are some excellent quality jeans and a great value. They are versatile in that they're lightweight enough for warmer weather and thick enough for colder weather. The flare bootcut bottoms truly do make them more chic and flattering. There are infinite styling possibilities with these jeans and they're suitable for everyday wear, casual or dressed up. For reference: I'm 5' 7.5" with a 33" waist and 41" hips and the size large fits perfectly. I highly recommend these Utcoco High-Waisted Bootcut Jeans.

This is an absolutely fabulous pair of jeans. They are cute, stylish and unique. The fabric is soft, comfortable, with a slight stretch and medium thickness. These jeans really make a statement with the crunched up elastic, matching buttons and nicely finished cuffs. I am 5'7", 130 lbs and I ordered extra small. I wish I went with one size up. I think the small would fit me perfect. These are a little snug. Also, the inseam is more suited for somebody shorter than me. Even though they are not a perfect fit on me, it does not take away from the fact that these jeans are really nice and great quality.

I ordered these leggings for myself in a size large, which is my regular size. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and 165 pounds and they fit perfectly. I got them in the hot pink color, and yes, they are very pink. Think darker bubblegum, Pepto Bismol pink, which happens to be one of my favorite shades. These are made to look like denim skinny jeans, and while they obviously are not actual denim, they are cute and you can tell that is the look they were going for. (also, they have no functional pockets, but they are warm). That part honestly adds to the charm. They are high rise in the front, but the back dips down into a V shape, which is a little unusual. It did not bother me, but it is something to be aware of if you prefer a straight waistband all the way around. They are very soft and on the thicker side, which I really like. They feel well made and not flimsy at all. They also have the scrunch butt detail, which some people love for accentuating the backside. For me, they are mostly just comfy leggings for around the house. I plan to wear these with an oversized t shirt, or layered under sweats when it is cold, the same way I wear most of my leggings. They are buttery soft, comfortable, and cute enough to feel put together even when lounging. Easy 5 out of 5 stars. Thick, soft, well made, and a great pink.

Fabulous classic denim shorts with a relaxed look. They have a high rise that fits perfectly around my waist. The button closure and zip fly makes for easy putting on and taking off is a breeze and the zipper never gets stuck. At 5’4” and 175 lb. the length is perfect with a 7-inch inseam that falls a little lower than mid-thigh for me. The length and fit around my thighs make them the best fitting shorts I’ve ever owned. The hem is cuffed making them stylish and contemporary. The classic five pockets are perfect to carry all your small essentials easily. They are loose and stretchy making them cool and breathable in warmer weather. They let you move freely and are super comfortable against your skin. I can wear them all day without them losing their shape. The fabric is 58% Cotton, 24% Polyester, 16% Viscose, 2% Elastane. I chose the classic blue color which is true to the shorts pictured. They have a distressed look but not to be confused with the torn denim look. They are very versatile and can be worn with a simple T-shirt, sweatshirt, sweater, denim jacket or an elegant top. You can pair them with flip-flops, sandals, strappy heels or my favorite western boots. The care is also very easy: machine wash and dry on low and they come out perfect, ready to wear. Don’t be alarmed by the mom jeans description. They are truly flattering and I definitely recommend.

The hem is cuffed making them stylish and contemporary. The classic five pockets are perfect to carry all your small essentials easily. They are loose and stretchy making them cool and breathable in warmer weather. They let you move freely and are super comfortable against your skin. I can wear them all day without them losing their shape. The fabric is 58% Cotton, 24% Polyester, 16% Viscose, 2% Elastane. I chose the classic blue color which is true to the shorts pictured. They have a distressed look but not to be confused with the torn denim look. They are very versatile and can be worn with a simple T-shirt, sweatshirt, sweater, denim jacket or an elegant top. You can pair them with flip-flops, sandals, strappy heels or my favorite western boots. The care is also very easy: machine wash and dry on low and they come out perfect, ready to wear. Don’t be alarmed by the mom jeans description. They are truly flattering and I definitely recommend.

I ordered these to have an additional option for warm-weather wear, and I can already tell these cute, classic, 5-pocket jeans shorts will become one of my go-to items this summer. They’re high rise, which I prefer, and a good length. They extend mid-thigh on me (I’m 5’5”). Their fabric is 52% cotton and 42% synthetics, which makes them quite stretchy and giving and therefore comfortable to wear. I’m impressed with how well-constructed these are. No loose seams or threads but also attention to little details such as the stability of the cuffs. The cuffs are stitched in 4 places so they won’t unravel during wear or in the wash. Lovely! Finally, they’re sized as expected. I ordered my typical size, and they fit me fine.

These jeans are really cute, I love the style of the ends being frayed and the color wash on these jeans look great. These also have moderately deep pockets, which we all know is rare for women's pants. Unfortunately these did not fit me as expected, since they said mid rise I figured my normal size would be appropriate. But upon trying these on I realized I would have to wear them up over my belly button for them not to look baggy and extra long, even pulled up these still drag on the ground and I'm 5'8. The middle of my stomach is larger than my hips where I usually wear my jeans so this made wearing these a little too tight to be comfortable, I don't like the feeling of a button digging into my stomach. If you have a flat stomach and don't mind high rise jeans these may be right for you.

Cute pair of wide leg jeans that fit well in the waist and hips but they are a bit too short for me. More so than the inch I was expecting. I like the color and the look of the jeans though. I ordered the XS and I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 31-25-34 with a 32.5 inch inseam for reference. The size Small I recieved only had a 30 inch inseam. Otherwise, nice stretch and nice size pockets.

These jeans were ordered for my daughter who had the following feedback... I am frequently looking for cute, new, stylish jeans to wear. I was attracted to this pair, because of the flare fit. I was disappointed that the jeans were not true to the picture in the fit - the jeans are more of a bootcut fit than flare (as shown in the pictures). The color of the jeans is also different from the pictures online. The jeans have a yellowish-green hue which is not shown in the pictures online. I was a bit nervous about ordering a size "M" in jeans, because I typically wear a size 30". The waist of the pants fit nicely, but they were a bit short (I am 5'6" and have average length legs for reference). The fabric does have a nice stretch to it, because of the 1.5% spandex. The fiber content is fine, 51.% spandex, 68% cotton, and 30.5% polyester. These will not breathe like regular jeans and I could see them getting hot as they are worn. These jeans are easy to care for, because they can be washed on a cool cycle, but should be line dried. There are some positives for these jeans too. The front pockets are deep and the button and zipper closure work well. I appreciate that the button loops are reinforced with felt circles on the inside to prevent them from tearing over time. I also like the fringed hem on the jeans. Overall, these will be okay jeans to wear outside for yard work, but I am not overly impressed. The color and fit are not true to the pictures, so this is why they received three stars.

Pros: Go by the sizing chart and your measurements to get a good fit. I'm a size US16 and the XXL fits me perfectly. They have a high waist but the top is not so tight it will give you muffin top Nice deep pockets Good stretch so they are really comfortable when you're sitting The color and look is exactly as described The fabric and the quality of the build make it a solid investment at the expenditure of fiftyUSD Cons: These are really long. I have a them cuffed and the cuff is about 4 inches. This is not a con for me personally because I like the look but you may want to hem or cut them. I'm about 5' 5" for perspective. Overall, these are really nicely made and a good value for a cute pair of light colored jeans.

These jeans are stylish, and I’m glad I ordered them. I went with a size small, my usual here (5'7", 135 lbs, 34-27-37), and while they’re a little snug in the waist, I’d still call them true to size based on the chart. Comfort-wise, they’re great. The wide, open leg style gives them a nice, modern look. Problem with them is they really look like "Mom jeans", where the front puffs out just a bit and the pockets don't lie flat, also puffing out so I look a little wider than I am, especially since the waste sinches so much. Another drawback but not a dealbreaker is breathability. Some jeans feel lighter and more airy, while these are on the heavier side, trapping heat more than I expected. Case in point: I tried them on just before walking my dog in 80-degree weather at dusk, thinking the wide leg might help keep me cool. Spoiler—it did not. That said, aside from my legs cooking a bit, the jeans themselves were genuinely comfortable and easy to move in. Overall, stylish and comfy for everyday wear—just not my first pick for warm weather or sexy.
reviewer_name_raw: Stylish, Comfortable Jeans—But Don’t Expect Breathability

The denim fabric is light weight, soft and has just the right amount of stretch, making them comfortable and easy to wear all day/night. The zipper is a bit rough to zip and the button seems weak which is why I deducted a star. The bling detail is what really makes these jeans stand out; it adds just enough sparkle and style without being too flashy. You can easily dress these jeans up with heels and a blouse or keep it casual with a hoodie and sneakers. Quality is nice and if you want jeans that are eye-catching, comfortable and fun these are a great pick!

These jeans look good on me and fit me well (aside from the length), but I can’t rate them highly because the sizing and fit seem very off from what’s shown in the photos, plus they have the dreaded mothball smell that some denim has, which I have never successfully gotten to come out of any denim, ever. The fabric does have some stretch that makes these very comfortable ... if they fit you. The button at the waist has that cheap metallic rattle that makes me think of off-brand clothes I’ve seen at stores with odd names that also sell skintight neon club dresses — although it does have a single rhinestone on it, which is funny and kinda cute. Also, the tag says you can hand wash them even though the listing says dry clean only, so that’s okay. About that sizing: Normally, I wear an XS or S (with a 26 inch waist and 33 inch hips). I got these in small and they’re semi-fitted in the thighs and baggier on my lower legs, nowhere near the wide leg style that’s currently trendy. They definitely don’t look the way they look on the models. There’s a video review from Yvonne M. in the listing and these look just a little looser on me than on that reviewer. They are high-waisted, as described, but they’re also several inches too long and I’d have to cuff them seriously or have them hemmed to wear them (I’m 5’1”). I’m not sure these would fit like the product photos even if you size up, as the waist might be too big then, but if you do want the wide leg look, you don’t mind returning things, you’re not sensitive to smells, you’re tall or don’t mind hemming, and you’re really inspired to take a chance on these, then yes, I’d say get at least one size up. Otherwise, maybe take a pass.

As soon as my daughter saw these come out of the bag, she was thrilled! Finding a good fit is usually a challenge for her as she is between Girls large and Juniors Xtra small sizes and very petite (5 ft. 1 inch @ 78-80 lbs). While these are currently too long and loose on her (as seen in the photos), she loves them so much she has no issues keeping them until she grows into them. ​The Sparkle: It hits the "sweet spot." It’s not "little girl" glitter or overwhelming rough feeling rhinestones; it’s a sophisticated shimmer. ​The Cut: These are high-waisted and very classy. They definitely lean toward "dressy casual" rather than everyday school jeans. ​The Belt: It comes with a matching sparkle belt. While my daughter opted out of the belt, it’s a great accessory that can easily be styled with other outfits. ​Fabric & Care (Important!) Check the label carefully—these are Dry Clean Only. The blend is 73% Viscose, 25% Polyester, and 2% Elastane. Because of the high viscose content and the rhinestone embellishments, these cannot be tossed in the wash, not even gentle cycle. ​Maintenance: To keep the rhinestones secure and the viscose fabric from spotting, you'll want to avoid getting them wet. If they do get wet, blot them immediately and air dry flat. ​Storage: I’d recommend hanging these on padded hangers or storing them flat with tissue paper so the rhinestones don’t snag (less likely than larger ones but still possible) on other clothes or leave impressions on the fabric. For a pair of embellished, dressy wide-leg jeans, the quality feels appropriate for the price point. They feel like a "special occasion" softer denim rather than a standard pair of school jeans.

As soon as my daughter saw these come out of the bag, she was thrilled! Finding a good fit is usually a challenge for her as she is between Girls large and Juniors Xtra small sizes and very petite (5 ft. 1 inch @ 78-80 lbs). While these are currently too long and loose on her (as seen in the photos), she loves them so much she has no issues keeping them until she grows into them. ​The Sparkle: It hits the "sweet spot." It’s not "little girl" glitter or overwhelming rough feeling rhinestones; it’s a sophisticated shimmer. ​The Cut: These are high-waisted and very classy. They definitely lean toward "dressy casual" rather than everyday school jeans. ​The Belt: It comes with a matching sparkle belt. While my daughter opted out of the belt, it’s a great accessory that can easily be styled with other outfits. ​Fabric & Care (Important!) Check the label carefully—these are Dry Clean Only. The blend is 73% Viscose, 25% Polyester, and 2% Elastane. Because of the high viscose content and the rhinestone embellishments, these cannot be tossed in the wash, not even gentle cycle. ​Maintenance: To keep the rhinestones secure and the viscose fabric from spotting, you'll want to avoid getting them wet. If they do get wet, blot them immediately and air dry flat. ​Storage: I’d recommend hanging these on padded hangers or storing them flat with tissue paper so the rhinestones don’t snag (less likely than larger ones but still possible) on other clothes or leave impressions on the fabric. For a pair of embellished, dressy wide-leg jeans, the quality feels appropriate for the price point. They feel like a "special occasion" softer denim rather than a standard pair of school jeans.

user_comment: For reference, i'm 5'1 and 150 pounds.I ordered a small, xsmall would of been better. Over all pants were great quality, denim with plenty of stretch. When ordering size down ,im short and these drag . The drawstring works and can help if you have a smaller waist.

These jeans are gorgeous and if you have a little bit of a butt, these jeans are for you unfortunately, I do not. They’re good quality. The fabric is nice and heavy. They will be nice to wear for winter. I am 5 foot two and I typically wear a size 8, so I ordered the small but these are quite large on me. Unfortunately, they only go to a size small so I’ll have to return them, but they are definitely worth purchasing. I ordered the light blue jeans and they are exactly the color that are in the photo. The gems on the jeans are plentiful on the front and the back of the gene whereas I’ve seen some that only have gems on the front . They seem to be attached to the jeans very well. I don’t know if I would put them in the dryer and definitely wash them inside out. Don’t hesitate to buy them. They’re a good purchase and the price is appropriate.

Obsessed! The glitter detail makes these jeans look so unique and stylish. They fit perfectly at the waist and the wide leg is super flattering. Great for parties or a cute casual look. I’m 5.2 and around 130 pounds I ordered a size S and it fit perfectly!

Really sad these didn't fit because I liked them a lot. The sizes are not accurate at all and they run very small. I normally wear a US size 18 in Levi jeans so I figured ordering both a 2X and a 3X would be safe. I was completely wrong. The 2X, which is supposed to fit sizes 20-22 wouldn't even fit over my mid thigh. The 3X (says fits size 24-26) I was able to squeeze into, but they were pretty snug, if they weren't stretchy I wouldn't have gotten them on, plus the length was very short for someone my hight (I'm 5'11). Other than size, they were really nice pants and had a nice stretch to them, but returned them because they weren't a great fit and I wasn't going to waste almost $60
reviewer_name_raw: Very small, not accurate US sizing

user_comment: My daughter is a 20-year-old college student who lives for nights out, rooftop bars, and any excuse to dress up. These gray rhinestone flared jeans have officially become her party-uniform. The placing of the rhinestones is not too thick and reflects light nicely. The sparkle on these is unreal — they catch every bit of light in the room which seems to be trendy right now. It's that perfect “I didn’t try too hard but I definitely did” vibe. At 5'2" / 105 pounds (typically wear a size 0), we ordered a size Small and they have a loose fit. The high waist sits farther (more on hips) without a belt. The pants are long- great for heels or boots if you are short and don't want to step on the bottom. The flare balances out her shape, and everything is comfortable whether dancing or walking between spots with friends. The denim is not stiff, the moveability is a lifesaver after a long night. If you like nightlife, photos with good flash, or clothes that make you feel instantly confident, these jeans are so worth it.

These are so gorgeous. Straight out of package the rhinestones are GLEAMING. I expected to see little rhinestones loose in the bag when I pulled them out but no, all gems were still secure on the pants. I squated a few times in them and no gems came off then either. I would be cautious though if your thighs rub together, there are rhinestones between the thighs too. The jeans have minimal stretch, straight legs and a tapered waste. I typically wear a size 10 or 12 US and the large just barely fit me. Granted I am bloated this week, so hopefully they will fit more comfortably once the water weight goes down. I'm also 5' 9" and typically need long length or tall jeans. These hit me right at the ankle. I wish they were just a bit longer but for average length jeans they're not the worst. Also just fyi, they smell like burnt popcorn. Hopefully that will go away in a day or two after I air them out. Even so, I'll definitely be wearing these this holiday season.

I'm a longtime wearer of skinny jeans and have never found a pair of wide leg jeans that I like. I think my shape is a major factor here though. They arrived as pictured and described. Here's what I ordered, Fit Type: 26" Inseam | Size: Large Petite | Color: Ice Blue For reference, I am pear shaped, normally a size 6 in pants, 40" hips, 28" waist. I'm 5'3" tall, 135 lb. I ordered based on the size chart in the lower pics. Happy to say that they fit well. If you're like me, with a small waist and larger hips and behind, you'll love the fit of these, no gap. High waist is comfortable. The pockets are deeper than most. Uploading some photos so you can zoom in and get a closer look to see what I mean. The control top is comfortable, I wouldn't say that they have much slimming going on but not having buttons is definitely smoother and sleeker looking in general. Details & stitching all look good, stretchy. They are not really cropped at my height and I'm tempted to cut them a bit shorter. I feel like the wide legs just make me frumpy and are unflattering, especially when this wide. I washed them on cold and hung dried. I don't feel like they shrunk but I'll update after more wear and washes if that changes. Super comfortable and a decent value at the time of my order. Nice weight, they're not thin. Recommended for other body types. I wouldn't order them again with my shape though, just personal preference.

Cute jeans!!! I am 5'4" and the length is a little weird for me - not short enough to look short on purpose. But I will still wear them. ; ) Pros: +thick high-quality stretchy denim +cute style! Cons: -smells chemically. Hopefully comes out in the wash -pockets are uneven (see photo), but hopefully not that noticeable?

My husband and I were pleasantly surprised. When I first pulled them out of the package, I thought they looked somewhat cheap and wasn’t sure if I wanted to try them on. Once I put them on and showed my husband, he said they looked like quality jeans, similar to the name brands I normally wear. I felt confident enough to wear them out. Then a few others commented on how nice they looked on me. They’re soft and very comfortable to wear. I didn’t have that need to change my pants into something comfortable when I got home, like I normally do with regular jeans. These feel like comfy leggings. I like the high waist* (see below) When it comes to my pants and jeans, I love that form fitting stretch, and these have it. Fit well and hugs me in the right places. The stretch held up well too - didn’t wear out and get too baggy or saggy over time. My only cons - back pockets, waistband, length options, variety/colors, and cost. Back pockets - Position them in towards the middle (crack) and up just a bit. I think this helps enhance the appearance of your butt. With age and weight-loss, I’ve lost my butt and need all the help I can get. Help a girl out! lol Waist - I like the higher waist but could use a bit more strength to secure my mommy tummy. When I sit, the top band is tight, as it should be with this style, but then my pooch pops out a bit. Again, help us moms out a little more. Length - Offer short, regular, and tall. I’m just under 5’6” and many times the length of pants fall just a little short on me, especially after washing and drying. I have a feeling these will shrink up just a bit. I’ll try to back on this. Variety - Offer various shades of blue jeans. I believe right now there are 2-3 options, but I’d like to see more. Perhaps offer different colors as well. Cost - I don’t like paying over 25 for no-name jeans. If I’m going to spend more, I’ll go with my usual, name-brand that I know for a fact have great durability. I will pay the normal price if lengths are offered, waist security is added, and back pockets are slightly adjusted. Overall, I’m impressed by these stretchy pull on jeans and plan on buying more as long as they’re offering the discount/coupon.

These jeans are soft, stretchy, and comfortable! I’m not usually much of a jeans person due to the stiffness, but that’s definitely not a problem with this pair. The color is a light blue/medium blue, but it was difficult to photograph to see the color. I didn’t think I would like the seam down the front of the jeans, but it ended up making the legs look more slim. I’m only 5’1” and these jeans weren’t ridiculously long on me and won’t need to be hemmed to wear, which was a nice change for me. The high waist kinda snatched everything in, but was a little bit snug. Still wearable because the waistband area is also soft and slightly stretchy, but it would ideally be a little bit bigger. I’m a consistent size Large and these were mostly a perfect fit. My only complaint is that the full price for the jeans is pretty pricey, given how many options are out there. Mine were at a sale price that was acceptable, but I still feel like they were priced a little high, and I definitely wouldn’t buy these at full price.

I really like these jeans a lot. The denim is very thick but has a lot of stretch to it, so they're comfortable to wear all day. The legs are very wide but not distractingly so. They are a bit shorter than I expected. I am not super tall (5'8") but I feel like these would look best styled with platforms and they are a tiny bit short for that. Overall, a good deal for a trendy look. They run true to size and stayed snug, never feeling stretched out of restrictive. They come in S-M-L-XL, which is a bit unconventional as opposed to the traditional numbered sizes, but I ordered L and they fit perfectly. I can see them pairing with a loose t-shirt, fitted tank, or going out top equally well.

I am 5'8" and 165lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear a size 12 in jeans, sometimes a size 10 and occasionally a size 14 depending on the brand. There was not size chart for these so I got them in a size L (I own clothes from size S to 2XL, so who knows about sizing anymore); typically I am between a M and a L. The L was bigger than expected, I could have ordered a size M. However, they are very stretchy and comfy and I would prefer them to be a bit bigger rather than too tight, I can always just wear a belt with them. The length is perfect for me.As I said these are incredibly stretchy, you don't have to undo them to pull them up and it feels like wearing sweatpants. They are 80% cotton, 18% polyester, and 2% spandex. They aren't super soft but are still comfortable. The material is fairly lightweight but still had a durable feel to it.I love the design of the front and back pockets. The pockets look cute and are very functional and well sized (the front pockets are huge). These have a wider leg on them which I love as well.I love jeans that look cute like jeans but feel like you are wearing comfy lounge pants, so these are a huge win for me.The denim isn't the best quality but stitching looked clean on these and they washed up really well without any shrinkage. I do think they are just priced slightly too high for what they are, I would expect to spend $40-$45 for jeans like these but I guess tariffs and all that.Recommended if you are looking for a lightweight super comfy and stretchy wide leg jean.

I love these retro hippy jeans! I had to send a few pairs back, but now I have a size that fits just right. For reference I’m 5’7” 150lbs with a thick middle and thin legs. I’m 59 y/o but tend to shop in the juniors department because of my legs being thin. I’m a woman’s size 10 so that’s what I ordered at first. These jeans run large in the middle and they’re very stretchy. I sent them back and got a size 8. They were also a bit too roomy in the middle. They fit snug, but I could stretch the waist out several inches. Pushing my luck, I sent them back and got a size 6. This girl hasn’t been a size 6 since the 70’s! They fit and felt great. The waist is not too tight to expose a muffin top and actually still has a bit of room if I stretch it out. Posting photos to show measurements. If you have thicker legs, there is still plenty of stretch in these pants as long as you like them snug. The length was perfect even after I machine washed them in cold water. I hung to dry overnight then put in the dryer for 5 minutes just to soften up. Directions say hand wash, but I raised five kids so I never had time for such nonsense :)

user_comment: I bought these for my 60’s themed birthday party. I bought 2 pairs in 2 different sizes & brands. These I liked best cuz of the red embroidery detail. I am a chubby 5'-8 size 14 and chose the XXL for size 16-18 as I suspected these would be small. Glad I did, as these fit me nicely but still somewhat tight around my legs, so note that these run SMALL and are not true to size. I would suggest ordering a size up. Because the of stretchiness of the elastic waist, these are very comfortable when sitting down with just enough stretch in the waist and hips to allow a more roomy fit. It's a medium weight material - not thin and just about as thick as some skinny jeans. The pockets are nice size, as I can fit my phone in the front pocket easily.Overall, I like these but not sure I would order another pair at the price advertised as it's quite expensive for shorts whereas I can get name brand much cheaper.

Wow, what a find! I recently discovered this Wiholl brand, and I have ordered several tops from them with great results. This particular sweater is extremely well constructed from a silky-soft knit, and the color is stunning — sort of a warm cinnamon-y terracotta. I’m typically a size 13/14 and a bit of a shorty, so I generally order an XL in tops to keep them from being too clingy around my middle. For this style I took a gamble and sized up to an XXL, hoping that with its fine weight it would drape beautifully and float just a bit away from my torso … more like a tunic. And it does! For me (5’ 3”) the hem hits at low hip, giving me the versatility to wear it with jeans OR leggings, and my lower hemisphere is nicely covered. The icing on the cake is that this sweater is easy care, and it can be laundered in the machine. I plan to turn it inside out and wash on the delicate cycle with cool water, and then place it in the dryer for 5 or 10 minutes before smoothing it into a towel to finish air drying. The garment tag states that washer and dryer are fine, this is just me being cautious because I love this sweater so much! *** Because I rely so often on the reviews of others when I purchase on Amazon, I give my honest opinion about items such as this in the hope that you’ll find it , too.

These are great if you want the look of jeans and the comfort of leggings. I wore them while decluttering my closet, crouching, squatting, climbing and crawling around, something I would normally do in yoga pants. The tummy control is a nice feature. I also like the thickness of the material. It's thick, but not stiff or too hefty. They seem like they should last quite a while. I am 5'2" and got the 28-inch inseam. The length was perfect on me. I did take away one star for the very strong bleachy chemical smell that took 4 washes to get out. I've noticed this a lot lately with clothes and shoes I have purchased on Amazon. I am not sure what the chemicals are for, but it is worrisome when it takes 4 washes to get rid of it. I will probably purchase these in black as well.

I would have given these black jeans looking pants 5 stars because the shorter inseam is clearly stated, just don't think they're really tummy control pants (the waistband is tighter than the belly). But I do like them, so would recommend them if the inseam is a fit for you and you don't want super tight tummy control features. These are very soft, comfortable, straight-legged, have plenty of pockets, stitched well, and are not thin, but not extremely thick or hot - good sturdy quality. They have stretch so are easy to move in, but also like that they're mostly natural cotton so are durable, breathable, and easy to clean. I care for our mother who is 5'5" and short waisted so I ordered them for her. She usually needs an inseam of 32-33", and these are basically floods on her at 28" - not short enough to be capris, but I did just fold them up a couple times to make them more like capris, and she likes wearing them. Just not in the winter. They are actually quite slimming, smoothing, and flattering. So just check the measurements, and don't expect super tight tummy control, but at least they won't make you feel like you're being squeezed. Otherwise, they are very comfortable, give a little support, and seem to be made well.

Now here is a truly comfortable pair of pull on jeans! They are fabricated from a cotton, polyester, and spandex blend, giving them plenty of stretch and ease of wear. The weigh of the fabric is medium. They look like denim jeans but they feel like comfy leisure wear.I wear a 10 to 12 in pants and the size Large fits me perfectly. I am 5’4 and these are ankle length on me. I do think the band at the top (see photo) provides a light tummy control. I would recommend washing these pants inside out, though this is not specified on the tag. This will minimize potential pilling. The tag does call for line drying.
reviewer_name_raw: Easy to Wear, Easy to Love

user_comment: These cotton-blend jeans are great. They're soft and stretchy and wildly comfortable. I'm 5' 3", 140lbs, and ordered a Medium with a 28" inseam. The length is perfect. If you have a bit of a booty they will look fabulous on you. I ordered the dark blue color and though the stitching is visible, it's not super obvious; the dark wash is the more obvious feature. The jeans do not have a zipper since they are "tummy control" and this only adds to the comfort in my opinion. They're also a great option for pregnant or postpartum women for this reason. Just order a size or two up if that's your intention!

I really love the fit they’re super comfortable but way too big. I’m 5’6 140 lbs and the size 4 is HUGE fits closer to a size 8. I had to tighten the waist with elastics.

I got these in dark leopard and they are so cute and on-trend. The dark leopard looks great with my new black cap-sleeve sweater, plus a lot of other tops in my closet. I'm so glad these fit! I've recently ordered a couple of pairs of jeans from different brands, and despite going by my waist measurement on the brands' size charts, they were too big. So this time, I went by the waist measurement on the brand's size chart but I ordered the size a couple of inches smaller than my actual waist -- and these jeans fit great. They have a really cute silhouette even though they don't look all that enticing in my photos. "Barrel" jeans sometimes look odd or bow-legged in seller photos, maybe it's just to show the true cut of the jeans, but once you get them on, the silhouette is really cute. These jeans give more of a wide-leg look rather than a bow-legged look. They hang really cute; they are basically a wider-leg jean that gets slightly tapered toward the ankle. It's cute, casual, flattering and on-trend silhouette. These look great when they hit a bit above the ankle and look cute with flat sandals, platform sandals or sneakers. I am short (only 5'1"), so I cuff up the bottoms a couple of times so they hit just above my ankle, and not only does the length look right, the cuffs look cute, too. The denim is medium weight. I don't find it stiff like a few others have mentioned. It gets softer with washing, too. Even though the fabric is supposedly only cotton with a little bit of linen, it has good stretch, which helps in the fit department. I'm glad it stretches, but it seems like there is probably some spandex in the blend which is not mentioned. As mentioned, I ordered a few inches smaller than my actual waist size (per the size chart), and the jeans fit great. I am really short and the jeans are long on me, as expected, which is why I have to cuff them as mentioned above. I will probably tack/sew the cuffs in place, or I might have them cut and hemmed; I haven't decided yet. For size reference, the size Medium jeans I received have a full inseam (when not cuffed) that is about 26.25 inches, and a 38-inch outseam. When laid out flat, the waist measures about 14.75 inches across (see my photo). Overall, I just love these fun jeans. The black-on-brown leopard print is cute, the silhouette is casual and flattering, the denim is a medium weight good for year-round wear, and they fit great.
reviewer_name_raw: Cute and fit well. On-trend silhouette.

It's cute, casual, flattering and on-trend silhouette. These look great when they hit a bit above the ankle and look cute with flat sandals, platform sandals or sneakers. I am short (only 5'1"), so I cuff up the bottoms a couple of times so they hit just above my ankle, and not only does the length look right, the cuffs look cute, too. The denim is medium weight. I don't find it stiff like a few others have mentioned. It gets softer with washing, too. Even though the fabric is supposedly only cotton with a little bit of linen, it has good stretch, which helps in the fit department. I'm glad it stretches, but it seems like there is probably some spandex in the blend which is not mentioned. As mentioned, I ordered a few inches smaller than my actual waist size (per the size chart), and the jeans fit great. I am really short and the jeans are long on me, as expected, which is why I have to cuff them as mentioned above. I will probably tack/sew the cuffs in place, or I might have them cut and hemmed; I haven't decided yet. For size reference, the size Medium jeans I received have a full inseam (when not cuffed) that is about 26.25 inches, and a 38-inch outseam. When laid out flat, the waist measures about 14.75 inches across (see my photo). Overall, I just love these fun jeans. The black-on-brown leopard print is cute, the silhouette is casual and flattering, the denim is a medium weight good for year-round wear, and they fit great.
reviewer_name_raw: Cute and fit well. On-trend silhouette.

I recently ordered the Sidefeel High Waisted Jean Skort in the Bluing color, size 8, and overall, I really like it! The material feels soft yet durable — it’s got a nice stretch to it that makes it comfortable to move around in. The fabric feels high quality, not stiff like some denim can be, and it has that nice bit of flexibility that makes it easy to sit, walk, or bend without feeling restricted. I love the asymmetrical wrap design, which gives it a modern and flattering look. The high-waisted fit also helps accentuate the waistline, which I appreciate — especially postpartum, when I’m still getting back to my pre-pregnancy shape. I’m currently three months postpartum, 147 pounds, and 5'0.5" tall. I usually wear a medium, and while size 8 fits, it’s just a bit tight around my belly area. I honestly think size 10 would have been perfect for now, but once I lose a bit more of my belly, I know this one will fit like a dream. The zipper quality is great — it glides smoothly and doesn’t get stuck, which is always a plus with denim. The color (Bluing) is beautiful, a nice medium blue that goes well with just about anything — from a casual tee to a dressier blouse. Overall, I’m very happy with this skort. It’s cute, comfortable, and stylish — I just recommend sizing up if you’re in between sizes or recently postpartum like me. I can’t wait to wear it more once it fits just right!

The quality of the jean material is nice and thick, as in your typical jean material. I think this is a great value for the price of the item. I would recommend this skort.

Comfort Colors Adult Heavyweight Short Sleeve Tee, Style G1717/G1745, Multipack Big guy here (6 foot 4, 275lbs, wide frame). Ordered these comfort colors shirts in black and white (size 2XL). They have become my new favorite go to shirt. Why? Material: 100% cotton (no synthetics) with a soft feel Fit: Great fit overall, including arm sleeves and neck. Very comfortable, with a nice little weight to the shirt (not thin). Length is awesome. I’m able to put my hands up in the air without my tummy showing. Versatile: Can pair these shirts with jeans as is for a great look or spruce it up a bit more by adding in a jacket on top. I wear them to work, out with friends, and for bowling due to how comfortable it is. Durability: So far the shirts have been held up (2 months in) after multiple washes (wash on cold, dry delicate). I wash the white shirts with detergent and bleach to keep them bright white. Value: Good price point for the shirts vs other brands on/off Amazon Other shirts I’ve liked: I used to wear the True Classic shirts (own 20+), but the quality and sizing has been all over the place with the last few orders. I still have those, but prefer these comfort color shirts due to the comfortable fit, quality, length, and overall value of the shirt. Def a top choice shirt for anyone, especially for those in the big boy club.

Comfort Colors Adult Heavyweight Short Sleeve Tee, Style G1717/G1745, Multipack I bought 10 different shirts in various colors in hopes to have some blanks to replace my current work shirts. After doing some research, I thought these would be the best option for the money. Unfortunately I'm not overly pleased. First, the collars are tight around the neck. It's not uncomfortably tight, but you feel it around your neck, unlike other shirts. If you have sensory sensitivities, I would stay away. Next, I wanted to see if they would shrink during laundry, so I decided to wash one before opening the rest. For whatever reason, these shirts take forever to dry. They also get super wrinkled if you don't hang them immediately. I was surprised to discover this, since I hadn't seen it mentioned elsewhere. Overall they fit pretty good, but they're also on the thicker side. Some might like that, but overall these small factors led me to decide to return the remaining shirts I haven't opened. Oh, and size-wise, I'm 5' 10, 200 lbs. I often find myself between L and XL. I went with the Large and it fits proper in the torso, but if you have a bit of a chubby chest it'll show the pecs, if you know what I mean. Attached photo is literally straight from the dryer after being the only shirt in there for an hour. Incredibly wrinkled.
Seriously the perfect top for wearing tucked in to jeans. I am 5’1 128 lbs curvy build and small fits perfectly. Color is exactly as pictured.

Freaking love these tops and I have officially become a fan of ANRABESS. I got this scoop neck in four colors. Black, Beige, Dark Brown, and Brick Red. The brick red looks more muted in person and it is a gorgeous warm shade. I am wearing a size Medium and I sized down for a more snug fit. For reference, I am 5’7, 170 pounds, athletic build, and usually wear a size Large. The material is soft, stretchy, and really flattering. Great on its own and perfect for layering. I originally bought the crewneck version of this top and liked it, but the scoop neck is definitely more flattering on me. Just such an easy, versatile staple and absolutely worth grabbing in multiple colors.
reviewer_name_raw: Soft, flattering, and perfect for layering

I’m 5”8 and currently about 170 pounds. I purchased these jeans in a large, the length and fit is perfect. I bought these jeans when I was 14 weeks pregnant. I am now 21 weeks pregnant and have not needed the extender that came with the purchase, but I will probably need it in the upcoming weeks.
Fit well and are comfortable. Good length for shorter people (I’m 5ft2in). If in between sizes, go with the smaller size!

ZMTUO Skinny Jeans for Women High Waisted Stretch Vintage Denim Pants It wasn't until I took pictures that I realized just how SLENDER these pants make my legs look. They make me look like I've lost about 20 pounds and the heavens above blessed me by redistributing them straight to my tooshie. I'm 5'5 1/2, typically wear 14/16 in women's pants and ordered the XL based off the size chart. These are true to size, color- and COMFY. They don't just look good, they FEEL good. I haven't, but if I NEEDED TO- I absolutely have a full range of motion to round house kick my enemies like Jackie Chan. Zipper is solid- doesn't snag or pop open after the bloating hits when you're done with a nice spaghetti dinner. The pockets are REAL pockets. Not that "to the first knuckle" fraudulent "pockets." The back ones are also real and they add a little something to look at on your bum. The material on these are the four way stretch sort of jeans, sort of jeggings- but not so at risk of holes and ripping. These shape your body nice so if you've got a little tum or cellulite- these are the girls girl of pants- they got you covered. I have several pairs of pants from this brand and their sizing across the board is consistent and true. They provide quality at a lower price- these are the pants you get at the mall, without the mall prices. I don't just recommend these pants, I also recommend the brand. They're solid, sexy, inexpensive, and worth it.

I am not taking a star off because of quality but because the seller says this is a two-day delivery. I ordered these on August 4 and they arrived today, August 23. That's a lot longer than two-day expectations. I am tired of vendors doing this. I want the jeans, be honest and tell me it's going to take nearly three weeks. So a star comes off for not being up front. These are super comfortable and I think very flattering for my figure. I ordered a medium - I'm 5'5" and 125 pounds. For all other clothes I order small, but for jeans, I didn't want to risk getting the wrong size. It was a good choice. They're a tad loose, so I don't look like the model with the jeans looking like they were spray painted on, but I'm too old to go around looking like an overstuffed sausage. I'm all about comfort - with some pizzazz and style thrown in for good measure. Style - okay - I wasn't prepared for how LOUD the double and triple white stitching is on these. My 22 year old daughter proclaimed, "The 2000s want their jeans back" that's okay, I like them, and she was an accident anyway. But it will take the right person to wear these. They are loud. I happen to love the stitching on the pockets - I went for the angel wings and cross or sword, or whatever that's supposed to be, and I love it. The material is thick enough to give you the high-quality jeans vibe. I haven't washed them yet, but I'm guessing they'll do just fine. They have enough stretch to go on comfortably. Now, is this a $50 pair of jeans? I can't answer that for you. For me, I can't remember the last time I spent that kind of money for a pair of jeans, but I think that's the going price for something that's not a mega-high name brand, so yeah, these are good enough to have that price tag IF you actually get them in two days.

For me, I can't remember the last time I spent that kind of money for a pair of jeans, but I think that's the going price for something that's not a mega-high name brand, so yeah, these are good enough to have that price tag IF you actually get them in two days.

I am not taking a star off because of quality but because the seller says this is a two-day delivery. I ordered these on August 4 and they arrived today, August 23. That's a lot longer than two-day expectations. I am tired of vendors doing this. I want the jeans, be honest and tell me it's going to take nearly three weeks. So a star comes off for not being up front. These are super comfortable and I think very flattering for my figure. I ordered a medium - I'm 5'5" and 125 pounds. For all other clothes I order small, but for jeans, I didn't want to risk getting the wrong size. It was a good choice. They're a tad loose, so I don't look like the model with the jeans looking like they were spray painted on, but I'm too old to go around looking like an overstuffed sausage. I'm all about comfort - with some pizzazz and style thrown in for good measure. Style - okay - I wasn't prepared for how LOUD the double and triple white stitching is on these. My 22 year old daughter proclaimed, "The 2000s want their jeans back" that's okay, I like them, and she was an accident anyway. But it will take the right person to wear these. They are loud. I happen to love the stitching on the pockets - I went for the angel wings and cross or sword, or whatever that's supposed to be, and I love it. The material is thick enough to give you the high-quality jeans vibe. I haven't washed them yet, but I'm guessing they'll do just fine. They have enough stretch to go on comfortably. Now, is this a $50 pair of jeans? I can't answer that for you. For me, I can't remember the last time I spent that kind of money for a pair of jeans, but I think that's the going price for something that's not a mega-high name brand, so yeah, these are good enough to have that price tag IF you actually get them in two days.

The denim is definitely sturdy, with a little stretch (not as much as I'd like), and I actually love the white stitching, but these definitely run small, and I would not say they are a true bootcut. More like the suggestion of a bootcut. I did size up when ordering, but I do not like tight jeans and should have gone up two sizes instead of one. I tried these on both before and after washing, as I wanted to see if they'd shrink (they did not) and if fabric softener would loosen them up a bit (it did not). Considering the tighter fit, I did my best to see any evidence of "butt lift" - that can't happen without a tight fit - but didn't see that, either. At my age, maybe there's just not enough left to work with!!! They're nice enough jeans if you get the right size, but there's a lot of denim out there, and I feel you could get a better fit, true bootcut, and more stretch and softness elsewhere for this price ($49.99 when I reviewed). I'm 5'5", 130lbs., and usually wear a medium - I ordered these in large and although they are perfectly wearable, I didn't feel truly comfortable in these jeans.

The denim was soft and just the right weight. The price is fair for such a nice pair of jeans.

I was so surprised how flattering this sweater is on my body type. The sweater does run large, and I’m glad I sized down from an XL to a large, which fits perfectly and really accentuates the positive. I’m short, 5’3 with a short torso and large bust. The hem stops at my hips. I think for someone taller with a more narrow figure it may not fit well. I like the shade of navy is deep without being too dark. The knit is super soft, with a nice heft. Viscose is always hit or miss, but the quality and comfort is great.

I really like this sweater. It has nice details on the top half and the fabric is so soft! Its perfect for Florida winters that aren't too cold but this feels cozy. It hasn't shrank in the wash, has no pilling, and fits true to size-- 5'3" 145 lb size M. Great price, good quality.

Gloria Vanderbilt Womens Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean Petite I am 4' 10" and approx. 190 lbs. I own this pair & 3 other washes in a size 16 Short Plus. I'm not sure I've ever had a pair of pants that fits me well and doesn't need to be hemmed, even when I'm ordering "short." There is slight gaping at the waist, but far less than on others brands with my body type. Makes them good, comfortable sitting jeans, as opposed to some "standing" jeans - iykyk. I wear these to work in a nice casual dress setting. I wash ~ 4-5 wears. They don't stretch out very much over multiple wears and reset well after a wash. The color has been holding extremely well over multiple washes as well.

Lee Women's Regular Fit Chino Short The Goldilocks of shorts - not too long, not to short, they're just right. They fit above the hip bone and below the belly button. I got this top in khaki and I was not expecting to love it this much!! For the price, I honestly thought the fabric would feel cheap, but I was shocked when I tried it on. The material is thick, soft, and feels really high quality. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 124 lbs, usually a size S, and this fits true to size. It’s definitely more of a winter tee since the fabric runs on the heavier side — perfect for cozy days at home but also cute enough for dinners, restaurants, or a casual date. The sleeves are my favorite part — they give such a trendy, elevated look and make it feel way more styled than a basic t-shirt. Super easy to dress up or down and looks more expensive than it is. Such a good find!

(5ft 4in - 115lbs - Ordered size SMALL) Super cute top!! Fits perfectly (Although i personally wish i sized up to Medium because i like things more oversized). I was on the fence about ordering this, but I'm so glad I did. It's boxy in the perfect way. Cropped without being too short - it's very easy to avoid baring anything with high-waisted pants (or maybe even mid-rise). I usually wear a small (5'8", 155lbs) in tops and rarely a medium. I wanted to boxy, oversized look so I got a medium, but I definitely could have gone with the small and still achieved that. The material is thick and good quality. It's actually knit, not printed (you never know!). I get compliments when I wear it.

First let me say, I do love the leggings and I have bought them multiple times. They are comfortable and stretchy. They look good when they are on. If you are looking for something that is comfortable, cheap, and easy to throw on, this is it. But be aware they do NOT last long and are not “fleeced lined”. There is NO WAY these should be considered “fleece lined”. When they are stretched, you can see through the material, and I have bought them enough to know that it won’t take long for the seam to pull apart in the crotch area. I just bought these ones and have worn them for a few hours. I am 5’2 and wearing the L-XL. Currently I weigh 180lbs. You can literally see the wall through the material. There is nothing in there that I would consider fleece lining. And if you look at the seam, you can see already see where it will eventually rip away. So in short, a great bottom for cheap, that won’t last very long, but is comfortable. It is not fleece lined as advertised though.

I can not state in words HOW MUCH I LOVE these leggings! I'm about 20 pounds overweight right now and these are high waisted which holds in my fat so well! I've included photos of both the blue and black leggings to show the leggings holding in my belly. lol. They're stretchy so they're also SUPER, super comfortable. I'm 5' 1" 138 lbs. I ordered the Small/Medium, Blue & Black Pack. These fit perfectly. One of the problems I frequently run into being so short is that leggings don't fit well in the knee area. They're usually loose and saggy. Sometimes they're also loose and saggy at the ankle . But these leggings are perfect! You can see in the photos they're tight and fit awesome around my knees, ankles, and everywhere else. And as an added plus they make my booty look very round and perky. In reality, my booty is sort of droopy but for some reason these perk it up. They do have a sort of fleece lining, but for some reason they're not hot. I did not feel overly warm wearing them. Also PLEASE NOTE - they are NOT real jeans! The buttons, belt loops, pockets etc are simply painted on. These do NOT have buttons, belt loops, or pockets and they are NOT jean material!! They’re cloth. Although from far off they do a great job of looking like real jeans. But honestly I love them more than any of my jeans. Can't say enough great things about them.

You can see in the photos they're tight and fit awesome around my knees, ankles, and everywhere else. And as an added plus they make my booty look very round and perky. In reality, my booty is sort of droopy but for some reason these perk it up. They do have a sort of fleece lining, but for some reason they're not hot. I did not feel overly warm wearing them. Also PLEASE NOTE - they are NOT real jeans! The buttons, belt loops, pockets etc are simply painted on. These do NOT have buttons, belt loops, or pockets and they are NOT jean material!! They’re cloth. Although from far off they do a great job of looking like real jeans. But honestly I love them more than any of my jeans. Can't say enough great things about them.

Ok let me start of by lmao lolol these are soooooo cheesy 😂😂😂 so easy to tell they're fake but I like them because if you wear a tunic it hides the cheesy pockets which really is the only downfall besides the camel toe it gives you but like the pockets you can hide it with a tunic and also by wearing a panty liner I mean unless you have a moose knuckle lol but the pros of these are they are very comfortable and fleece lined so they're very warm. Idk what the other reviews are talking about because yea they're not thin but like not extremely thick where it's too hot. I can wear these in the winter here in vermont and be comfortably warm. So clearly someone should not wear these in warm weather especially in the summer or like if you live in florida. Now I'm 5'4" so these are a little long on me but what I love about these is you can fold the buttons up and it doesnt show the fleece because theres a inch of Jean print before the fleece begins making it perfect to fold. I also like how it fits on my tummy because it doesnt cut in and make my tummy look like 2 parts lol but yea I also like how they're high waisted above my belly button (again I am short) so it gives me a slight hold you in feeling. I'm a mom of three so I have extra fatty skin that unfortunately hangs :/ but yea these are also smooth like lularoe BUT with that comes with these being shiny like silk so that it's pretty obvious that they're not Jeans. Now I also like how even though they are high waisted the pockets still sit correctly on my behind. My boyfriend says they look good lol. They dont fall down but they do bunch up a little in the legs from being obviously leggings. Jean's do that to me anyway though. Oh and I did forget to mention I am a big girl I am 210 lbs carry most of the weight on top so my waist and hips are 45" so it is really surprising they fit me without cutting into my waist. I dont think this would mean itd be too loose on a small girl because like leggings are form fitting no matter what but definitely stick to your size because these are true to size. I usually wear a one size plus and I bought a 2xl. But yes sorry for my review being all over the place but for $8 I guess this is a fair quality pair of leggings for that price. I guess I would recommend these IF you wear a tunic to hide the pockets. I definitely would not pay over $8. Well that wraps this review up, thank you for reading! If you found this review please click the little button because I love knowing that I helped someone :)

For reference, I'm 5'4", 194 pounds. Hips 48", waist 45". I ordered the 2XL in blue. (It probably doesn't mean much because I rarely buy anything with a fitted waist, but in American plus size brands, I usually wear between a 16 and a 20.) These leggings are very comfortable. The material is stretchy and super lightweight but has just enough of a fleece coating inside to feel verrrrry soft on your skin. The waist is quite high, and there's a little extra length at the ankle. The color is maybe a bit too bright to be hyper realistic, but otherwise these leggings look very much like jeans... The pockets on the butt were so real looking that I had to make sure they were only printed on. The fit: snug like leggings should be, but a little bigger than I expected. As you see in the pics, they're not in any danger of falling down, but they're not even half as tight at the waist and hips as most of my leggings are. If you're close to my size but with a bigger butt, you're probably still okay. The one thing I didn't like: they arrived with a very strong "dry cleaner" smell. I'm sure it's nothing a good wash won't fix, but it was very noticeable as soon as I opened the package. Beware if you have a really sensitive nose. Overall: excellent. Can't beat it for the price. I'll be wearing these a lot.

I love these leggings. They are super soft and fleece lined (which is so comfortable). I am 5'2" and 117 lbs (29-30 inch waist) so I bought two pairs in large/xl based on my waist (shown in the photo attached) and I own a pair in small (which is what I originally purchased). Honestly, you would not see a difference in a photo of me wearing either the small or the large size. The large is a little longer in the legs and comes up above my bellybutton, but other than that I don't feel much of a difference. It definitely feels more comfortable pulling it up so it aligns with the top of my legs to look like jeans rather than tights (and I'm short!). I will likely purchase it again in the large size since there's not much of a difference to me in tightness. Although I could see this stretching to fit a heavier person, I wouldn't consider it xl sized. I usually wear small or medium in other stores, and I'd consider this more of a medium size.

I bought these in size S/M but I think I could've gone with a Large very easily. I'm normally a size 6/8 and small in women's clothes, medium in anything"trendy" (like forever 21, H&M, etc.). I'm 5'8”, measurements 34-24-38, and most pants are a little short on me, these were no exception. I was expecting to probably have to exchange these for the larger size, especially right after I took them out of the package. They look like clothes for a ten year old, but these leggings stretch an absurd amount. I would not suggest a S/M for someone who has any extra "love-lumps" going on because I feel like these will role down in that situation. They're high waisted enough to feel really comfortable and they're not the warmest leggings but it's not like I can feel a breeze when I'm wearing these (like some other leggings I have, looking at you Target). Would buy again. My biggest suggestion is that, if you are trim and slim, buy the S/M. If you are anywhere else on the spectrum, buy the Large.

My dimensions are 130 lbs, 5'3", large chest (D cup), shoulder width 20" and I ordered medium in denim blue. The material's composition is 80% Cotton, 20% Polyester and 2% Elastane. Here are some reasons why I love cotton: 1. Cotton is a natural fiber known for its softness and breathability. 2. It allows for good air circulation, making it comfortable to wear, especially in warm weather. 3. Durability - cotton denim is strong and long-lasting, developing a unique fit over time as it molds to the wearer's body. 4. It is also biodegradable, making it an eco-friendlier option compared to synthetic materials. 5. Hypoallergenic Properties - pure cotton is less likely to cause skin irritations, making it suitable for sensitive skin. I am mentioning these reasons because in terms of this jacket the cotton makes this jacket perfect for layering OR even wearing alone as a top. The medium is slightly oversized on me, but still offers an attractive silhouette. The Elastane provides the jacket with the stretch that makes it comfortable to perform walking, sitting or reaching. There is enough room for my shoulders to perform daily activities--washing dishes, changing the laundry or blow drying my hair. The size I have works best with thinner tops when layering--medium thick or bulky sweaters will not work well. Given that this jacket is comprised mostly of cotton do not wash in hot water or dry on high heat--unless you want it to shrink. I washed it following the seller's instructions and had pleasant results--please see pictures. I did throw it in the dryer for 15 minutes on low/cool heat. The polyester works well in these conditions to de-wrinkle the garment. The jean material is medium thickness and soft, but I would not choose this jacket for warmth in 60 degree weather. I find the jacket perfect for 70 degree weather and above. The versatility of this jacket makes it a good investment piece, and the durable material offers longevity. The jacket looks cute with or without a belt, over an athleisure basic top, under a leather jacket or alone as a top. The color is as shown in my pictures, which appears slightly different than the denim blue advertisement. Generally, an indigo dying process is used to get "traditional blue" denim colors however I find that variations occur. Denim colors can change over time due to wear and washing which can create unique patterns and shades, making a garment distinct. The jackets hardware is attached securely and the buttons are easy to manipulate. The pockets are a generous size and I can easily store my phone and sunglasses in one pocket. I am thrilled with the style, quality and price point of this jacket.

The jacket looks cute with or without a belt, over an athleisure basic top, under a leather jacket or alone as a top. The color is as shown in my pictures, which appears slightly different than the denim blue advertisement. Generally, an indigo dying process is used to get "traditional blue" denim colors however I find that variations occur. Denim colors can change over time due to wear and washing which can create unique patterns and shades, making a garment distinct. The jackets hardware is attached securely and the buttons are easy to manipulate. The pockets are a generous size and I can easily store my phone and sunglasses in one pocket. I am thrilled with the style, quality and price point of this jacket.

My dimensions are 130 lbs, 5'3", large chest (D cup), shoulder width 20" and I ordered medium in denim blue. The material's composition is 80% Cotton, 20% Polyester and 2% Elastane. Here are some reasons why I love cotton: 1. Cotton is a natural fiber known for its softness and breathability. 2. It allows for good air circulation, making it comfortable to wear, especially in warm weather. 3. Durability - cotton denim is strong and long-lasting, developing a unique fit over time as it molds to the wearer's body. 4. It is also biodegradable, making it an eco-friendlier option compared to synthetic materials. 5. Hypoallergenic Properties - pure cotton is less likely to cause skin irritations, making it suitable for sensitive skin. I am mentioning these reasons because in terms of this jacket the cotton makes this jacket perfect for layering OR even wearing alone as a top. The medium is slightly oversized on me, but still offers an attractive silhouette. The Elastane provides the jacket with the stretch that makes it comfortable to perform walking, sitting or reaching. There is enough room for my shoulders to perform daily activities--washing dishes, changing the laundry or blow drying my hair. The size I have works best with thinner tops when layering--medium thick or bulky sweaters will not work well. Given that this jacket is comprised mostly of cotton do not wash in hot water or dry on high heat--unless you want it to shrink. I washed it following the seller's instructions and had pleasant results--please see pictures. I did throw it in the dryer for 15 minutes on low/cool heat. UPDATE: After writing the following review, I bought a pr in 1 size smaller & they fit fantastic. Still very loose in the waist. I could easily size down to large, but then I’d lose the oversized style. Def keeping these.ORIGINAL REVIEW: I really love these jeans even though the waist is large. They’re so darn cute & comfy. I’m 5’5,” 160lbs, waist is approx 35” and my hips are 42.” I’m a 12/14 or L/XL, but usually go up one size because I love a baggy fit. Yoeasy’s sz guide suggested sz Lg. However based on quite a few of the reviews, I decided to go to an XXL and these def run big on me, mainly the waist.I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for this review, but feel the current price of $30 is very fair. I own a lot of jeans from Free People & these Yoeasy’s are great dupes. I especially love how lightweight they are.I like these so much I’ve purchased the XL’s. I still want an oversized fit so I’m playing it safe. I should add I’m wearing my platform Crocs in my pics which add 2.5” to my height.
