user_comment: First, an admission: I am much bigger than I need to be because of a pre-denture diet. I am 5'5" and 230lbs (up from 150 18months ago!)I was already "curvy" but have moved into the the "much-too-swurvy" lane. These pants are in the 2X size. I wanted something comfortable & flattering that I can wear on a daily basis. Surprisingly, these fit the bill. I liked this first pair so much that I ordered 2 others. The last pair must have had to crawl from China because they took forever to get here. PROS: I am STUNNED by these leggings. When I opened the package, I laughed so hard and long that I think I lost about a pound. Which I need to do more of because I have put on weight. These are so narrow-looking that I didn't think there was any way I could get them past my knee. You can see from the photo of these next to a pair of my yoga pants that things were looking quite impossible. But... WOW. Not only do these fit but they are a lot more flattering than I expected. The material stretches like some magical polymer from another world but doesn't make my fat thighs look misshapen. They look awesome with both ankle-high boots and boots that come up to mid calf. If I wear a top covering my belly, the pants give me a slimmer illusion. Also, these flatten my belly a lot more than expected & I have a LOT of belly right now. If I keep them pulled up high enough, there is no muffin top or unflattering lines under a top. That's amazing. (I won't be gracing any BBW magazine covers, but I feel more presentable.) When I am sitting, there are no embarrassing rolls showing. Wild! There are no real negatives with these because of the price. I can tell that the stitching in the crotch area might be the first to give way. If they only last 3 months each, I am fine with that. For now, I will be living in these leggings. When I lose the weight, I will have to order more of them. Wait - there is one potential negative: I look so good in these that I might be less inclined to lose the extra weight! J/K

When I opened the package, I laughed so hard and long that I think I lost about a pound. Which I need to do more of because I have put on weight. These are so narrow-looking that I didn't think there was any way I could get them past my knee. You can see from the photo of these next to a pair of my yoga pants that things were looking quite impossible. But... WOW. Not only do these fit but they are a lot more flattering than I expected. The material stretches like some magical polymer from another world but doesn't make my fat thighs look misshapen. They look awesome with both ankle-high boots and boots that come up to mid calf. If I wear a top covering my belly, the pants give me a slimmer illusion. Also, these flatten my belly a lot more than expected & I have a LOT of belly right now. If I keep them pulled up high enough, there is no muffin top or unflattering lines under a top. That's amazing. (I won't be gracing any BBW magazine covers, but I feel more presentable.) When I am sitting, there are no embarrassing rolls showing. Wild! There are no real negatives with these because of the price. I can tell that the stitching in the crotch area might be the first to give way. If they only last 3 months each, I am fine with that. For now, I will be living in these leggings. When I lose the weight, I will have to order more of them. Wait - there is one potential negative: I look so good in these that I might be less inclined to lose the extra weight! J/K

All of their jeans run large (i’ve tried three different styles). I was a sz 6/8 before pregnancy. I’m 17 week/154 lbs and have a decent sized bump and I was swimming in the mediums. I returned them for a small, and I still have a ton of room to grow into. In fact, I wondered for a moment if I should try an extra small, but that seems crazy given I’m only going to get bigger. The pants are extremely stretchy. Even with the button fly and the drawstring, it would be very easy for someone to pants me. They are definitely not as cute as they are on the model — there’s a lot of a loose fabric between the back of the waistband and the top of your butt (even after I sized down). They want to slide down my body, and I have to hike them up a lot. But they’re comfortable and they look a lot better than most other maternity pants. And unlike some of the other maternity pants that I’ve bought, I believe these ones will last me through my third trimester. My other ones are already feeling a little snug in the belly band.

And make sure it comes in Amazon packaging. Genuinely disappointed

This denim shirt is made of soft fabric consisting of 72% cotton, 21% polyester, and 6% viscose. It's lightweight and leans toward being thin. Makes it nice for warmer weather as it breaths well. I did not realize that it was a thinner fabric and was expecting a thicker denim, but I'm okay with it. There isn't any stretch in the fabric. I love style with the seams on the front and back that seem to add some extra shape to it. The only part that I'm not so crazy about is that the hem is a straight cut, so it gives it more of a boxy look on my body shape. It has two front pockets that have button down flaps. The buttons are a bit stiff/tight to use, but were manageable with my arthritis in my fingers. The sleeves have 2 buttons each, vertically from each other, not horizontally. The collar is not stiff. The Indigo Classic Blue color is really nice, and matches what I saw on my laptop when I ordered the shirt. My photos (with me wearing it) are showing it as a brighter and deeper blue, than it really is. I did notice that the seam that runs horizontally across the shoulders on the back has a bit of fraying on the inside of the shirt. However, I saw that it is double stitched, so that should stop it from becoming a problem, I think. I ordered this shirt in a size XXL, with my regular shirt size being XL. From past orders of the brand, I know to size up a size to get the type of fit I prefer. I like a loose, oversized fit for my personal comfort. It's only for shirts and other upper body wear with the brand that I need to size up. All their jeans and shorts are true to size for me. This shirt fits me nicely and there wasn't any gaping between the buttons near my chest. The sleeves are bit long, but I'm a bit short in height and that happens often for me. I had wanted to be able to tuck it up and tie it with the sleeves rolled up for warmer weather, but even with the XXL size there is not enough of shirt tails for me to do it. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 42 B or C (depends on the bra), 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs. This is a nice denim shirt for warm weather and I will recommend it. There are other shades of blue, Almond Brown, and Aurora Pink colors available as well. For the thinness of the fabric, I feel that the current price (2-10-2026) of $31.99 is sort of pricey. Even the reduced price the coupon offers ($27.19) is still a bit much to be a fair value, in my opinion. The price for this shirt is the reason for my 4 star rating.

I own several pieces from luvamia at this point, and they’ve consistently proven to be a solid, reliable brand. This long-sleeve, lightweight, denim button-down is another strong example of that consistency. The fabric is soft for denim and comfortable enough for all-day wear. The seam work and stitching are clean and well finished. I ordered a size medium in Indigo Dark Blue. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 154 lbs. The color is accurate to the listing, a rich, classic dark indigo that works year-round. The sizing is exactly true to size. And I mean exactly. It fits well, but it’s a more tailored fit than I expected with very little give or stretch. If you’re planning to layer it over tees or tanks, I would recommend sizing up for a bit more room. I personally wish I had gone up one size to give myself more layering flexibility. The Western style is subtle without leaning into costume territory. The two flap chest pockets are fully functional. The cuff buttons are a little tight, but not impossible. The sleeve length is spot on for my height, and they look great worn down or rolled up for a more casual feel. Styling-wise, this is versatile. It looks just as good with jeans for a classic denim-on-denim look as it does with a short or long skirt. It would work for casual days, concerts, smart-casual office settings, or layered open as a lightweight overshirt. I washed and dried it normally with no special settings and it did not fade or shrink in the wash. At the time of my review, the price is $31.99. Here’s where I hesitate. The quality is very good for fast fashion but this isn’t a premium heritage denim brand. The construction is solid, but the pricing is pushing into territory where you start comparing it to higher-end options. For low-to-mid $20s, this would feel like a no-brainer. At just under $32, it’s priced overconfidently.

CAKE THE LABEL Women's Mid Rise Regular Straight Jeans Classic Pants I’ve lost weight in the last year and I guess it all came off my butt 🤦‍♀️😩 because the butt of all my jeans ends up sagging like a loaded diaper after only wearing them an hour or so. I’ve tried SO many pairs off of Amazon recently, most of them Levi’s and all of them returned with a hard pass. I finally ordered these and wish I had sooner because these are PERFECTION in denim form. I’m apple shaped, with narrow hips and big boobs. I’m a Torrid size 2 on top, and wear a 33 in most jeans (Abercrombie/Agolde) depending on how much stretch they have. I ordered a 33 in these and it’s like they were tailor made for me. The waist is perfect and flattering and not high but medium, hitting me right at my belly button and the top of my hip bones. (I’m super short waisted) These manage to flatten my stomach AND flatter my butt, which is nearly impossible to do. Usually if jeans fit me in the waist they’re too big in the hips/butt and visa versa. The material is soft and stretchy without having so much stretch that you’re basically just wearing unflattering denim leggings… you know the ones. 😒 I’m 5’6 and they’re slightly long on me, but since the leg opening isn’t super wide it’s not an issue. They are a TRUE straight leg, which is why I’ve returned so many pairs lately… they’ll be described as straight but be huge and wide leg on me OR they’ll be described as straight and be more like skinny jeans. I know this is par for the course when buying jeans online, though. Thankfully my search is over! I’d give these 20 stars if I could, highly HIGHLY recommend! 🙌🏻🙌🏻🙌🏻

Im 5’2 and weigh about 127 lbs with a bit of a mom pouch lol Okay with that being said, onto my review! When I first took them out of the bag I thought they weren’t going to fit cause the waist looked a bit small but I saw they were stretchy. When I put them on the fell I love with how soft, warm and stretchy they were! The pants weren’t too tight or too lose. They were just perfect. Obviously I knew that they were going to touch the floor cause I’m short which wasn’t a big deal when you fold them just a tiny bit. I highly recommend them specially after getting asked where I got them and that they are cute so yes buy them lol! I will do an edit this weekend on how warm they kept me on a 10 degree day in MN!

Ok so I’m top heavy after three c sections and a hysterectomy. I’m 5’3 and 130 ish pounds . I love these shorts but will say if I was not c section mom I could go small instead of medium. They are perfect on my waste but loose on and legs and booty I have neither lol so if you are built like me with a small waste go small instead of medium . I’m about to buy a couple more .. I’m 55 btw lol

I wasn’t sure how the quality or fit would be, as I feel like Amazon can be hit or miss. However, these were totally a winner for me. I got beige and it’s really a pretty color. The material seems really thin at first... like “can you see what color underwear I’m wearing” thin. However, they're not see through and don’t feel too fragile. I’m 5’0” and these were wayyyy too long for me. I cut probably 5-7 inches of length off the bottom and they are still just above the ground. They don’t have much stretch at all, but are still very comfortable. Even with those things considered, I love the fit and the style!!! I get so many compliment. They’re arguably my favorite pants. I just hope they hold up overtime.

Hybrid & Company Maternity Women's Super Comfy Ultra Stretch Skinny Jeans First things first, I normally wear 12-14 jean size and I'm 5′ 2″ tall and weigh 190lbs but I have grown in belly size so hope this helps others choose your size. (I bought the Light Wash3 in size 1X) I am now 5 months pregnant so my normal day-to-day jeans won't budge. These jeans are the first jeans that have fit me without constricting my body, belly or legs got some great stretch to them and also don't drag past my feet, they're perfect length. Not to mention they're not baggie on me making them a lot more flattering than most maternity pants I've worn. I am extremely shocked with how perfect these jeans fitted me and how amazing i felt in them, for $20 dollars these are the best jeans I've owned in my opinion, even though some say they're cheap looking or feel cheap, not to me NOT FOR 20 dollars! High end maternity jeans are way over 50-100 dollars nowadays so these are great for my budget. The belly band is long enough it reaches right below my bra and is very stretchy but not loose, I still have 4 months to go so i hope they'll fit then. I will make sure to buy different colors and update this review accordingly. Now for the negatives (not a huge deal breaker but important to note) These jeans have no front pockets, they're fake pockets which was a disappointment but for some dumb reason women pants normally don't have pockets so im used to it (I wish they would add pockets though, please do!) Another negative is the color is a bit different from the model, mine were a bit lighter in color but honestly not a huge deal for me. Third negative for me is that I expected them to have a hem at the end like in the picture of the model but also something that isn't a huge deal. Lastly, they did have a strong chemical smell like some reviews have mentioned, but the smell reminds me of mothballs so I assume they use some kind of moth control to keep their jeans protected, after I washed them the scent faded away. All in all, these negatives are not a deal breaker whatsoever, so I will definitely get more!

I’m 5’2” and about 165–168 lbs for reference. The denim is very heavy and structured with minimal stretch, but it feels high quality. I ordered a L and 2XL to compare. The L was too small in the thighs (couldn’t get them past my butt), and the 2XL had way too much extra room. The XL ended up being the perfect fit. They were a little snug across the front of the seat when sitting at first, but I got used to it. Once I was settled into them, they were pretty comfortable. Overall they worked perfectly for my event and I would definitely buy them again.

This denim babydoll top surprised me in the best way. For reference, I ordered a size Medium and wear a DDD cup, and it was still very flattering. The structured denim gives it shape without pulling awkwardly across the chest, which can be hard to find with this style. The fabric is slightly stiff, but in a good way, it helps the puff sleeves and peplum shape hold their structure so the top looks polished instead of flimsy. It doesn’t collapse or wrinkle easily, and the silhouette feels intentional and styled. The overall aesthetic is very cute and outdoorsy. It feels perfect for casual daytime activities like apple picking, visiting a farm, going to an outdoor market, or even just walking around an outlet mall on a nice day. It has that effortless, slightly vintage-inspired summer vibe. Overall, I was genuinely impressed with how good it looked on and how flattering the cut was. Definitely 5 stars for fit, structure, and style.

Its SO hard to find plus size baggy pants that fit thighs/booty.. GET THIS!! Im 198lbs and I got a 2xl because I wanted a really baggy fit, the waist is roomy and I can tie them if I need them tighter. Perfect for that grunge look!

It has a decent bit of give to the fabric so it's not stiff. I LOVE the elastic at the ankles, if they didn't have them I would probably have to hem the pants. The size guide is accurate in my opinion, I ordered an XXL to get the oversized look. I am about 5'2" and 175lbs. I am very happy with these pants.

EVALESS Womens Boyfriend Washed Crop Denim Jean Jacket Coat I have this jacket in black, medium blue and the lighter blue and am seriously considering the grey and white! There is nothing so versatile as this denim jacket to add to my collection. I love the distressed look because it gives the jacket a “edgy” look and feel. Once out of the package and washed the material settles in to a nice softness. They fraying is done well enough that it doesn’t unravel or deconstruct after wearing or laundering. I am 5”11 170lbs and wear an XL. I could get away with a L but I love to layer a hoodie underneath quite often. I would definitely size according to how you wear your jackets as there is a slight stretch to it. The overall fit is comfortable and gives you that long loved jean jacket feel. A great value for the money and highly recommended. Another winner from the Evaless brand…versatile, stylish, and on point. I always get compliments on my clothing and my ability to mix brand names with my Amazon FAVORITES and this jacket is ✨.

These high-waisted, wide-leg cropped jeans are amazing! At 5'3", they hit right at my ankle, which I love. The stretchy denim makes them super comfortable, and I wear them year-round. Lately, I’ve been pairing them with black kitten-heel boots for a chic look, and they also look great with my navy sweater that has matching buttons. Stylish, versatile, and flattering, highly recommend!

The small was the best fit . My waist measurement currently are 33inches 5’4 and weight 160 two c-sections so I have a lower belly but the medium was too loose and I wanted a tighter fit over all. The length was long with sneakers so I tailored them . Super lightweight and comfortable I was on the floor with my baby and pants were great . Only odd thing was the string was really long.

My dimensions are 5'3", 125 lbs, shoulder width 15", bust 36" to 38", waist 28" to 29" and I ordered medium in Almond Brown. The fit is true to size and the color is as shown in the advertisement. (Color in photo's is not an accurate depiction do to lighting.) The fit is a relaxed fit with a mild stretch; if a looser fit is preferred I recommend ordering up one size. The material is a soft, medium weight, denim that is comprised mostly of cotton with a touch of Viscose. Usually, I would not be a fan of polyester (21%) in denim with this weight, however I find it helps with wrinkle prevention and the durability of the material. I have washed this top several times and if removed quickly from the dryer I am not burdened with too many wrinkles. I do have to use a low heat iron on the pocket flaps and collar area for a more crisp appearance. The double button cuffs, traditional collared V-neck and finished seam detail offer the wearer versatility; Hence, I have worn this top to work in a "business casual," manner and with shorts to tool around town. "Luvamia" continues to impress me with the quality of their products. This is the fourth product I have received from them and once again they deliver on style, comfort, fit, color and quality. If you prefer to invest in pieces that can provide more than one use this is an excellent investment.

I got these delivered last night and rushed to wear them today because they are so cute and fit awesome!!! I have gotten probably 10 or more compliments already! Love these!! They’ve got a bit of stretch and are so comfy. I’m 5’10 and I love the length. Great quality and very flattering. They do have an actual button which is different than other maternity pants.

Size stats: pre pregnancy- 5’2”, 216lbs, size 16/18, XL, 1X. Current size-26 weeks pregnant. 5’2”, 212lbs, making my 18’s work but needed maternity jeans for sureee. These are great! I sized down because my pants size is determined by my stomach and less so my hips/legs. (I was a 18/XL pre-pregnancy and bought the large.) The over the stomach band isn’t too tight and I think I’ll be able to wear them the whole pregnancy, the only issue I see happening is that the band of the “jeans” that buttons is a big tight already, but I’ll just have to have it under the belly and not trying to stretch up as much as I can. The thighs and calves are snug without feeling skin tight. No bagginess. I’m 5’2”, and the length is perfect. I could roll them up once for a more tailored fit but I’m not going to get my hem wet by accident either way. I like the wash and the subtle wear on them. Butt is very flattering which is hard to find in maternity jeans. Real front pockets that would fit more than half a hand! Lol Worth the money.

The over the stomach band isn’t too tight and I think I’ll be able to wear them the whole pregnancy, the only issue I see happening is that the band of the “jeans” that buttons is a big tight already, but I’ll just have to have it under the belly and not trying to stretch up as much as I can. The thighs and calves are snug without feeling skin tight. No bagginess. I’m 5’2”, and the length is perfect. I could roll them up once for a more tailored fit but I’m not going to get my hem wet by accident either way. I like the wash and the subtle wear on them. Butt is very flattering which is hard to find in maternity jeans. Real front pockets that would fit more than half a hand! Lol Worth the money.

I have been a sucker for camo for years. I'm so happy they haven't gone out of style. I found a cute way to dress them up with my jewelry . Got my inspiration from Kira and came right here on Amazon to make a purchase. I'm only 5 ft tall so they're pretty darn long That didn't diminish it for me though. I just bought some iron on quick hem and that did the trick so they're no longer dragging. The material is not very heavy or thick. The quality isn't bad, material isn't thick at all. These won't keep you warm in the winter if that's what you're looking for.

Love these, but sizing is way off. Very small, order at least one size up. I love the choice of colors, the tapered leg is nice too. They have a little bit of stretch, but not too much that they will be falling off after you wear them for a while. Not as heavy as a denim, but heavy enough that they’re not see-through. I am typically a size 9, but the 11 fit me much better. I could get the nines on, but they were tight and clingy. The ones pictured in me are size 11. I liked them so much, I bought two more pairs. Hopefully the sizing is consistent. For reference, I am 5’6 and around 140lbs, I am in my 50’s.

Lucky Brand Women's Mid Rise Sweet Straight Jean The color, length, and stretch were perfect. Just what I was looking for. However, these are described as mid rise and they fit more like a low rise. I am returning them for that reason only. I really loved them otherwise! Rock & Republic Women's Denim Rx Fever Stretch Jean Legging I’m roughly 5’1”, 125 lbs Size 6 short Pant Measurements while wearing… WAIST: 33 LENGTH: 34 from top of waistband to bottom of pant leg THIGH: 21 CALF: 14 OVERALL FIT: perfect all the way around, not too gappy or too snug (for my taste) COMFORT: very comfy! QUALITY: good quality, medium weight denim fabric with excellent stretch WASH / DRY: I washed these upon arrival in warm water, dried on medium heat, removed promptly - minimal shrinkage from what I can tell. I love the way these feel on, my only complaint is the awkward location of the back pockets. They look ok with a shirt, but even then still a little low for my taste.

Carhartt Women's The Essential Canvas Pant These are exactly what I've been looking for. Waist fits great with a "relaxed" fit in the legs that leaves room for crouching and moving around (which i do a lot of in the hvac/pipe work field) and plenty of pockets in the right spots. Great for winter as well as you have room for a layer underneath for fleece leggings or long John's. Nice durable canvas-like material that still has a bit of stretch. Honestly these are perfect for what I do at work and still somehow have a fitted silhouette even though they are considered relaxed fit. Long story short I really like them, and ordered 3 pairs. Definitely recommend, these are probably the best women's Utility work pants I've found so far and I've tried a decent amount. I'm 5'3 and currently 135ish pounds and ordered a size 4 regular

Ariat Women's R.E.A.L. Mid Rise Boot Cut Jeans These are a high cotton content, low stretch true denim jeans. I like jeans without any stretch; these have some but not too much. The mid rise sits low enough that my belt sits in that sweet spot just below my actual hip bone. They give me muffin top but all my jeans do that and it doesn’t bother me. They are long enough to make a nice pile on top of my shoes, which I like. There are darts next to the back pockets that allow them to hug the butt nicely. Roomy front pockets big enough for my hands with back pockets big enough for my Otterboxed iPhone. I am 5’5” and 127-129lbs depending on the day and I ordered size 27R in Ocean color. I debated getting size 28R but am glad I went with the smaller size so they wouldn’t be baggy.***Update: washed and worn = my new favorite pair of jeans. Washed in cold water, dried on ultra low. They fit a tiny bit snugger after washing now but not much, certainly not enough to order a size up. The fit on these jeans is unbelievable. They are perfect butt huggers. I wear my jeans for 3 days between washes and I’m on day 2 right now. They don’t loosen up as the day goes on and they don’t wind up being too baggy after wearing them for 2 days. No saggy crotch here, no hiking them up; just perfect.Oh, and something I didn’t notice before but is a wonderful feature on the jeans is the extra right and left belt loop in the back. 7 belt loops to support the length of your belt will keep your belt from ever coming over the top band on your jeans when you bend over or squat down. These jeans pair perfectly with my 10 year old Ariat Fatbaby Center Stitch belt. Highly recommended!

This pair of floral print denim skorts are well made of 54% cotton, 28% polyester, 8% lyocell, 7% viscose, and 3% elastane. The fabric has a nice amount of stretchiness in it. They are soft and comfortable to wear. The thickness leans toward being thin, but heavy enough to be durable and not see through at all. I love the floral print on them! It's pretty much why I dared to order them at my age of 60+, lol. They have an elastic waistband on the back that helps make them a super comfy fit on my waist. There are two buttons, one for the front flap and one for the shorts behind it, along with a zipper on the shorts. There aren't any front or side pockets, but they do have 2 average sized pockets on the back. I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular size for pants, shorts, and now skorts. They fit comfortably in all ways, without pinking or binding anywhere. They are a tad short, but I knew that when I saw them. It was the floral print that made me do it! For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs. These aren't very flattering for me, but I will wear them at home when summer gets here. Overall, this pair of skorts are well made and cute, so I will recommend them. The current price (2-13-2026) of $29.99 is an okay value, but at this time there is a coupon available that lowers the price to a better value. They have differing shades of blue available and differing floral prints for each shade. I hope the floral prints are going to be more popular for shorts and jeans in the near future as I really like them!

Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Bermuda High Rise Short Standard I’m 5’1” 163lbs ordered a 12. These are high waisted and hide my tummy bulge. The length on me is about 1” above my knee which I love. I bought them in white and Dark Denim. I have already worn and washed both them. The fit remained great even in hot water for whites. This review is for the XIEERDUO Tunic Sweater. I ordered it in khaki, size large. It fits true to size, but just a heads-up, it’s definitely more of a lightweight shirt than an actual sweater. The fabric is very thin, but it’s soft, comfortable, and incredibly wrinkle-resistant. It also handles the washer and dryer surprisingly well. The layered look with the buttons is really cute and flattering, but the material is a bit more see-through than I expected. Because it’s so lightweight, it also highlights my “muffin tops”. This top is a great affordable option for anyone who likes light, breathable layers and doesn’t mind a more form-revealing fit. It’s cute, just not the best choice if you’re trying to hide anything underneath it. 👉For reference, I’m 175lbs, 5’5 tall, 36D cup and typically wear a size Large in tops and 12 in jeans.

It’s cute, just not the best choice if you’re trying to hide anything underneath it. 👉For reference, I’m 175lbs, 5’5 tall, 36D cup and typically wear a size Large in tops and 12 in jeans.

The fabric is very thin, but it’s soft, comfortable, and incredibly wrinkle-resistant. It also handles the washer and dryer surprisingly well. This pair of overall shorts are well made of 75% cotton, 24% polyester and 1% elastane. The material is soft and has a comfy amount of stretch in it. The fabric thickness is between medium and thin, but not see through at all. There are 2 back pockets, 2 front hip pockets, and the bib pocket with a pouch pocket and 4 slotted pockets attached on the front of the pouch. It must be my body shape, but I could not get the bib pocket to lay flat on my chest. It kept pouching outward. Maybe if I carried my phone in it, it would have helped? The shoulder straps are easy to adjust with a sliding buckle. The side buttons on each hip are easy to use, though I did not need to unbutton them to put the overalls on or take them off, thanks to the stretch in the fabric. I ordered this pair of overall shorts in a size XL, which is my regular size for shorts, jeans, etc. They fit me quite comfortably around my waist and hips. The legs have some room to allow for airflow. I did have to adjust the shoulder straps a good bit to tighten up the sag. Must have had a taller person in mind, lol. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 42 B or C (depends on the bra), 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs with an apple shaped midsection. These arrived with a small amount of damage to one strap buckle and fabric damage on the back of one leg. The same type of markings were on the plastic branded bag they were rolled up inside of, as well. One buckle has a bent cross piece, but still functions fine. The fabric damage is an imprint of something heavy, and 2 small cuts (side by side) on the back of one leg. They are not in a compromising area, so I will wear them anyway. :) I'm sure this was just an accidental slip-up, and not a problem for the rest of the other overall shorts. This pair of overall shorts are nice, quality made and comfortable, so I will recommend them. They fit true to size and are easy to wear. The current price (2-11-2026) of $31.99 is a fair value, in my opinion. There are several shades of blue, along with cream white and washed black colors available to choose from.

These overalls are cute, flattering, stretchy, and actually comfortable (which is shocking because I usually hate jeans with a passion). But to be fair, the material isn’t real denim or even close to it. It's more like a soft, stretchy, medium thick fabric that hugs your body. For reference, I'm 5’4”, 115–117 lbs, 36-25-38, D cup and size small fit perfectly (not tight and not loose). Length hits mid-thigh, and the fit is more curve hugging than traditional boxy overalls. Now… the smell. When I opened the package, I was personally attacked. It took THREE WASHES to make them wearable, and I still had to put Downy dryer sheets in my pockets like some kind of walking air freshener. Not even exaggerating, I really did this. These are definitely style over utility. If you’re looking for rugged, tool-carrying, garden-working overalls where you can hook a hammer and stash screws like a weekend DIY, Home Depot poster child, these aren’t it. These are your “cute brunch, picnic, or summer BBQ where you pretend you might garden later but absolutely won’t” overalls. Final verdict: Cute and comfy once you decontaminate them… just be prepared to use way more laundry pods than recommended and an entire box of Arm & Hammer odor absorbing baking soda first. 😅

My measurements are 130 lbs, 5'3", waist 28 -29", hips 38 - 40" and I ordered the medium (US 8 - 10) in Bay Blue. I find the overalls are true to size, and the Bay Blue is as shown in the advertisement. I do plan on wearing them over the Summer, but I wanted to style them up for an office look. I wore black tights, with kitten heel black booties, a puff sleeved black blouse with a collar. I added a scarf--shaped as a tie. I wore a long sweater belted at the waist over the top. I was comfortable, yet office ready too. Overalls are a versatile garment as they can be dressed up or down AND can be used in different environments. The material is composed of 75% Cotton, 24% Polyester, and 1% Elastane which allows the wearer freedom of movement Elastane, temperature-regulating Cotton, and wrinkle controlling Polyester. I am heat-sensitive, so when I do layer it has to be with Cotton. The adjustable straps are easy to set and forget; as are the side-closure buttons. The bib front pockets are perfect for storing your phone or glasses, and the short's pockets are perfect for resting your hands in when sitting or standing. I have enjoyed wearing these overalls, and look forward to using them over the Summer too.

This dress is really cute, but unfortunately it did not fit me right. I am 6’0, 145 pounds, 36D bra size. I bought a Medium and it fit, but not comfortably. I was being choked. It was so tight in the neck area. I might try ordering a Large to see if that works as it was also a little too short for my body type. The material is a thick stretchy denim, it’s good quality. Overall it’s a great dress, flattering. I think I just need to size up. I will update my review after I try the Large. Giving 5 stars for now because it is great otherwise.

I love these. First of all, I'm 5'6" and around 125lbs, which usually lands me in between sizes. I wear a small OR medium, depending on the cut and brand. For that reason, I hesitate to buy clothes online. But it worked out very well with these pants. I chose a small and they are perfect. What makes them extra comfortable—the fabric is my favorite kind of denim: the kind that stretches. My middle-aged body likes the stretchy material, and when weight fluctuates or my body does something lovely, like decide to bloat for a day, these pants adapt beautifully. I really like the pockets and the styling, and the mid-weight of the denim. They aren't airy and light, but not thick and heavy, either. They super casual but make every outfit look put together. I wore these out with a chunky bangle and a white button-down and felt amazing. I love these pants and I'd definitely recommend them!

Hixiaohe Women Autumn 3/4 Sleeve Retro Short Denim Jackets Light Blue Jean Coats I really like this jean jacket. It is of extremely nice quality and looks great and I like the cut and style. It is made of real denim material, meaning there is no stretchiness to it, so I think it is well worth the $30 price point. I like where the pockets are located. I do have to return it because it is a bit too small. I wear a XL (14-16) in tops and usually fit great in tops with a 41" bust, this jacket lists the bust as 46" which is why I went with my normal size. I could button it up if needed, but it would have been quite snug around my chest. Also, it is tight around my shoulders so there was some restriction when moving my arms forward. So, for me and my body style I may choose to exchange it for an XXL. This jacket would look great with anything, for me I am trying to find cute things to wear with my new dresses.

user_comment: I’m 5’0” and about 110 lbs and ordered an XS, fully expecting the length to be long — which I was fine with. What I didn’t expect was how big these are in the waist. They’re supposed to be high-waisted, but because the waistband is so roomy, they don’t sit where they should. Instead of giving that flattering cinched look, they drop down and lose the whole shape. Pros: ✔️ Trendy wide-leg, street-style vibe ✔️ Nice relaxed boyfriend cut ✔️ Functional pockets ✔️ Denim feels durable Cons: ➖ Waist runs very large on slim builds ➖ High-waisted fit doesn’t work if the waist doesn’t hold ➖ Not petite-friendly without alterations If you’re on the slimmer side, especially under 5’2”, an XS may still be too big in the waist. For me, it just didn’t give the structured, high-waisted silhouette shown in the photos. The style is cute in theory, but the proportions just aren’t dialed in for smaller frames. If you have curves or prefer a looser, lower-slung fit, these might work better for you. For petite and slim builds, though, the sizing is definitely something to be aware of.

I think I lucked out here. If I’d gone by the size chart measurements I should have ordered a 0X. I’m 5’7” and weight between 155-160 and typically wear a large in pants. I went with the large over the 0X because that just couldn’t be right. The large fits perfectly. It’s the right size through the waist as well as hips and thighs. What I absolutely love about this pair of pants is the wrap waist. Someone is brilliant to have come up with this. I’ve had six children, the mom pooch is real. Plus I’ve lost weight recently and it does the saggy thing that happens. The right side front wraps to the left and buttons closed. You get a little compression and it holds my belly in a little. Then the left side of the waist wraps across to the right and buttons closed. I just got a little compression wrap from my jeans!!!? I totally did! And it’s not squeezy or uncomfortable, it just pulls everything in and makes a smoother silhouette. There’s no muffin top, no lumps or bumps. It just looks like I lost a good five to ten pounds. These feel nice, the denim is soft and comfortable and I can move easily in them. I wore them out to eat and was not at all bothered by them. You don’t want to be thinking about your pants when you’re eating in a restaurant and trying to enjoy someone’s company. We’ve all been there, though and it’s not fun. Especially when you’re typing to discreetly button your pants again when you stand back up and hope no one sees what you’re actually doing. I got the denim color and it’s a true blue denim, exactly what you’d expect. They are made well and all the stitching and seams look well sewn. For $40 they are a fabulous price. I’ve already ordered a second pair. My recommendation is to buy the size you would usually get. You might need to hassle a bit with a return, but I think you’ll be pretty close going with your usual S/M/L/XL size. I would absolutely go through the hassle for these, especially having free returns.

Love these jeans, may even buy another pair in a different shade. I got the Iceland blue and it’s a light wash color. I’m 5’3” and have a short torso with long legs with a 4 month bump. I even seem to have a B bump and I’m a little self conscious about that and want to smooth it out. These jeans don’t really do that, but that’s alright. Before pregnancy I was between a 2 and a 4 and weighed 128 lbs. Now I’m around 10 lbs more than that or so depending on the day. In these photos I am wearing maternity leggings underneath for the cold, so these are just slightly looser without that, but I liked the fit enough when I tried them on without leggings I planned to keep them. I will be able to grow even more into them. I also didn’t iron these so i think they’d look better with some laundering attention. These jeans fit PERFECT. I ordered the 2-4. The bump material is loose enough to allow more growth as I progress, and the little sizer buttons on the side help with keeping it a little tighter right now. It’s not slipping off me at all. I like the fit around the back booty because it’s not too tight but it hugs my shape and looks defined with more room to grow thanks to the stretch in the denim. The loose fit on the leg is in style right now. All in all, these are great and i recommend them.

Sidefeel Womens Smocked 3 4 Sleeve Button Down Denim Jeans Dresses I love this dress. I ordered a small (I'm 5'2 and 128 pounds, size 00 and small normally). This ran big but I think that it's supposed to. It was really comfortable, the weight was good and it's made of a softer material. It looks like denim but it feels like a cotton blend. It does NOT have pockets and those would've made this the perfect outfit! It buttons from collar to knee and the dress itself stopped a few inches above my knee when standing up straight. The sleeves stop a little above the wrist and have a scrunchy cuff but it doesn't dig into your skin. LOVE it.

Ordered blue, medium, based on Amazon recommendations 5‘6“ tall 135 pounds waist 29 inches hips 39 inches Medium fits as in the picture. Not tight and waist, not tighten hips and has plenty of stretch. If I wanted tight sexy fit, probably could have ordered the small. This is great and will likely fit me if I gain a little weight. Hits me approximately 4 to 5 inches above the knee. I could put it higher on my waist and make it shorter or lower on my waist to make it longer. The stretch makes it feel like I’m not wearing anything at all. Deep front pockets that hold my iPhone 15. Great back pockets that also hold my iPhone. I would buy this again. The color I bought is denim/faded denim color with gold stitching. For information, in most things if the hips fit, the waist is too large, and if the waist fits, the hips are too large. In the skirt, the waist is fine, and not too snug and the hips are fine and not too snug.

5 ft.8, 140 lbs. I was looking for a mustard yellow denim skirt and this one seemed decent for the price. It fit well and I washed it in cold water and low heat dry and I don't think it shrank at all. Nice pale mustard yellow color. The zipper is a little funny but works fine. It was definitely a shorter hem than I figured when ordering but I'm tall-ish and have long legs so that's not uncommon with skirts for me. First time I wore it as part of an office outfit with a print blouse and pumps with low heels. I got some nice compliments but I admit I was a little self-conscious because I felt like I was showing a LOT of leg, especially sitting down. I wore basically the same outfit another time but with ballet flats and a light wrap cardigan and that seemed like a better combo than with heels. Would also work well as a casual outfit with sandals/flip flops and a camisole etc. The material is thin but not see-through so I might buy it again in white. It's like a pencil/miniskirt (almost) at least on my body type...

Nice quality dress with stretch. For reference I am 5'3" and 190lbs and purchased an XL. The fit and length on me was perfect. I paired it with a long sleeve lace top underneath to dress it up a little. I personally did not find the top to run small, like others have mentioned. I ordered my true size and it fit beautifully. One key feature I really liked was that the buttons don't open up the entire length of the dress. It only opens up to where my knee is sticking out for a little leg show. I chased toddlers around at my baby's birthday party and was so nervous my dress would come completely unbuttoned, until I noticed they only unbutton by the legs. Just a little key feature for those looking for that added security. The fabric feels high quality, the blue denim color was lovely and it has pretty deep pockets too. You can definitely dress this outfit up or down.

I love the Luvamia stretchy jeans shorts for summer wear. The low waist in a crossover style goes with pretty much everything. I pair these with a loose top or baby graphic T. I also live in Florida so I won't be wearing these for long periods of time but wanted something a bit thicker to maybe hide my dimply legs in the colder months. These do a great job of hiding my dimply legs as far as I can see, so that's a big win for me.I bought a pack of all black l-xl and tried all 3 pair on. They fit perfectly. Same fit for all 3. They bunch up at my ankles but I'm okay with that. I'm short and I'm used to it. They are very high waisted as well. They are not a compression legging so if you're looking for that, these aren't it in my opinion but I guess it depends on your body type. These also fit well enough so that I don't have to wear a longer shirt to cover my butt so I'm happy all around with these.They are soft, stretchy and very comfortable. They feel pretty darn warm so far, and I'm confident when it gets cooler down here, these will be perfect.Great price for 3 pair as well. Very happy with purchase so far.

Everything about the fit was great EXCEPT for the length. They are shorter than desired and I am only 5’4”. Makes it so I need to wear with taller boots. The point of these pants is to keep warm and chilly exposed ankles is not what I need. Otherwise they are warm and seem durable.

I know that most listing photos are AI these days, but the listing photo is not a good representation of these pants. There are a few things I like about them: the stretchy waist, the color of the denim, the wide legs. Most things I do not like, however. Even with the stretchy waist, they still run a bit small, even before washing them and without putting them in the dryer. They are as comfortable as a slightly too small pair of pants can be. I'm very annoyed by the cuffs of the pants - It does not appear any care was given to them being cut off, as you can see by the crooked cut in my photo. There is a back stitch at the bottom of the side and inner seams, but no stitching along the bottom, so there's nothing to keep them from continuing to fray up with each wear and wash. Fortunately I have a sewing machine so I can add a seam to the bottom. I haven't decided if I'm going to fold them under and add a traditional seam, or just add a seam to the bottom so that the fraying is limited, as I think both could be cute. As they are, the legs are either a bit too short or a bit too long for my taste. They're not full-length jeans, but they're not capris, either. I'm 5'5" and the fit could vary depending on a person's height. They're not incredibly flattering, but they aren't unflattering either. Considering that I'm going to have to do some work on them, I definitely do not consider them a good value at the full listed price, and even at the current listed sale price it seems a bit high. I would give more stars if more attention was given to the way the legs were finished - or, rather, NOT finished. I guess I'm just a bit underwhelmed. They aren't bad, but they aren't great, either.

Exactly what I wanted. Im 5'4 208lb I wear 16wide usually. Based upon customer reviews, Amazon recommended size 18 and they were right. The fit is good. I have a big belly with a fupa. This is a really lovely blouse. It is made of a soft jean fabric that can be purchased in various traditional shades for jeans, from my light blue to black. I really like the sleeve detail and the fact that it’s a button-down shirt gives it a look that can be worn up or down depending on the situation. The fabric feels really nice and it does have stretch to it and I really like the button down shirt, curves, side look, and thought the design looked really nice on the fabric. Unfortunately, there are two significant flaws to this blouse. The first one is the price. Paying $50 plus tax for a blouse that can be dressed up, but never enough to be considered business attire, seems excessive. And given the current economic issues around the world, is likely going to put a lot of people out of purchase range. Like, these aren’t pants and aren’t 100% Jean material so the cost seems unnecessary. The second, and most important one, is that this runs small. Like, very disappointingly small. Looking at the pictures in the sellers page the models look like they are wearing blouses with breathing room. I figured if I purchased my usual size that I would be fine even if it ran a little small because the picture showed a comfortable fit. If you look at my included picture, you will see that this was completely wrong. I’d recommend sizing up at least one size, if not two, depending on the look you’re really going for. Otherwise, you are likely to end with something that just is not flattering. On top of everything if you are someone with large breasts, you might want to size up two sizes. I am a larger C or small D cup in my current size and the blouse looks completely unflattering and too tight, to the point where the button that runs across the chest area is very obviously being stretched. Not a look anyone wants to have.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice blouse, but runs too small

user_comment: These denim shorts from Cicy Bell are really nice. I love the pocket size and placement in the front instead of off to the sides. I think it has a slimming effect. There are also two large pockets in the back. The vertical stripes are slimming also. These shorts have an A shape but aren't too high rise either. So, they flare out at the bottom quite a bit. The fit is comfortable if you get the size right. The material of these denim shorts is cotton with a little bit of elastane for some stretch. They will shrink a bit. They were large on me, so I washed them in hot water and tumbled dry on high.As far as sizing goes, I'm going to say these shorts run small. I am now wearing a 4-6 pant size and had ordered these in a 12 some time ago. I did shrink them but should have been swimming in them and they still actually fit me. For reference, I am 5'7", with a 28" waist.At the time of this review, you have your choice from five different colors and the price for these shorts is $24.99 which I think is reasonable.

Lee Women's Secretly Shapes Regular Fit Straight Leg Pants I’ve been searching for comfortable casual/dressy pants for work for a while, and I had such high hopes on these Lee straight leg mid rise pants. The current trend of high waist pants are uncomfortable. I was pleasantly surprised by how well these fit: they’re tailored and professional looking yet casual at the same time. They are the perfect length for which I was searching, to wear with loafers on days I don’t wish to wear high heels. They’re fitted, comfortable and stylish but unfortunately they are too tight in the buttocks with a dental floss look from behind. I’ll be returning them. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 110 lbs. and I ordered the size 2. The fawn color is a good neutral shade.

These cargo jeans from Knoint are nice. The main reason I won't wear these jeans though is the bottom hem. It's drawstring and it won't stay drawn. Another reason is they don't have any front pockets and that's always a deal breaker for me. Lastly, they are high-waisted elastic the entire way around and it cinches in. These jeans are nice enough for the right person though. Those things are all just my personal preferences. They have two cargo pockets on each side with ties at each pocket. And they have two functional back pockets. The denim is made of polyester and spandex, so it won't fade easily or shrink at all. And it's fairly heavy. I think the way they fit is flattering and they are comfortable enough.I would say these jeans are true to size. I'm between small and medium in bottoms right now and ordered the medium. They fit with a little extra room as expected but I think the smalls would have been too small. For reference, I am 5'7", 28" waist, 36" hip.At the time of this review, you have your choice of seven different washes and styles and the price for these jeans is $42.99 which I think is getting a bit high for polyester denim.

This review isn't for the gal in her 20s, but for the older mom who is trying to step away from skinny jeans. That's no simple task. These jeans and I are getting used to one another, but they have a lot going for them. They are high-waisted, so cover any muffin top or extra weight on the hips. The elastic waistband was a little daunting to me when I first opened them, but it doesn't show or look frumpy, because it's higher and at the back and really just keeps things tight and comfy. The pockets are deep and useful and the jeans have stretch. That said, I ordered a medium and really probably needed a large. I am 5'7" tall and 155 lbs with generous hips and should have definitely gone up to a large. So, from my experience, they run a smidge small. They also have a long length. They work for me, but if you are too much shorter, you may need to hem. The wide leg is more in style and comfy, but I still have a hard time feeling like it is especially flattering. These are good quality jeans that will last a long time and I'm hopeful I can keep adapting to the different cut.

These jorts are super casual and on trend with their super wide leg. The wider the leg, the more casual the jort. These aren't Bermuda shorts, they are longer, they reach to the knee, so I was keeping my eyes out for jorts, not just Bermuda shorts. These are perfect for casual errands, the beach or the park. They are so comfortable, and you don't have to worry about them showing too much or being too short. They are almost like skater shorts, but flare out as they go, so they are their own style. You can be modest and in style too with these jorts. They've got spandex, so they stretch comfortably, and the fabric is really soft to the touch. For reference, I'm 5'5", 110 lbs. with a waist of 27 1/2" and purchased the size small, my usual size. They fit perfectly. I love cotton in my clothing, which is why I love denim. You can't beat the texture, look, breathability and comfort that cotton offers. These jorts are forgiving if you have flabby thighs. They cover them! I love the deep blue color, the denim, the cotton , the stretch, the comfort. These jorts are high quality. For the coupon price of $23.99, these are a fair deal, but I would love to see them at $19.99 for a great value even more.

Really cute, lightweight, flowy dress. I also have the maxi version. Great quality and can be worn with sandals or boots even. Got a lot of compliments when I wore it. I'm 5'5, 150ish lbs, 36DD, size med-lg. I have this in a large but medium works too as I have the maxi in a medium. It's generous even with a full bust.

Yes, these fit like one would expect Spanx jeans to fit. My caveat is they are more of a Spanx legging than Spanx jeans. As you can see they have no zipper and you pull them on. Credit is given for it being a very realistic looking faux zipper front! Once you wrassel (I'm from Mississippi and that's what I felt like I was doing-wrassling) your way into them they are VERY comfy. Plus, while you can feel the control in the fit, though they are actually really comfortable to wear. They stretch enough, while they do not lose any of the control that Spanx is famous for providing! The quality is definitely there, as we would all expect from the Spanx brand. I am 5'4" and 128 pounds and a Medium fit me perfectly, but they were loooong. I get it, it is easier for those who are pocket-sized to hem a pair of jeans than our more statuesque sisters to create more fabric from nothing. But, have any of y'all tried to find a seamstress lately? I don't want to resort to the iron on hem, but, sigh, what else can we do? Overall a nice fitting, pull-on jean/legging. I wish they offered a petite version. Even my high platform Docs don't give me enough leg for these not to drag.

Cute, but definitely the wrong color. I ordered the khaki (3 I think?) and the photo looks more brownish, which is what I wanted, but the actual pants are way more of a burnt orange. I was pretty sad because I had a sweater I really hoped would match. Aside from color, I’m 5’5” 140lbs with long legs and the length was perfect, fit was good. They are soft and very comfortable, but I honestly feel like the material was a little cheap looking. All in all, I would have kept them if the color was more brown. Thinking about ordering the other khaki option and seeing if they are the right color.

I bought these for my teenage daughter who is 5'5 and 117 lbs. She loves the style and color, and they fit her great! She says they're comfortable with the draw string as well. The material feels very durable and soft. She wants some more in other colors! Great jeans!
These are so cute, I got so many compliments. They’re very stretchy and comfortable. Only thing is they’re a bit small so if you’re between sizes I’d size up. I’m 5’7 and they were the perfect length

I like and dislike things about these pants. Disappointing: the zipper is sewn in too close to the edge of the flap so you can see some of the zipper material which is a darker color. Because of that, it looks like I'm busting out of my pants when I wear them. No woman wants that! Maybe I got a pair that needed better QC? In retrospect, I should have returned them but I was leaving for a trip and needed a pair of jeans. I have to make sure I wear then with a shirt that covers the zipper area. To note: I am 5'8 and the pants are a little longer than I expected (that isn't a big deal to me.) The style is cute with the wide leg and high-waist. There is quite a bit of stretch in the pants which make them very comfortable to wear. I recommend the pants but if the zipper is off to the side like mine, I would return them.

These pants are chef kiss! I have a hard time finding pants I like as I’m curvy in the hip thigh bum area and average height of 5’4. These pants check all the boxes! Nice oversized leg but not too baggy or loose the comfy adjustable waist and the adorable print! I ordered the xl I could have got a large as well but I will keep these as I like the more oversized look. I typically wear a size 12 in jeans as well get them you will love them!

NYDJ Women's Petite Sheri Jean Medium weight denim jeans with just the right amount of stretch. I purchased size 0P. I’m 5’3”, 100 lbs, waist is 25, hips 34.5”. Didn’t need to hem them. I think elastic is sewn between the denim band layers — is what seems to keep the jeans snug without the ever so annoying gap in the back. They definitely are body-hugging waist to upper thighs, but not like skinny jeans. It was difficult to get an accurate color photo, but the two close up photos are the correct hue, although the color “Rinse” is a touch darker. Also like that the leg is just a narrower straight leg versus a skinny leg.

I am 5’4” and 175 lbs. This lightweight top is perfect for me - I got a LARGE because I like a slouchier fit, but I could’ve gotten a medium and it would have still fit. I love this too so much! Great for summer heat. Can be dressed up or casual.

I loved this shirt, it really put together my business casual look for work. The color was exact from the picture. It definitely exceeded my expectations. I ordered two more. I ordered the small. I am 5’2 140lbs.
I bought this for work and love it. Great quality and for. Material is soft and stretchy. Lightweight so perfect for summer. For is true to size, for reference I’m 5’7” 145lbs wearing a medium

I love these shirts! They are great with dress pants but can also be worn with jeans to dress them up as well. They are very comfortable and are good quality. ***for reference I am 5’6” and weigh 145 pounds
Cute, comfortable, and can be dressed up or down. I am 5’6 150 lbs. I purchased a large. Light weight and good quality.

Love this polyester material soft and shiny with breathable holes.. Covers my chunky arms to my elbows and the pleated top makes this so dainty and feminine of a top it does see through on the holes but that's the sexy part about it v-neck is very beautiful with the color that goes Midway around slimming in the front good for the big busted woman I normally wear a 42 DDD bra size 14-16 pants size. and this blouse is the right size for me.. I bought these in Small because reviews said they run large. Definitely not true. I wanted high-waist elastic-waistband jeans to be able to wear crop tops. I reordered these in Medium, and they’re still very snug. I’ll wear them (5’6”, 127 lbs.) but am not really impressed. The material would not be good for jogging, as it’s winter-weight heavy and stiff.

I was looking for mom jeans ,came across this and it's very nice and comfortable. Just what I was looking for. I have a gut and I normally wear Large, so I risked it getting a X Large. Fits great! I'm 170 lbs and I'm 4'11.
Love these joggers. Super comfy and flattering. I’m a size 8/10 in jeans. 5’8” 160lbs and ordered a large and they were perfect

I am 5'8" and I tend to need a tall size (just barely). These pants in size 6 and light brown fit perfectly. The material is soft with some stretch, almost a jegging-type feel but still a pant. The quality seems good but due to the thinness of the soft material, I can tell it would be prone to stretching/tearing if worn too tightly. I purchased these for a work as a healthcare professional. I can't seem to find similar styles in local stores anymore.

I love everything about these pants. The color is a beautiful deep burgundy. Material is soft. It has 4 real pockets. Length is perfect for someone of my height. I ordered a 6. For reference: I am 5.5 1/2 and 140lbs. I am currently in-between sizing. I usually wear a 7/8. Some 6's fit, but tend to be snug in the crotch area when sitting. So I figured it would be a safe bet to go with the 6 since they run big and they would not be snug and they are not snug at all. I probably could have ordered a 4 and would be completely fine. I may get the Royal Blue in a 4 and compare the fit of the two, but we will see. Overall I am very happy with these pants. I am giving it a 4 star rating b/c through out the day, they did tend to ride up a bit & are starting to bunch at the top of my boots, but that could be b/c these are big on me, comfortable, but big. I would definitely recommend getting these if you are on the marked for comfortable, burgundy pants.

With my last order, I now have seven pairs of these pants: Navy, Black, Papaya, Mauve Pink, Medium Wash, Black, Wasabi, and Hazel. One to complement each of my date night tops! They're really more jeggings than jeans - I never use the zipper or snap, I just pull them on. However, the fabric is thick enough that they look more like slim fit jeans than leggings once they're on. Both front and back pockets are actually functional. I love that they come in individual sizes instead of just S, M, L, and XL. At 5'3" and 107 pounds, a size 6 is comfortable on me. I could probably wear a 4, but I like a looser fit. I won't subtract a star, but I was disappointed with my last pair in Navy - the color is so dark, it appears to be black, as you can see in the photo of the black pair next to the navy one. (Navy is on the right.)
I love the yellow color and feel of the material, but they are tight in the legs and very long. I am 5 ft. 2 in. I have to roll them up 2 times. But other than that I really love them.

I love this top! I got it in the deep pink color, size small. The fit is definitely relaxed, just like the pictures, so if you want something more fitted I would size down. For reference, I’m 5'2", 130 pounds, and usually wear a size 2–4 in tops. This one works well for me tucked in, which I think is the most flattering way to wear it. The pink is a little lighter in person than the Amazon picture shows, but still really pretty. The material is lightweight and has a textured feel to it. It isn’t super soft like cotton, but it’s breathable and great for warm weather. I like the cap sleeves and the simple V-neckline because they make it easy to use for both casual outfits and business casual. It looks nice with jeans, skirts, or work pants. Since it’s polyester, it did come a little wrinkled from the packaging, but hanging it up after washing helped a lot. Overall, this is a cute and versatile summer top. The relaxed fit is comfortable, the color looks nice in person, and it’s easy to dress up or down. I would definitely recommend.

This blouse is super flattering and comfortable. I ordered a size large at 160 lbs, and while it is a little roomy around the waist, I found the fit to be really nice. The material quality is soft, not see-through, and perfect for warmer days without needing an extra layer. I chose the hot pink color, and it is incredibly vibrant and looks fantastic against my skin tone. If you prefer a more fitted look, I would suggest sizing down, but I personally love the length, cut, and overall style.

Comfortable fit!! Definitely recommended. I was nervous about the size i selected because the size chart seems a little off. However they fit just right. Its a little bit of space at the top but that didnt bother me personally. Ordering another pair in different color!!! They can be worn with a heel or sneakers and still look super cute. Im 5'6 and 212 lbs. When measured i did my hips which is 41in my waist is like 37.5-38. Hope that helps!!!

I loveeee these pants ! I really could have gone a size down but these still fit just fine! I’m 5’3 and the pants are a big long but there are snaps at the bottom to adjust the height and also they will be perfectly fine once I put shoes on! Stretchy back part of the pants.

I love these pants they are so cute, high waisted with an elastic band in the back so they stay in place. I am short 5’3” and these pants drag on the ground. My only negative is that they come with straps for around your ankle but depending on how you snap them they fall easily. I got tired of picking them up off the ground and just put them in one of the many pockets. Received so many compliments that I just started sharing the link with my friends. I’m a girls girl no gate keeping here!

These jeans are super cute, comfy and I received many compliments. I love that they are long - I’m 5’6 and with heels, my feet are still covered. The only thing I didn’t like was the baggy look in the crotch area. Overall, best purchase. I ordered a small!

Absolutely love these jeans! I was nervous after reading some reviews but decided to try them anyways. They do run a little big but it still worked out for me as I like a more loose fit AND they’re TALL GIRL FRIENDLY! I’m 5’9 and 230lbs, ordered a 2X. It is a tad “bunchy” in the crotch though area like some reviewers mentioned but I still loved wearing them and I received many compliments. Didn’t have any issues with the buttons either, I would definitely order again!
Love these shorts! I’m 5ft 1 inches tall and weigh 140 lbs. I first purchased a large not realizing how stretchy they were so I bought two more pairs in a medium and they fit me perfect. I love them.

They are super cute! Great stretch BUT they are shorter than I expected. I’m 48 yrs old and wasn’t planing to wear “daisy Dukes” the Garth concert. They ride up my thighs which then makes the the pockets stick out of the holes in front and second I know I’ll be tugging them down so my butt cheeks don’t show. I hope they don’t shrink. Anyway although I’m petite I am curvy so if you have a booty and a small waist just want to give you a heads up on the fit.

I’m 5’9” and between a size 10 and 12, 170 lbs. these are a perfect length. I don’t feel they are too booty. The stretchy material is excellent. There is no muffin top. I just ordered another pair in another color. I got the style 17-dark blue, size large.

Size fits, love the stretch and button fly, super comfy… disappointed that the color was very blue rather than light blue and the destruction on the sides is a very different pattern. They’ll do just not what I was actually ordering I’m 5’4” and 148lbs and the large fit well.

These were so tiny went to come out of the package I asked my 12-year-old daughter to put them on just for fun but she couldn’t fit either!!! She only weighs 100 pounds!! I bought a large I’m usually a 7-8 or 27-28 Photo is the button up ones I bought here the other is my normal shorts just fir reference to how small they are

I’m 5’5” and about 140lbs. Tend to carry most of my weight in the tummy area. I have a hard time finding pants that fit my belly area comfortably but don’t leave my thighs swimming in excess fabric. I love these pants so far! I love the rise. I love that I can sit and move comfortably. They are fitted enough through my thighs so I feel good about myself and they don’t cut in to the waist/belly. The fabric feels nice and thick compared to other pull on jeggings I have tried in the past. The length is also good on me.

First I order this small, perfect color, hot pink, but they appear to have rolls under the butt cheek and it’s not me. It’s the pants so I decided I will try the medium. The medium is the same way but the color is a fusion color not hot pink the medium was a little bit bigger but they still have these rolls that appear under the butt cheek. I’m 52 120 pounds the length is perfect on the small on the medium they were longer. I really tried to like these pants because I wanted a pink pair so bad but they’re just not gonna work out for me. On both pair looks like I’m wearing pants that are too tight for me and my rolls are showing under my butt. Same with the med. Its something in the material because you can even see that when you don’t have them on. I really wonder if it would go away when they were washed but for this price I would be stuck with jeans that I didn’t really like. I hope this helps.

(Ignore the hairspray on my mirror!) I am 5’0, inseam 29”, waist 29-30, hips 40-41. I got the large. I am usually a size medium or 8-10 in bottoms. I did try the medium with these but returned them because I found the waist too tight. They’re nice and stretchy, softer material. NO gap in the back, hallelujah. The pockets are significant! The last time I had jeans with large pockets like this, they were from the men’s section. I measured the inseam at 29” on the size large. After a couple washes the black has faded only a bit, but I don’t mind that. They did not shrink, and I put them in the dryer on low. Very happy with these.

I really liked the styling of these Womens High Waisted Wide Leg Jeans by Allimy, so I ordered them. My new "normal" after losing a bunch of weight is a size 18 but these only go up to 16, and that's what I ordered. The waist is 35" but it has a lot of stretch and fit my 38" waist with no trouble. These jeans are really very comfortable to wear, but I don't like the way they look at all.For one thing, they are Very high waisted and also Very stretchy. They tend to shift down a bit (for those of us who don't have hips to hold things up), so then there is extra fabric in the crotch area. They also are so stretchy that they like to make "pudge pockets", a term I just coined, thank you very much. Any place where there is a bit of pudge, they seem to like to grab and emphasize it. This is because along with cotton they have 34% polyester and 1% spandex, which makes the fabric very soft, smooth, and stretchy, but I prefer the crispness of all cotton.Some other issues I have are that the fabric is very heavy, especially the flare part sewn in at the hems. I mean to say literally, when holding up these jeans, they feel like they have weights in them... which I guess is from the polyester fabric that is obviously denser than cotton. They're also very, very long. I am 5'8" and short waisted with long legs, so it's rare that I find a pair of pants too long for me, but these are. I also don't like the way that the bottom part is added (sewn) to the leg, because it makes it lay funny. I like my jeans to come forward over my shoes, but these like to drape width-wise, which just looks odd to me.They are well made, nicely styled jeans and I really love the way they look... until I put them on. Even the hips look weird from the back, because these jeans flare out at the hips, but I don't own those (LOL) so from the back they are baggy at the sides of the butt.My final conclusion- cool looking jeans with an uncool fit. Currently they are selling for $37.99, which is a reasonable price for sure if you can get the sizing right.

These jeans are exactly as described - high waisted and wide legged. The first thing that struck me when I opened them was how different the material felt from a lot of jeans that I have now. It doesn’t feel like the stretchy kind of material in skinny jeans or the soft, yet incredibly thin material of other brands. This feels like the jeans of the 90s or before. A nice, thick, durable cotton denim. I really liked that about it a lot. The jeans are definitely high-waisted, and, being that I’m not a super huge fan of high-waisted in general, these are much too much for me in that department. I’m merely 5’1” which may be part of the problem, but these come up on the bottom of my ribs. They virtually cover my entire stomach. If that is a feature you look for in your high-waisted jeans, these will make you very happy. They have about a 3 inch wide cuff on the bottom. As stated, I’m 5’1” and will only need to hem around 2 inches off the bottom. I feel like the length may be a problem if you’re about 5’5” or more, as these could end up possibly being too short. These jeans have that great worn-in color, deep pockets down the front, and with the baggy feel, it gives that perfect comfy boyfriend jeans vibe. I do feel like they may have shrunken up a bit when washed, even though I did not dry them. They just felt a bit tighter on the stomach and it was harder to button them than when they were brand new. However, as a female, we fluctuate with a few pounds here and there, so that’s also a possibility. I like these jeans. I’ve always been a fan of baggy and bootcut jeans. I think these would look great with the right kind of shoe and can also keep you looking slim with the high waist. Definitely the feature I like the most is the craftsmanship and the thick denim which feels like they’ll last a really long time

Update: After having a friend who is far more curvy than myself try these on, we’ve determined that these jeans are great for curvy gals. She is the same size as me, but curvier and they look awesome on her and fit perfectly. For reference, I’m 5’8”, 170 lbs, and normally wear regular-length pants. These pants seem to run a bit long. They’re too long for me to wear with flats, but they work well with platforms or a slight heel. I wanted so badly to love these pants, but they’re not a 5-star fit for me. The material is supportive yet stretchy, and the high-rise design helps support my postpartum mom tummy, which is a plus. They seem to be good quality, and the back pockets are thoughtfully reinforced. My only issue is that the crotch bunches up a bit when I walk. It improved somewhat after washing, but it’s still noticeable. I think with the right styling, I can make them work, so overall, they’re nice—just not a perfect fit for my body. One other thing to note (in case anyone is sensitive to smells): these pants have a strong chemical odor, even after one wash.

I like the fit and the style of these jeans. The material has a nice stretch to it, making these comfortable to wear. I did have a problem with the length of these jeans. They were way too long for me. I’m 5’5”. I ended up cutting off the entire bottom hemmed section. I did leave the seam at the bottom and I’m not going to bother to hem these any further. See photo for reference. Now they fit just right. Still almost hits the ground with shoes on, but for this style of pant, that would be appropriate. I’m not really missing that overly wide hem. I think these jeans look just as good (or better) without it! Just my opinion/preference.

These high-waisted boyfriend jeans in black are a stylish win for my fall wardrobe! The rich, deep black color is absolutely stunning and true to the pictures. They have a very chic vibe that fits my love for denim and elegant looks. The premium cotton-elastane-polyester blend (65% Cotton, 34% Polyester, 1% Elastane) feels stretchy, soft, and breathable, striking the perfect balance of comfort and durability. On my 5’1”, 138-pound frame, the medium size provides a relaxed, straight-leg fit that’s flattering without being too baggy, elongating my legs beautifully. They are a tad long but a pair of 2 inch heels will help. The design details are spot-on for my style. The high-waisted cut slims my waist, and the side slant pockets, back patch pockets, and front zip closure add a utilitarian-chic touch that’s both functional and trendy. The full-length inseam drapes nicely, making these jeans perfect for pairing with my favorite ankle boots or sneakers. I’ve styled them with a flowy blouse for a casual brunch look and a fitted top for a more polished vibe—they work effortlessly for both. The fabric is machine-washable, which is a huge plus for my busy lifestyle, and it holds up well without losing shape. My only minor note is that the stretchy material, while comfy, might feel slightly snug after long wear for those who prefer a looser fit. Still, these jeans are a fantastic for a great price.

The best thing about these jeans is how they make your rear look! I got a size 4, which says 3 /4 in the description. I think they fit true to size for a 3 but could fit someone who is a size 4. They have a good amount of stretch. OVERALL STYLE: This is a super cute style if you are tall or have really long legs. The wide cuff at the bottom is trendy but doesn't work for me at 5'1. I'd have to cut the whole thing off. The wide legs are perfect! COLOR: The color is nice. It doesn't have the white or faded white outlines around the pockets and seams like it shows in the pics. I think that would make it cuter. MATERIAL / FABRIC: It's a soft, lightweight denim with a good amount of stretch. The cuffs at the bottom are two layers of fabrid so they will hang/move well if you're tall enough to pull it off. QUALITY: The quality and stitching seems good. VALUE: I think these are a good value at the current price.

I love these shorts, but I ordered two pairs, both the same size just a different color. The denim pair was a bit tight for being a large, but I was able to wear them in. The light gray pair however, was much tighter, so I had to return them. Unfortunately, there is no larger size. For context, I am 5' 5" and 165 lbs. I wish I could order another size because there are my favorite shorts when they fit.

Another winner from Anrabess! I ordered this sweater in my usual Anrabess size medium and it fits perfectly. I am 5’7” tall and weigh 175lbs and I can confidently order a medium in an Anrabess sweater and know it will fit. I love the boatneck sweater. It is the perfect weight for the warmer climate where I live. It is still hot here…in the 90s, so I’m waiting for Fall to arrive so I can wear this adorable but simple sweater. My closet is full of Anrabess sweaters and I’ve never had an issue with quality. I ordered the color Deep Taupe and it is a perfect taupe color. It will be perfect for my upcoming vacation capsule wardrobe when traveling to Europe in October.
