These Capri jeans are nice, quality made pants. The fabric is medium soft and medium thickness. With the wide leg design, they have good airflow for keeping cool on warmer days. The material consists of 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. There is a nice amount of stretch to make them extra comfy on my waist. They are good for any activity I would be doing as the roomy legs and stretchy fabric allow for plenty of flexible movement with no pinching or binding anywhere. The button/zipper fasteners work smoothly without any snagging. They have 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets. There are belt loops for anyone who wants to wear a belt with them. I wasn't sure I would like the Bay Blue color, but wanted to see what it really looked like, aside from photos. I love it! The Bay Blue color is one of those ones that looks far better in person than it does in photos. I ordered these in a size XL, which is my regular size for jeans, pants, etc. They fit me very comfortably with just the right amount of snugness on my waist and hips. The legs are roomy and breathable. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs. They are probably slightly longer on me than they are intended to be, but I'm shorter than I wish I was, too. Overall, I'm happy that this pair of Capri jeans turned out to be so nice and I highly recommend them! I will be wearing these often when warmer weather arrives. The current price (1-23-2026) of $29.99 is a fair value, but there is also a coupon available at this time to make them a good value. I would definitely order these again. There are other shades of blue, plus Cream White and Washed Black to choose from.

These are nice quality capris, or jorts as my daughter calls them. When I first held them up, I thought there was no way they would fit because the waist looked so small. They are incredibly stretchy. They zipped right up, and I can even pull them away from my waist. The front pockets are a bit silly since they are designed to look more like back pockets, but I don’t really mind. Overall, they are well made and have so far held their color and quality after being washed. For $20, they feel competitively priced and like a good value. **I included pictures of them on my daughter. I’m 5’57”and 150lbs and the size med fit me well, she’s 5’5” and 110, and they fit her as hipsters, but were too loose at her waist. (She haas the pink socks)

I ordered these jean shorts and was pleasantly surprised by the fit. For reference, I’m 5'2" and about 150 lbs, and I ordered a size 8, which fit me well. The shorts have a comfortable amount of stretch in the waist, so they don’t feel restrictive when sitting or moving. The length goes down to my knees, which I really like because it feels more comfortable and appropriate for everyday wear. The denim feels sturdy but not stiff, and they’re easy to wear for longer periods of time. I think these are comfortable, well-fitting jean shorts and a good value for the price.

LOVE IT! Totally purchased on a whim and it fits like a glove. I’m 5’2 and about 103 pounds and it has some room to stretch too if someone wanted it tighter. It is short but not too short for my liking plus in the photos I’m wearing heels which of course makes it look shorter. Purchased an XS.

I want to clarify that this is not a skort; it is a regular denim skirt. I am 5'1" and typically wear a size 4-6 in bottoms. The medium size fit me well. The skirt is lightweight and very stretchy, which makes it comfortable to wear. It’s cute and can be paired with a variety of tops. Overall, it’s a nice skirt, but I’m not sure about the $24.99 price point.

IWD Denim Mini Skirt is a a well made and comfortable to wear short skirt that features a lower waist, zippered fly, and 5 pockets. At 5'5" tall and weight of approx. 125 pounds, average build. I ordered this denim skirt in, Color: Black & Size: Large, which fits me comfortably and stylishly. The waistline is a little loose fitting, but I went up a size when ordering. I enjoy the way cut of the skirt and the overall fit. It has a flattering fit and is very comfortable. Machine washable, 32% polyester and 3% spandex denim fabric, this is an easy to care for garment that holds its size, shape and color, after laundered and tumble drying on low heat setting. Overall, this black denim mini skirt by IWD is a great wardrobe staple that many would look good wearing. It's a piece that I wear often and feel good in it!

For reference, at the time of this review, I’m 140 lbs, 5’7” and the size Medium fits me well. The denim material is good quality - not too thin, and has just the right amount of stretch. Zipper is metal and smooth. Has "real" front pockets, nice and deep! Plus the back pocket placement is good. Sometimes when the pockets are too far apart, it can make my behind look quite w i d e. The best part is that this skirt isn't too short on me; the hem hits me mid thigh (not upper thigh, as it does for the model in the listing). A nice buy!
Pretty much exactly as pictured. Material is stretchier than I like/is expected but that makes it pretty comfortable. Size is perfect for me. I am 5'1" and 118lbs.

Smart staple as an underlayer with jackets, vests and such.. slat sleeve cut and elastin fit removes excess bulk to offer smooth lines. Full finishing detail gives option to wear on its own and look the part. Solid $10 price- easy care: machine wash cool/line dry to avoid shrinkage. See pics for visual. Size chart is accurate- offers stretch in fabric with length to pelvis for optional back side coverage with ability to tuck in/out. White is universal to easily pair and/or, dress up or down. Meets variety of need. For reference Lg. fit: 40+yr, 5'2", 143lb, 38C in silhouette cut. Hoping info can help you.

I chose this t-shirt in orange and I love the color. It's bright and cheerful, a true orange that's neither too red nor to yellow. It's the perfect orange in my opinion, and as far as pants or shorts go, it's a color that looks great with blue denim, white, khaki, and army green. The t-shirt itself is fairly basic but I like that it is updated with cap sleeves. I've seen cap sleeves all over the place lately, so I like that this tee is a wardrobe staple but is still on trend. The neckline is open enough to be comfortable but it's not low cut. The fabric is mostly polyester, which means it is easy to care for and won't fade or shrink like cotton can. The fabric is not shiny and doesn't feel synthetic; it feels similar to cotton, but smoother. It's really nice-- It's soft, lightweight and breathable. The 5% spandex gives it some extra stretch and comfort. As for fit, I looked at the seller's size chart and order a Large based on my bust measurement. I have about a 39-40 inch bust, so I chose the size Large for a couple of extra inches of wiggle room. That strategy worked, and the T-shirt has a relaxed fit without being oversized. In my photos it looks sort of short, I'm not sure why but probably due to my poor photography skills. The shirt does hang down past my hips, so don't worry about how in my photos it looks almost cropped. Since the fabric is soft and somewhat thin, it is easy to tuck it in or do a front tuck if you find it to be too long to leave untucked. Overall, this shirt is a wardrobe staple with updated cap sleeves, a wonderful-feeling fabric, and a relaxed fit. I know I'll wear it over and over probably 1,000 times.

This jacket is awesome. Period. The fabric is a perfect weight and stretch. It has just enough give to be comfortable but it's not so stretchy that it doesn't look like denim or tailored. It's a bit wrinkled right out of the package but I low tumbled it with a damp towel and then "snapped" it by the shoulders and it was good to go. I'm 5'8, but long waisted, and it hits at my natural waist. Not my hips, so if you're looking for a longer jacket and you're tall this might not be the one. It's not cropped, but it isn't what I'd call long either. It's belly button length. I'm 44dd, hourglass, 220ish, but I got this with the intention of not buttoning it, like a shell layer. I can button it, but it's snug. The shoulders fit perfectly, no tightness even with my arms extended. It lays perfectly and stretches just enough where need be. Fit is so hard to know by images but this fits my frame perfectly! The color matches white accented clothes. The pictures are two dresses that I'd consider white flowers against pure red and mustard yellow just so you have an idea. I'd call this parchment or somewhere between ivory and white. It's a warm tone white, not bleached stark white. It's always so hard to tell so I tried to take pictures that show natural light versus shade. Natural vanilla ice cream is what I'd call it and it matches so much if your wardrobe leans warm. The weight is spring, summer night, early fall. You'll get plenty of use from this jacket. The stitching is SOLID. I have a jean jacket from that brand, you know the one, and this matches that. The pockets are real! It even has interior pockets like a jean jacket. Every button is riveted tightly. This jacket is A+. A++ for extending the life of my sundresses into fall with boots and a belt.

This jacket is PERFECT! 10 stars! I LOVE it so MUCH! I do not like collars not sure why LOL and this denim jacket has no collar! ❤️ I’m buying another one! I’m having a happy fit because it’s so very soft, medium weight thickness is perfectly comfortable , the modern urban chic style is such a casually elegant vibe— creamy ivory color is so very pretty! The 100 percent cotton used to make this jacket is HIGH quality, feels like the softer long cotton fibers! The Medium fit is spot on for my 5’3” height and 225-130 weight. Chest 36B Waist 28” Hips 38” I’m not ever going to put this on any kind of heat in my dryer (just air dry) as heat breaks down and destroys any cotton fibers, also I don’t use harsh detergents-only plant based. It reaches to the top area of my hips somewhat covering up my bum . The sleeves hit me at the exact perfect place that I like—about 2 inches past my wrist, above my knuckles but it’s hard to tell from my photo. My hand looks so awful 😞 after overusing hand sanitizer for 2 straight COVID years on the aircraft (Flight Attendant) that I’m so embarrassed, but wanted to show where the sleeve lengths. There are 2 large, deep interior pockets! Nice! My other denim jackets don’t have that! (see pic) I can’t believe how professionally well made this LUVAMIA Jean jacket is! Even the metal buttons have their name brand on it so it’s obvious they take pride in the little details in their garments! This jacket truly is dept store high end quality. I shop at SAKS ( I hope they make it through bankruptcy!) and NM and BD a lot, and of course Amazon. I highly recommend it. Incredibly well structured and designed, as well stitched. A great Amazon find and a value for my money! Just get it!

This fits so nicely and is very flattering!! I LOVE that the sleeves actually go over the shoulder like a real sleeve!! The buttons in front are great! It's thick too, so it's not see-through but not thick enough to be hot, it's still very breathable. I can't wait to dress this up! (Don't mind the workout attire at the moment LOL) I'm 5'3 140lbs 32DDD; I ordered a small and it's a perfect fit!
Nice casual shirt. I like that the buttons actually work. Got size small and I’m 5’5” 125 lb

These jeans are AMAZING. My butt has never looked THIS GOOD! They are pricey but very worth it. They are long but I'm 5'2 so almost every thing is quite long on me and also the bell bottom was larger than i thought which was a huge plus. I got size 5 in the botton fly style medium wash and the bottom leg opening is 22 inches all the way around. SO AWESOME! If your teetering on the edge of buying them, do yourself a favor and buy them now!! You wont regret it! They are good quality and make u feel so sexy! Sizing is also spot on =) if i could afford it i would buy several pairs!UPDATE: 3/11/18So the jeans are now pilling some and now there is run on the front thigh =( maybe they are too soft? I still love how they look but I guess I will have to be more careful with my other pairs in the future and hopefully this won't happen with them. I really hope they don't cuz I truly love the fit of these jeans, they are the only ones that can make my butt look so darn good! Cuz I don't have a lot to work with lol

I have been eyeing these jeans for a little while, but I couldn't justify spending any money because I had to move, then move, and then move again. Long story short - my mom bought them for me (thanks mom for the jeans and posting my review!) and I'm so thankful that she did! When I put them on I immediately thought “these are more comfortable than any other pair of jeans I have ever tried on or worn”. Then I got a look at them and they honestly fit better than any pair of jeans that I've ever owned! **I am HUGE in the gut area but you can't tell that in these jeans!!! I genuinely love them and would absolutely recommend! *I am 5’8” and 185lbs and ordered size 13*

I love the fact that I am typically a size 10 in pants but can now claim that I wear a size 7. 😉 The fit is great, the jeans are very well made, and they are the perfect length. I also love that they help with the tummy control I require to even feel comfortable enough to wear jeans instead of yoga pants. For reference, I am 5'8, 165 lbs and typically wear US size 10. I ordered a size 7 because that is what Amazon suggested would fit me best.

I was pleasantly surprised with how well these fit. They offer a bit of stretch, which I find comfortable , especially for a high rise jean with so many buttons- and their still snug in all the right places. In the photos, I was 110 pounds and am 5'4 and I felt that the jeans gave me a really great silhouette, when I usually feel like jeans don't give me much figure. I bought these in dark denim, which I think looks absolutely great and is really flattering. And the size I chose was 1, which can sometimes be a hit or miss with brands but nah- these were just right. I was worried about the buttons because I thought they would take forever to unbutton and button but nah- they are surprisingly simple and uncomplicated. I love these jeans so much that I even convinced my friend to buy a pair in the same color. And she really loves them as well. I look forward to trying more pairs from the brand. 😊🧡 Point is- definitely worth it

I really like a lot about these jeans. They are awesome looking when on, but it took awhile to get there. I am 5'3 and around 135 lb on a good day. These bad boys were super long so before I could wear them I had to see a seamstress who hooked me up by taking off several inches. Now the length is perfect. These pants definitely stretch, but they can still be tight up at the top, especially if you are little thicker in the middle. Hence why the buttons look a little stressed haha . My ass is shoved in there because I dared not go up another size and have to take in even more material, but we have to sacrifice somewhere. All in all, I didn't pay much for these and I know if I wanted super high quality I could go elsewhere, but these were cute and affordable and they are made in the USA! That is practically unheard of- so if you are wondering whether to do it or not, I say just give them a try.

These jeans are a good gamble. I’ve gotten some from vibrant that had tears or runs. That were stretched the f out, or so long if need to grow another foot. (Warnings to getting the shortened they shrink and curl up) But this pair turned out good. Just a bit small in the button area. Hopefully the button slots last. Hate for them to pop all the time. I’m 5’4 105lbs and I ordered a size 3. I’d be in love if they were idk 25 dollars but 40 or higher is crazy. I just can’t find any other pants on here this style. Oh and they are Not denim jeans! More jegging material. They are also a bit tight in the top on my pair.

It's so hard to find high-waisted bootcut jeans! I knew these were Juniors, so I ordered a size up (11), but wound up needing a 13. So these are small AND Juniors. The 13 were slightly loose, but I heard these shrink and they did after I washed them in hot water/hot heat dry. Now the 13s fit perfectly!They're almost a little too high for me. I wish they were one button lower. They were about 2-3 inches too long, so I definitely had to get them hemmed. Photos are before hemming or washing.I'm 5'3" and 130 lbs, pear/hourglass body shape. I think I normally wear a 9 in jeans.

I weigh 170 and am 5'4. I love these jeans. They are so comfy and the high waisted fit is perfect for holding the chub I have in. These fit tight just like them in the thighs, but loose in the legs which is exactly what I was looking for. Thy are about an 1-2inches long on me. I have healed boots so thats not a problem for me, but if you mainly wear flats you may want to get them hemmed.

I've been losing weight for about a year and a half. Now all my clothes are way too big and hang off my body, looking horrible. I don't have a lot of money to just keep buying clothes, so I was waiting until I got closer to my goal weight. Now that I am, the hunt begins. Since we can't try clothes on in the store, I'm going to try to do it for us and give you as much information as I can. Money is tight, so every decision is a big one. My history: I was about 260 at my heaviest. All the clothes I bought were loose-fitting on me most of the time. I'm not used to clothes that are form-fitting, tight, or fit correctly. I'm finding that I love the way they look and feel, but I'm not used to the restriction that they cause, like the restriction in the arm and shoulder area of some of the blouses. I need to get used to that. So, keeping all that in mind, my measurements are: 166 lbs, 5 ft 4 in, over bust 39, bust 43, band or under bust 37, waist 39, hips 42 (or the biggest part of my tummy & butt), neck 15, head 22, sleeve length 21, waist to floor 37. Please forgive the selfie stick and my face in some of the pictures, as I'm trying different ways to take proper images so y'all can really see the clothing. I got these in a large size. They looked super small when I pulled them out of the package. But they stretch so I was able to get them on. I’m not sure how I feel about them yet. I have to get used to wearing something so short. The quality of these shorts concerns me. The holes in the shorts are actual holes, and the fabric of the shorts themselves is so thin that if any part of that hole spreads even a tiny bit, I believe the rest of the fabric would easily tear right open. The legs are actually cut as though I cut them to make them shorter. It's clear the fabric threads will continue to pull out from the bottom of the legs. Both seams on each leg do have a stitch to stop at least the seams from undoing any further, but that too feels like an afterthought. I'm also not a fan of the zipper or the button. The quality of both is not that great. Reviewing these shorts, all I keep thinking is that they were meant to be pants, and something happened, so they turned them into shorts, hoping to at least save some part of them. I don't feel like these will last a very long time. I can see them holding up for maybe a summer, but not much longer than a season. Depending on how well the zipper holds up. The positives I can offer are that these shorts stretch easily, provide support and fit nicely, even if they're a bit too small. The material is soft and they felt good on my body. While I’m not sure they’ll last if they do, I think $15 would be a good value for them.

Really cute on! Great length, and as a woman with longer legs, it's nice to finally find some that are cute without making me feel like an old woman. I'm 5'7, 130lbs 32in inseam, I'm wearing a small and they feel perfect. I wasn't in love with the way they look like you literally just cut the legs lol but I plan to maybe roll them a little, or honestly they'll probably soften a little with time/wear. I Feel like they'll still be long enough even if I roll the legs like once or something. There's a fair amount of stretch to them also, they're really comfortable. I am overall really happy with these and excited to wear them when it warms up.

These are a very lightwieght denim short (almost too thin but I still love them). Shorts these days are too short for me and I spend my time pulling them out of my butt or thighs as I walk. But I am too young to commit to the long knee length shorts I keep seeing out there. These were the perfect balance of short but not too short. I didn't feel like an old lady in them, I felt cute and comfortable. Walking in them and not having to pull them down. They fit true to size, and sat nicely on my waist. Love the distressed look and the pockets are all functional, always a plus! Overall, great short for the price ($24 at time of review). I will get a lot of use out of these! For Reference, I am 5'5'', 130-135lbs and wearing a small.

These pants are cute and the reason why I gave only 4 stars is because I ain’t wear a size large and I typically will go up one size because in the past with ordering my clothes on Amazon the same size is actually too small but these are more like a XXL. They’re not real heavy they’re made of really good material and I love the fact of all the pockets but I am 5 foot one and they stand past my feet. Also a good half to a full foot so they’re pretty long the stretch, stretching us in the waist is perfect. The material thickness is perfect. It’s just they are too big these like I said we’re made bigger than they should have been, but I have the perfect person for these. I do recommend them, but definitely get your size. As you will see in the photo how big they are I was so hoping to be able to wear these because these are perfect.

I love these barrel at cargo pants! The fit is incredibly flattering and the army green color is perfect, especially in the fall. The pants do you have a decent amount of stretch and an elastic size waistband as well as a functional zipper and button closure. I like that the barrel is a bit more subtle and subdued rather than and over the top barrel that can make you look a bit clownish. I have worn and washed these pants several times as well as my teenage daughter who loves them probably even more than I do. I have washed these in warm water as well as cold and stuck them in the dryer as well as hung them to dry and no matter what I do, they are good as new after each wash. I haven’t noticed any shrinkage after drying which is a plus because I’m 5’9”. Because of the amount of stretch in these pants, I do recommend taking your normal size and you will still have a nice loose fit. Also, because of the elastic in the waistband, you can wear these higher on the waist as I am in my picture or pull them down lower for a more low rise fit.

These cargo barrel jeans are very trendy and extremely comfortable. The high waist is flattering, and the wide-leg, baggy style is very relaxed. The army green color is a nice change from basic denim. Good quality, no odors. The small size fits me well, and the stretch makes them easy to wear all day. (Reference: 5'2", 120 Lbs, size S).

I'm not sure I like the trend of jeans that make everyone look bow-legged, but they looked comfy and fun so I decided to give them a try. I'm 5'5" and around 155 pounds; normally I wear a size 14-16. The size chart for these had Large for 12-14 and XL for 16, so I went by the measurements. According to those, an XL should have been right, perhaps even a bit snug. Wrong! They were a fair bit too big. They wouldn't have stayed up and I would have had to bring up the hem so I didn't just walk on it. They weren't attractive on me. I ignored the part that said they were non-shrink and washed them in the hottest water and put them in the dryer at its hottest setting. I am here to tell you that if you do that, they WILL shrink. Now they are only a little bit large on me. But I won't be walking on the hems, they're almost right on the hips, and the waist needs an occasional little hitch back up. Or a belt. But overall, now they look nice on me. They have an oddly slimming effect. They are quite comfortable and I enjoy wearing them. I'll probably do another hot wash/dry to see if I can get the fit just a little better. They are a solid pair of pants, well-made, solid stitching, good zipper. Love the extra pockets. Just take a close look at the size chart and try to make your best decision there.
It does run pretty big, I got a size small. 130 pounds, 5’4. I had to tie it in the back.
This was exactly what I wanted. I’m a D cup size, 160, 5’2” and I got the medium. Luckily it has some stretch because it only just fit. But it’s exactly what I wanted and worked great.

Overall impression: (Size L) Comfortable, flattering, stretchy, usable pockets, working buttons, can be worn open or closed. The pocket placement is a bit weird, but works. It holds my whole clump of keys and they don't protrude from my belly because they hang out under my boobs, and I appreciate my [5'4", 145#] profile better with fewer protrusions from my belly. A phone can partially fit, but likely fall out if you bend over. For me, this vest is perfect for dancing, because it's sleeveless and I can keep my lanyard on and tuck my phone in one pocket and keys in the other so they don't swing all over the place when I'm bouncing around. This alleviates so much stress from having to keep an eye on my stuff. Item: Genhoo Denim Vest Sleeveless Jean Vest Top for Women V Neck Button Down Cropped Denim Top with Pockets Fair market value: 36.99 Current price (as of this review): 36.99 Pros: Comfortable, flattering, stretchy, usable pockets, working buttons, can be worn open or closed. Cons: shallow pockets, items fall out when bending over Note: I received this product for free through Amazon Vine in exchange for an honest review.

This is actually pretty comfortable. Definitely true to size and the color is pretty - not a cheap “stretch jean” tone. It washed well and the style is cute, I don’t love the little flare out at the bottom, it would be better if it wasn’t so lose right at the bottom. I’m 5’6” and just under 130#

This is not super cropped but I think it looks shorter on the model because she’s tall. It still is a little short if you get what I’m mean. I’m wearing a L and I could have downsized but I plan to layer this over stuff for the winter and I don’t like things to be too snug fitting I prefer things a little loose , I’m 5’4 , weight 146 ,

This sweater is very much a dupe for a Quince sweater in terms of style, quality, and softness. The design is beautiful and classic and the texture is so soft. Since it is rather thin material and is light-weight, a higher price would have been a bit much, so I do think it's a good value for about $25.00. That being said, it is a very light sweater, with thin material, and is somewhat see-through (see pictures), at least in the color white. I am wearing a nude bra underneath the sweater and you can still see the outline, which I don't love-- this is one reason I'm giving 4 stars instead of 5. Another reason is that I love the detailed pattern, but wish it extended to the back of the sweater, and wasn't just on the front. Finally, it's not particularly wrinkle-resistant as you can see. A lot of my complaints about the item seem to be in large part because of the color I selected. In a different color, you might have these problems, in fact, there are a lot of great reviews of the other colors which you should definitely check out. For reference, I'm 5'5" about 130 lbs. I usually wear a size S or XS in women's tops. I got a size small in the color white.

Dokotoo Women's Casual Long Sleeve Wool Hollow Out Sweaters is a nice addition to the fall wardrobe. This light weight sweater is soft and comfortable with style. The fabric is of good quality and itch feel. The design is stylish for work or out on a day trip. The fit is true to size chart and comes in many colors. Model 5’3” / 115 lbs / 32DDD / size 2-4

This is a well-made, very pretty drop-sleeve sweater that I absolutely love. I ordered the green in size large (I’m 5'4" and 148 lbs. so you can see the fit in my photo). The knit is lightweight, soft to the touch, and drapes beautifully. What really makes it stand out is the delicate detailing at the top—it transforms a simple sweater into something much more special. The design includes tiny openwork "holes," but they’re small enough that with a flesh-tone undergarment, shoulder straps won’t show through. It washed well, and I chose to lay it flat to dry. For the quality, the price is excellent and I’m very happy with this purchase.

The quality of these Madrirdma straight leg jeans is truly amazing! The jean material is soft, stretchy and so comfortable! They’re now my favorite pair of jeans. In the warmer weather when I wear sandals I like to roll them up at the ankle; they look so cute on me. I’m 5’7” and about 175lbs. and a size large fits me nicely. You can’t go wrong with these jeans especially at this price.

5’6”, 170#’s Ordered XL High waisted fit provides good tummy control. I can sit without a muffin top spilling over the waste band. These jeans are comfortable and have good stretch. The design provides a slimmer appearance, especially in the thigh area. I wish they were about an inch longer or you could order in lengths. Also, there is one seam on the back that doesn’t line up (see photo).

I’m 60, 5’5” and weight 160lbs. My waist is approx 35,” hips 42” and my thighs are 22” around. Obviously I carry extra weight in my tummy and I’m also extremely short waisted. Not being familiar with this brand, per the size guide provided, I decided to be cautious and ordered XXL. Turns out I could easily wear my normal XL or maybe even a Large. These have a LOT of stretch to them. However they’re super comfy and have a lot of great little details (pls see images). These remind me a lot of my favorite pair of boyfriend jeans from the brand Carve which retail for approx $100. I wore these for 7 hrs straight and due to their stretchiness I was able to wear lower on my hips which works out great for my short waist - it helps to give the illusion that I actually have a longer waist! I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review, but purchasing at full price in XL. At the time of this review a 15% off coupon is available brining my cost down to approx $45. I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest choosing your normal size or lower. For example, if I wanted these to be slim/snug fitting, I’d order these in Large. I should add I washed these on room temp water & dried on med. There was minimal shrinking, if any.

I followed the sizing chart and got the XXL as I’m in between 14 and 16. It fits a little loose, which might be due to the stretchy fabric. I love the button fly; it reminds me of my high school years. They’re comfy and can be dressed up or down (but mostly for casual wear). The length is just right for my 5’5” frame. They did sag a bit when I went out for a few hours so I’ll need to wear a belt.
Absolutely cute pants. I’m 5’5, and a size 7, it fit perfectly, I just wish they were high waisted so I didn’t have to constantly pull them up with every movement I make.

Honestly, I did not expect much from a random brand of jeans. Bought them for a concert. As you can see by the picture, they aren't navy. Really faded color, even before washing. I am 5'2 and generally wear a size 14 short/petite. My butt isn't the biggest, and my legs are skinnier than my waist size. I bought both L and XL. While the large 'fit', they were really snug, so the XL it was. Flattering on my legs, but really long. Had to cuff them a few times over. Can't say they really butt lifted, but they did hug it pretty nicely. Hubs liked them. Will I wear them again? Meh. Would I buy them again for a concert? Probably.

I'm pleasantly surprised by the fit! Typically skinny jeans don't fit my thighs, but these have enough stretch there's not an issue! I am short with a 28 inch inseam so they were long but that was really easy fix by rolling the bottom up twice. I'm 5'3" 148lbs and got a small as most reviews suggested sizing down. I typically wear mediums in most clothes! The small is perfect, if you want the fit that shows on the main pic!! Definitely getting another pair!!!

This is the best leggings I’ve ever had. I love how they make me look so much slimmer. They keep everything in and the material is extremely high-quality! I am a huge leggings fan and this by far are top on my list. I just ordered two more pair, let’s not forget the price is unavailable as well for such an amazing quality. I am 5’7 tall and am 175 pounds Ordered XS-S and I feel 10 pounds slimmer instantly wow!

Customers should know that these are definitely ALL OVER COMPRESSION leggings, from waist to ankle! The material is soft &hugs the body beautifully. The elastic back pocket is great & it’s pretty big so u can fit your phone, money, credit cards or whatever u can fit in there. They are NOT see through. As for the built in butt lifting band, it is noticeable to me… only bc I know it’s there. If someone is admiring your leggings they probably won’t point it out, or ask about it unless u show them. (hope that makes sense, lol) The quality of these leggings is amazing. I bought mine for $19. Others are selling them for $25 and ⬆️. There is one thing I do not like about them is the bottom aren’t hemmed. I’m worried that they will fray after I wash them(?)…idk. I bought them in Black. I am 5’4” tall & weigh 165. I bought a M & they don’t fit how I’d like them to. I thought I bought a L-XL. I’m going to exchange for the correct size.Also, I know that Prime is July 8-11 & u just might get em at a greater price! Even if you don’t… get them for less than $19, they’re WORTH IT! Hope this helps! 😊

Fantastic leggings! The fit and feel are magic! Compressive and supportive without giving up comfort and wearability! They have a high waist band, with a large back pocket that will easily fit your phone, so you can be hands free and on the go! They also offer an easy cut tab on the ankle for us short girls! I love the grey color and I love the booty lifting support! They fit pretty true to size. I typically wear a M in leggings and these are a size M/L. I’m 5’3” and weigh about 155 lbs.

I am 5’4 and around 115 pounds. I originally ordered the small and had to return to size up. I was confused at first with the bottom of the legs. After talking with the seller, I was informed that the cut of the bottom of the legs is designed in such a way that you can cut to your desired length. The tummy control is amazing and I love it!!!! They fit so well around my waist, hips, booty and stomach. I love the fit around my waist and I give it 10 stars!!!!
I got these pants in a 2xs. They are so light (thin) making them breathable. I love them length of the pants as well I’m 5’6 and 268lbs I wear a 20-22 these was a perfect fit. They even had stretch

Here are my current measurements: Weight: approximately 197 lb. Height: 5 ft 4.75" Waist: 37" Lower belly (over the belly button): 40.75" Hips: 45" I bought an XL based on the size chart stating that it would fit someone with a 37.8" waist and 47.64" hips. As you can see from the pics, it doesn't fit right. There's no stretch whatsoever so it has me wondering if I bought one size up would it "fix" my issue?" I can't even tell to be honest. I just know it doesn't have that oversized baggy look the model has... and if I ordered a bigger size to get the legs bigger, I wonder if the waist would be too wide?! That's the real concern. So Unfortunately I won't be keeping this.

These jeans are absolutely stunning. I’m talking show stopping statement piece! I couldn’t order my suggested size of a xl so ordered the xxl instead, and even though I sized up these jeans still fit me comfortably. They have great stretch, I can fit both of my arms in the jeans with my legs and still have extra stretch room. They’re absolutely amazing. They’re true to size and they didn’t shrink or fade after washing. I’m thoroughly impressed and I’ve already ordered a second pair but this time a size smaller. I’m 5’8 and 240lbs. The photo you see is of me in a size xxl, but a xl would be more of the fit I’m looking for. One photo is of me with my arm in my pants to show how much they stretch and the other photo is without the stretching. These jeans didn’t shrink or fade after washing.

My wife liked the feel and fit of these pants. The elastic waist is comfortable, and the wide-leg style is flowy and casual. The material has a nice texture and the pockets are deep and functional. The only issue she mentioned is the length; at 5'1", they are much too long for her. If your legs are on the shorter side, be aware that they run long. Otherwise, they are a great pants.

Im a fuller body size, they fit comfortably without looking sloppy. Not too long, I’m 5’5” and they hang appropriately. The texture is like crape paper. Color is flattering. Washed well with no shrinkage or fading.

These pants were just ok on me. I like flowy pants, but these hung down pretty far in the crotch area which made them baggy not flowy. I am short waisted so maybe this is just a problem on my frame. I am a size 2-4 and 5'4" and ordered the size small. Maybe on someone who is taller or at least not short waisted these would hang better.I did like that they were a nice cool breezy material that would be great for summer. The textured pattern really elevated them in my opinion and made them nicer than most summer weight pants. I wish they hung better on me because they are really cute pants.

These pants are so comfortable and soft! They come with an elastic waistband, and fit nice and loose. They fit true to size, and the length is perfect for me (I am 5'7"). These pants are also not too thick, so they can be worn in the summer or spring without overheating. I plan to wear them on my vacation to Mexico next month. The color is also true to what you see online, the picture I took just doesn't convey that very well. I am very pleased with the size and fit of these pants!

I was pleasantly surprised when I opened the package! This is a nicely made product that fits perfectly. The description says it’s oversized. I have to disagree. I am 5’3” and 115 lbs. If I had on a sweater or sweatshirt under this jacket, a scarf, and any other warm weather additions, it would be just the right size. The jacket has patch pockets, a heavy lining, and a good zipper. One of the best things about it is it’s washable. How many other nice-looking jackets can you toss in the washer and dryer?

I am 5’4” and 158lb. I don’t feel very confident in shorts since gaining 30 pounds, however these are perfect. I have a short waist and cannot fit into this high waist trend. These are a mid rise and sit perfectly above my hips. The length is ideal. Shorts that are too long are not flattering. These fall just below the chub and prevent chafing. I got a size 11 (I usually wear a 10) and they fit great. They do stretch and loosen up throughout the day, but that keeps them comfortable. I am shaped like a box so it works. However, if you have a small smaller waist, you might want to size down.

SO COMFY AND CUTEEEE ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ online shopping can be so tough especially as a tall girl with no butt ! but these fit perfectly on the first try!!! (5’11 ; ~120 lbs ; my waist sizes in jeans and shorts can range from 0-4 😭) I ordered these shorts in a size 1 and i love the waist fit, not too tight but still fitted. they are quite stretchy and made of good fabric material 💕 not too heavy or thin material. the length is very good as well, not too short. They do nothing for my butt, which isn’t saying much bc it’s hard to do 🥲 but they fit well through the butt and are not too baggy/don’t give diaper butt which is a massive win!! Good mid-low rise. Slightly flared at the bottom which I love so it isn’t tight on my thighs 😄 Only minor complaint is that the pockets are a little small and hard to access because of the buttons but 100% worth it because they are my favorite shorts !! I love the way the pocket style looks, will be ordering more in other colors!!! 😁😁[for reference, the boxers worn in the pics are pretty high-mid to high rise and can be left shown or tucked into the shorts]
Im 5'3" about 115 I bought a size 1. Run a little big. I like the gray. Soft material. Im happy with them. I like the length. Would buy it again or in other colors.

The most comfortable and cute shorts ever. Stretch is perfect. No cutting into skin, no muffin top. Lol. Brings me back to the 90s. They are soo cute. Love the pockets too. Flattering on belly. I'm between sizes rn. I'm 5ft 5, 190 lbs and 15 was a great size but I coulda sized down. Will buy more for sure. The manufacturer sticker on front pocket leaves adhesive that the wash doesn't fix. I've bought 2 pairs because I love the shorts, fit, stretch. Just perfect but stuck with the residue from adhesive. Bummed about that. I wish they wouldn't sticker them

These are perfect in every way, the fit, the colors, and the price. Although I’ve learned to get them on sale at $17-19 ! stretching throughout the day is pretty bad I’m 5’8 and 130lb I wear a 3. Years ago I was 152-155 I wore a 5 but they still start falling off. I wear a belt with them. THE WORST PART… they get these strange stretch marks. Every single pair I’ve ever had (maybe 20+?). I don't have a strange figure, I don’t wash them hot or use heavy detergent, I can’t for the life of me figure out why. Anyway. I keep buying them. I just know they’re eventually garbage. They’re just so comfortable, and the pockets are great. They’re great for work. I just wish they’d start making them in better material.

I'm in love with these shorts! Perfect for petite ladies! I bought them in black and loved them so went back and bought them in gray and blue. So comfy and a great short length. As others mentioned, I can't wear them twice or they get stretched out but bounce right back after washing. I'm 5' tall and 135 lbs and the size 7 is great for the comfort I'm looking for.

These fit perfect and are so comfy! I’m 5’6 and athletic - weight: 155-160 pounds. I ordered a size 7 as Amazon suggested. I’m normally a medium bottom. I’ve been trying to find shorts that aren’t high waisted or high mid rise because I absolutely hate that fashion trend and I find them very uncomfortable . These are perfect ! I will be ordering more !
For reference I’m 5’1 112lbs & got small & fits perfect!! These shirts are so cute and you cannot beat the $15 price!

The blooming jelly blouse in camel color is very pretty. It feels silky and soft and I like the details at the shoulders. The blouse is not see through and I am wearing it without a cami under. I am 5'2" tall, 135lbs, 36" bust, 30" waist and size Large fits a little big. I think a size Medium would be a better fit from the sides of the sleeves and from the chest but even slightly bigger on me I like the blouse. The camel color makes it easy to coordinate with dress pants or jeans and is very office appropriate. I like the fabric quality because it can be washed over and over without fading or looking old. I wash the blouse using a cold, delicate cycle and allow it to air dry. I use the steamer to remove wrinkles.

I'm 5 foot 2, and these were just a little bit long for me. They'll probably work better with boots, than with the comfy shoes I'm wearing in the pictures. If you're closer to 5 foot 4 or 5 foot 5, these will probably be the perfect length for you. There was plenty of room in the legs, and the entire waistband is elastic, so there's definitely some room for fluctuating weight. I ordered the medium, even though the size chart said my 29-30 inch waist should be in a size large, but the medium had plenty of room for me. The material is similar to a soft denim, so these pants will definitely work for cooler weather. So far, the construction seems decent quality, especially for the price.

My 12 year old is 5’8” and 120lbs, yea twelve. I purchased the petite large and they will work with a belt. The length fits perfectly, maybe too perfect as she won’t throw them in a drier for fear of loosing any length. The waist fits too big and she will need a belt. The quality seems good enough and they feel very durable with no stitching concerns. Come vacuum packed and therefore very wrinkly straight from packaging. Good length, good style, big waist.

For reference i'm 5'1 and 150 lbs ordered size M The length fit was just right. Hard to find pants that don't drag. The material is soft with a slight stretch making them very comfortable. Cargo style with front, side and back functional pockets. The color matched my skin tone and looks just like picture. Would recommend 10/10

I really wanted to like these & they were so close. They fit true to size in the waist & hips, but they were so long. I'm 5'4" and these pooled on the ground, even with shoes. They do claim a 32" inseam but since they didn't look ridiculously long in the images, I'd thought I'd try them. Nope - way too long. Super cute otherwise though! Sadly I had to return. It would be nice if they had pics with someone with average length legs. I generally wear regular length pants, so my legs aren't even super short.
It’s ok! I’m 5’2” , 133 pounds, and it’s too long, also when they said high waisted, not kidding, they are. Good fabric, quality. I say those are for tall and skinny women, not for me

I have lost weight and was excited to order a size down. The waist was great. Pictured is my old size 14 on top of the new size 12. The length was way to long and for straight leg cut they were very very wide. I am 5’6.

I am 5.5, 150 lbs. The size 10 was a great fit except for the length. A 30 inch inseam is too long for my height. I will have to have the pants hemmed about 2 inches. I give a 5 star for comfort. I haven't washed the jeans yet, but I did wear them all day and found them to be very comfortable.

These jeans are great! They have a bit of stretch which is so comfortable. They do run a bit long (I'm 5'7" 135 lbs) and bought the size 6 regular. With boots/ heels they'll be perfect, but with flats the hem is a bit too long. Will see how much they shrink in the dryer. But overall I am very happy!!

Where's my late 90s, early 2000s teens at? We are late thirties, early forties now. We grew to LOVE our high rise jeans. We accepted skinny jeans as any good young adults would. But don't y'all miss the nostalgia of our wide leg, drag the floor jeans!? I did. I was tired of the uncovered ankles. (Where did our 2 inches' worth of denim fabric go?) These jeans are IT for casual. I get the wide leg. I get the slight stretch. I get THE HIGH RISE, GLORY DAY!! Are they going to get soaking wet if I wear them in the rain? Yep. Are the back leg hems going to fray from walking on them? You betcha. I can't wait. Height 5'9, weight 305ish, size 22 with a little room to spare.

I am currently 3 months postpartum with my third baby. My current style has morphed into a combination of Adam Sandler and cave goblin. I wanted to find some easy outfits that are comfy, work well with nursing and that I look more presentable in to run out for errands or school drop off. I wanted some tops that I could layer with flannels and leggings and this shirt was a home run. My pre-pregnancy size was L-XL so I went with XL since I prefer a more oversized fit with this type of top. It is so comfy, great for nursing and throwing on after a shower. It looks great with my flannels and vests - I live in Minnesota and layering is life 75% of the year. I am 5’9” so tops like this don’t always cover the behind but this does just enough and flows well- as seen in the picture. The only issue is there was pilling after the wash- which I actually don’t mind. No issues with wrinkles or this beige color being see through. It’s a good thickness while maintaining softness. This was definitely a win for me and I plan on purchasing more when I decide on colors!

I can’t quite figure out why I don’t love it. I think it has more to do with my body and not the shirt itself. I would probably love it if I had a flatter stomach. But this shirt pronounces my belly more. The sleeve length is great (which is rare) and I love the color. I found the shirt to be a little longer than I like and I wish it had more of a silhouette that shaped a women’s body. Not bodycon tight, but just a bit more flattering to our hourglass figures. The thickness is expected for a long sleeve tshirt but would be better if it was slightly heavier or double-lined so you didn’t see your bra straps. It drapes/flows nicely but just not for my body type. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 160 lbs, and have a belly. I ordered the large.

I really like the feel of this top. Very soft and light weight. The length is good, but no where near the length it shows in the picture. Also, the thumb holes are rather snug, but workable. I’m 5’5” and 143lbs for reference.

I love this shirt! Just wore it for the first time to run to town. It's super soft and fits very well. I'll surely purchase more colors! For reference I'm 5 foot 145 and I purchased a medium. Purchase your normal size. I always wear an undershirt but that's my personal preference.

These jeans are comfy and cute, with a relaxed wide-leg style that I really like. I’m 5'8", around 230 lbs, and ordered a 2X. They fit a little tighter than the stated size at the waist, while being loose from the thighs down, which gives them a nice laid-back look. If you're plus size or have a larger belly, I'd consider sizing up if possible. They do have a traditional jean waistband with a zipper, button, and belt loops, plus a functional drawstring. The drawstring works like an extra adjuster and is a nice casual detail, but the waist and rise area itself doesn’t have a lot of structure or compression, so these don’t smooth or hold the belly despite the promised “figure enhancing silhouette.” The denim feels like a typical cotton stretch jean, not super thick, but substantial enough that they don’t feel flimsy. Length was great for me, which I was happy about since I was worried they’d be too short based on the photos. Overall, these feel like real jeans with a more relaxed, comfortable fit rather than shaping denim. Great value for casual days, errands, or when you want jeans that don’t feel restrictive.

4.5!!! I love these jeans! I ordered a size small (I’m between a size 2-4) in the color classic blue. For reference I’m 127lbs, 5”2! They do run a tiny bit bigger FYI but they fit comfortably. I don’t love the hump (gaping/fabric folding) that forms on the back (I’ve circled the area on the pictures) but it is not a deal breaker. I love the lace that’s intergraded in the jeans, it adds a unique touch. They are of stretchy material which I love. I expected a more flared look but I may just be more on the curvier side making these more straight leg… regardless I love the look. Overall a great find! I’m excited to style these bad boys :)

I’m in between sizes (8 & 10) so ordered a medium. They fit a bit large plus have stretch so I might could have sized down. I’m 5’2” so they are too long on me. Will have to hem. I love that they are dark black, not washed black. The drawstring is not needed and makes the jeans (especially black ones) look strange. I easily pulled it out. I plan on wearing a belt (it has belt loops) so you can’t tell where the drawstring was. They are comfortable.

I love Luvamia and these did NOT disappoint! Know that Luvamia denim often comes pretty tight and then loosens up with the first wear. Even after washing and drying in the regular laundry, it tightens back up some but never as tight as when you first put it on. So don’t panic if your item seems a little too tight at first. Wear it around the house for a bit to see if it loosens up okay for you. I ordered these in my usual size. I am wearinf a medium and I am 5’5” 169# in these photos. I was just about to have a tummy tuck when I got these, so I was okay with them being a little snug around the abdomen knowing that would be going away. The wide bottom legs are very trendy and I love the beachy blue color along with the tie at the waist. The fabric is flexible and soft yet retains its shape nicely and resists wrinkles really well. These are a little pricey compared to many other Luvamia products, but their products are always high quality and last forever do I don’t mind paying a little more.

I'm really impressed with these jeans. they are very well made and excellent quality. I expected them to be thin, but not at all. They are just right. They fall easily, rather than get bagged up. Even the length is great. I'm 5'3" and they don't drag on the floor. I typically wear a size 4 (edging towards 6) and a small fit me very well. They washed great, too. I'm not a huge fan of wide legged pants, but these are great. They work with a casual look or more professional. Great all around jean.

I’m giving 4 stars for the style, stretch and color options. I really want to give 2 stars for the fit at the top of the vest and near the armholes. It’s hard to explain the issue but I will try. I’m a 42DD and 195lbs. Amazon said best fit for me would be XL but I listened to the reviews and the size chart and ordered a XXL. It fit great across the chest buttoned up and no puckering at the buttons. However, the gap from the top of my breasts to the shoulders was huge! I could fit my whole hand in the gap and still have room to move it around. I thought, ok, I’ll order a XL just to see. The XL is too snug across the chest, but still had a big gap in the same area. Maybe someone with a smaller chest wouldn’t have this issue but this vest definitely isn’t for larger breasted women. I hate it doesn’t work for me and I’ll be sending both back. The vest seems quality made and I like the weight too. The style is very cute and versatile. Hope I find one similar to this style and the fit is good.
Exceeded my expectations! I ordered a small in medium blue and I love it. It fits great! I love the stretch - super comfy! (I'm 5'6", 128lbs, 36c)

Pleasantly surprised! Nice, thicker material. Very stretchy! I’m 5’9, 160lbs and a 36DD and the Medium fit perfect. Not too tight in bodice or chest. Since there is a lot of stretch, it is a little forgiving. Color was great and accurate to picture.
I love it. I am 5’6”, 137lbs. The app recommended medium but I got small because I wanted it fitted feeling. It fits perfectly!
Cute! 5'3, 208lbs, D cup. I ordered xxl and doesnt gap in the front.

The denim vest in Medium Blue color gives me a nice shape and accentuates my waist. I like to wear the vest with all the buttons fastened. I am 5'2" tall, 135lbs, 36" chest and 30" waist and size Medium fits well from the chest, back and waist. The fit on the side of the arms is a little off because the vest forms a crease at the front side of the arms. I wash the vest using a cold, delicate cycle and allow it to air dry. I steam the vest to remove wrinkles. I enjoy wearing it with skirts.

This is a cute denim vest I got for my wife. It buttons fully up the front and is tapered in at the waist to keep it from looking boxy. The denim is 75% cotton with 1.5% spandex and the balance is polyester. This gives it enough stretch to be comfortable, while still not losing the denim feel. This is a versatile piece that can be worn with or without a shirt underneath. My wife is a 34 C and was between the Medium and the Large on the size chart. I opted for the large as she didn't want it to pull across the chest if she had a shirt on underneath. It fits perfectly and I really love how it looks on her.

I love this lightweight denim jacket. I have been looking for a denim jacket with a gray hood and this one is a definite keeper. It’s perfectly oversized. I am 5’3”/130 lbs with a long torso and purchased the small. It is long enough that I can wear it with leggings which is exactly the length I was looking for. It has distressed holes in the sleeves that are so cute. Perfect seasonal transition jacket to elevate casual outfits.
It’s definitely oversized. I’m 5’6, 170 lbs. and it’s way oversized. There is not smell. It’s a cute Jacket but I don’t like the holes in the elbows. No stretch.

Oversized denim jean jacket with cotton gray detachable hood ( Dark denim ) pros: • nice quality Jean material • stylish for those who like rugged looks • oversized; I’m 5’2 and the length is long below my waist ( ordered a Medium) • durable, comfortable, and warm for all seasons • detachable hoodie, held on by inner plastic buttons • nice deep pockets Cons: • Does have an initial smell, not strong but certainly to wash well prior to wear Overall great oversized denim jacket. True to concept/ design of being oversized so size down if you want a shorter fit. Wash first and enjoy long term wear!

The description says oversized, but I was nervous to get a medium, so I went with my usual size, large. It's a little big as you can see in the picture. I am short, only 5' 1", so keep that in mind. The upside of this being a little roomy is I'll be able to wear a thick sweater under it in the winter if I choose to. It's a very nice casual jacket with just the right amount of "distress" to it to give it that well-worn look you'd expect in a jean jacket. It's thick enough to wear in late fall, and probably well into winter if you wear a thick sweater underneath.

These pants are super cute and fit to a “T” so they’re true to size for sure! I’m 200lbs and I wear a large however, in the photo it shows the pants with more of a ridged pant leg bottom and when I received them the hanging tassle things were SUPER LONG and nothing like the photo.. so I just cut them down and now the pants are perfect.
There's a little stretch. I'm 5"8 223 lbs and I'm wearing an XL Overall, nice product
Material is very soft and stretchy. Felt they’re flattering. A tad big in the hips/waist band but still fit without a belt. For reference, I’m 5’7” and about 130-135lbs.
These pants were true to size 5'8 215lbs. They were VERY comfortable and I loved they way they fit.

Initial reaction: seems nice, thick leather, longer than described. The small is listed for 27-30 inch waist, and the medium is listed for 30-34 inch waist. The actual range of the adjustment holes are 27-34 for the small, and 30-38 for the medium. The belts include instructions for how to shorten them, and includes a hole punch to use for that purpose. You could also use the punch to add additional adjustment holes if needed.My only concern is how well the buckle is attached, it uses a single screw type attachment, so that could be a weak point for failure at some point.
Love this. So comfortable, great stretch & color is perfect, I think I got the dark wash. 5’2 138lbs & medium fits perfect. Not too short in the front or back.

Fits way better than I was expecting and looks way more expensive than it is. Perfect fit, shorts in the back are cute and are great for comfort and modesty. Stretchy and doesn’t ride up too bad. Can be dressed up or down and perfect for spring, summer and fall outfits . My measurements are 5’4”, 162lbs with a curvy build (30” waist, 42” hips). I ordered a medium. Definitely recommend.

Fits great and super cute! Nice stretchy but thick material. I am 5’3” and 120lbs. As a mom this is such a practical piece of clothing! It’s super cute look of a skirt without showing your a$$ when you bend over! Also have a bit of “mom pouch” that I feel like is not as noticeable as with most higher waisted skirts. Will order in other colors.

I am 5'8" and my weight fluctuates between 150-155 -excuse the mess, I was in the middle of organizing drawers, so obviously the contents are all strewed about in this picture I took- These are so comfortable and so cute. The material is stretchy which makes them really comfortable. The material is light enough to not be binding, but thick enough to be durable (if that makes sense). I have washed them twice so far and they didn't shrink at all. Zipper hasn't been an issue, it zips without catching. I love the color of these. I wanted lighter pair and that's exactly what I got. I think I want to order a darker pair next.

Love these shorts. So cute. I was borderline on sizing for 27. I ordered the 27 originally and could get them on but they were tight so I returned them for the 28s. The shorts have ZERO give so if you are close to the end of the measurements size up. I recently lost weight and am in new sizing. I think I'd be a 4/6 in regular sizing shorts. I'm 5'6 123 lbs.

I'm 5'4" and weigh 130 lbs. I really like these shorts. The denim is comfortable and softer than another pair I purchased on Amazon at the same time. The fringe on these shorts was longer than I wanted. In fact, one side was longer than the other. but I cut them both down and am happy with them.

I’m always weary of buying clothes online especially pants but with me being pregnant with my second child and not having a maturity store nearby has forced my hand. That being said I am already in love with these pants. They are perfect for the women who are on the taller side of life. I am 5’6 and extremely tiny so it’s extremely hard to find pants that fit in general. I highly recommend these comfy pants and will be purchasing a second pair.

I ordered these maternity jeans for my daughter. Her baby is due in December. I got her a size large because she wants to make sure her clothes are roomy and accommodate her growing shape. Although I love these jeans and the way they are cut, I’m deducting a star because they seem to run a little small. This large fits more like a medium in my opinion. I’m posting a photo of myself wearing these pants and I’m not the one that’s pregnant. I weigh 133 pounds currently and I’m 5‘2“ tall. The jeans are plenty long, and I’m sure they will work on my daughter who is 5‘6“. They should fit her right now because she is only five months along. However, I’m thinking if you truly are needing size large, then you need to order this in an extra large. These are snug against my hips and thighs. At my size, you would think that they would be a little more roomy.

I have to give these pants 5 stars. They are incredibly comfortable and stretch well without losing their shape. I forgot until I tried them on that they were flared, and they sure do have a fun silhouette! The stomach panel is really soft, and I love that it is a silky material that will slide nicely underneath shirts as opposed to bunching. At 5’9 I kind of wish they were slightly longer, but honestly they look exactly like they do on the model in terms of length. Will definitely be getting plenty of wear out of these!

The pair of maternity jeans are so comfortable to wear. The medium fits so well and offers more room to grow as it is stretchy. The fabric is soft and not itchy on my sensitive skin and all stitchings are done well. The inseam is also the right length for my height 5’3”. It washed well in the washer and tumble dried low so no shrinking. Overall, these are great for its price and quality.

This fit true to size which I'm always nervous about when plus size. The belly panel is very soft and comfortable. I love that it has adjustable elastic along the top of the band so you can fit it just right to your body. The style is cute and these are a good price point. I typically wear a 2X in clothing and that size fit me well. I am 5'6"and the jeans hit the floor. They shouldnt drag once I have on tennis shoes and would still look good for anyone a couple inches taller than I am.
Love it Gained a lot of weight after babies. 168lb 5’6” Size 8-10 Used large
Love Love Love. For reference I’m 5’4” 130 I bought a medium. They have the right amount of stretch and the length is great. They feel great on. I’m going to buy another color.

I’m usually a size 6 but got these in an 8. I should have gotten a 6 but I’m keeping them anyway. I can wear them a little lower given that they are slightly big. They are very cute and well made. I am 51 years old and tall (5’10) and I do not feel insecure with the fit or length. The front pockets are super cute! Very happy with these!

I am 5ft 165 38d 34 waist 44 hips. I got the 14 and they fit well. There was a bit of stretch in the band and fabric they are comfy not tight. Front and back pockets are pretty deep. The front one go almost down to the end of the hem. I got the Iceland Blue. Samsung s23 6.1 inch fits in both pockets.

These shorts have a really unique cut giving them a little extra flare at the thigh. I do like that they have a string/rope waistband in case they are slightly large however they have absolutely no stretch to make sizing more forgiving. they ride very high landing on the natural waist. I am 5‘6“ tall weigh approximately 145 pounds and I am wearing the size 6. I think I have about maxed out although I would not want to go up a size for fear that they would look baggy. I do believe they are sized accurately. The quality is outstanding – these are real denim jeans. Real pockets are always a bonus. I am just missing that a little bit of stretch for added comfort. I hope this is .

I am 5'3" about 130 lbs, and ordered the white in size 6. They fit loose and comfortably. The waist is adjustable which and therefore the shorts ride lower on the waist. I like the length and do not find them to be short shorts.

I'm almost 5'6, 145 lbs and I ordered a medium. This dress is so cute and comfortable. It's super stretchy, and good quality material. The straps are also adjustable. I would size down for a stick to you dress, I perfer a little give. The medium still fits nicely but I could've gotten away with a small. Can dress up or down and I absolutely love it. Will be buying more for sure.
This dress is adorable! Bought it for the Houston Rodeo and it’s perfect! It squeezes in all the right places. I’m 5’3 115 lbs and bought a small.

ALMOST perfect, just needed a different size. While I don’t have the “best body” I do not like how much of my tummy shows in this dress as it is very snug. I went with what a bunch of reviews said to size down as it was said to be a very stretchy material. While it is pretty stretchy, I should’ve just gone for my normal size. I am 5’1” 175 lbs 38C and I purchased the Large, returning and getting the XL. Will update review once I try the XL. Dress itself is super cute, hits just over my knee and the slit is also a cute touch. Zippers in the front and back seem to be strong as well.

Super comfortable dress. I never size down but i wish I did for thos one. It fits really nice but I wanted the sexier/tighter look. I am 5'7 and average around 140 lbs. I have hips but in the dress you can't tell lol. I'd definetly recommend it and am okay keeping the medium as it is super comfortable and cute. Maybe small would be too tight, not sure..also zippers work perfect.
Thin Clingy I’m usually a medium but do not like it hugging. The rolls aren’t too attractive! Wonder if a large would be too big?? I’ll be returning. I’m 5,5” 144lbs 36c for reference

Absolutely great shirt!! Feels great on. I bought a black and a pink. The pink is kind of a peach. It’s beautiful. I’m 5 foot 2. 137 pound and a medium is perfect. The thickness of this shirt is like you paid 50.00 for it. Seriously. I will be buying these for Christmas gifts for sure. LOVE THEM!!!!
The sizing is way off. Im 5'5. 135 lbs, average build and it was very loose. Size down. Also the bottom holes were too small for the buttons to even pass through.

I’ll admit, I didn’t expect to love this shirt as much as I do — but it’s quickly become a go-to in my closet. I ordered the wine red in a size small (I’m 5'9", 126 lbs, 34" bust), and it fits beautifully: true to size, flattering, and just the right length to pair with skorts, jeans, or leggings. What Makes It Stand Out: Fabric Feel: It is buttery soft with just the right amount of stretch. It feels great against the skin and holds its shape. Design Details: The silver buckle detail at the neckline adds a subtle touch of elegance — perfect for dressing up a casual outfit without going full glam. Length & Fit: The longer cut is a win for taller frames. It’s loose enough to be comfy but still gives a flattering silhouette. Color Pop: The wine red is rich and vibrant — ideal for fall, but honestly wearable year-round. Perfect For: Dinner dates, casual outings, or even layering for cooler weather. I wore mine to a restaurant that I knew would be cold (AC), and it kept me cozy without feeling bulky. Care & Durability: Machine washable (yay!), and it comes out looking just as good. No shrinkage, no fading — just easy maintenance. Style Tip: Try pairing it with khaki skorts or high-waisted jeans and a statement necklace for a chic, effortless look. I also plan to wear with leggings too! Bottom line: This shirt punches above its price point. It’s soft, stylish, and versatile — everything you want in a casual top with a little extra flair.

For reference: 4’ 10”. 38F. I bought a navy large and it was too loose. I’ll wash it and dry to see if it shrinks a little. If I buy again I’ll buy a medium size. Also it’s a little see through. I recommend wearing a tank top underneath for extra warmth and coverage.

Runs big. Ordered a small in the beige. 5’2 140lbs. Drapes nicely but going it shrinks a tad when I wash it. It’s also long but I wear most of my tops tucked in. A coworker asked if it was Ralph Lauren so that’s a win. It’s 80 degrees here and it was just the right lightness to be outside. Soft. Great for work. Will buy on another color.

Another beautiful & well made quality short sleeve sweater. This material is soft, comfy and skin friendly. There’s absolutely nothing itchy about this sweater at all. There is a pattern to it that is nicely done but in the black, which I got, it is more subtle than the other colors (my opinion) I washed this in a laundry bag in cold & laid flat to dry. It held up just fine. I got this in a size small & I am 5’ 95 lbs and it’s too big on me. It’s not crazy big but it definitely runs big. This sweater can also be dressed up as in business casual or dressed down with jeans or leggings. In my case it was so comfortable that after I took pics I dressed it way, way down & wore it with a pair of black velvety like sweatpants & slippers. perfection….. :)

I love the color and buttons. The shirt is too large for me. I'm 124lbs and it's very baggy. Also I was hoping the inside would be softer. If this fit me correctly I'd be a lot happier. The outside definitely feels warm and would be nice on a fall day. The material is stretchy.

This is a very soft and lightweight Henley shirt that I think will be just the right amount of warmth for fall and layered for cooler winter days. It is comfortable and nicely constructed and the fit is just right. The color is also really pretty. I plan to wear this often either with jeans or casual skirts. I ordered a M and for reference I am 5'7" and 152 lbs.

First of all, you will notice in the photo that I cut these and turned these into raw hem jeans. Why? Well, I’m short, and these are long. Also, raw hem is in. But also, these are very cute jeans if you are looking for something simple, not skinny jeans, but not huge wide leg. These have a good amount of stretch and are very comfortable. High waist is great. They say to hang dry, but I took my chances in the dryer and so far so good. I usually spend a lot more on jeans and was happy to find these for $40. For reference, I’m 5’2” and 110 lbs and the XS fits me with room to spare.

I’ve tried on so many wide leg jeans trying to find the right fit and have hated all of them. I never thought I’d find such good jeans from Amazon. I’ve purchased 4 different pairs from this brand (Luvamia) and I love all of them. I get a lot of compliments on these jeans. For this style I ordered Medium. I’m 5’1 126lbs. I hemmed these a bit since I’m so short. I’m used to everything being long on me so that’s not a big deal for me.

These have distressed lines and spots yhat I do not like. I am 5'4, 125 lbs and the XS was a bit large, so they do not have the tummy flattening appeal I was looking for. Needed this color, but returning them and looking for slimmer legs found in bootcut jeans.
I love these shorts 10/10 I sized down usually a large and medium fits great it is stretchy material 5’1 175lbs
As advertised. I have a 31 inch waist and 42 inch hips. Large fit excellent with stretch. Only downside is no belt loops but I knew that when ordering. Does not gap in back.
Fits great! Little longer than I wanted but will be great for this fall and winter with boots! Fabric is nice and the back waistband makes more comfortable! I got the Large, 5’5 160!

This jean skirt is really nice quality with a stretchy material that makes it super comfortable to wear. I love that the elastic waistband in the back offers extra give, which is great if you have a smaller or larger waist. For sizing reference: I’m 5’6”, 150 pounds, and typically wear a size 10. I ordered a large but wish I had gone with a medium instead. Overall, it’s a flattering, well-made skirt—I’d just recommend checking the sizing carefully before ordering.

This is a great denim skirt that's perfect for fall and winter. This skirt is good quality and is a medium weight denim. The stitching is as sturdy and durable as jeans. I can wear this skirt with boots, flats, strappy sandals or tennis shoes. Just like jeans, it goes with everything. Topped with a t-shirt or sweater, this skirt is super cute. This skirt is perfect for shopping, fall gatherings, church, the office or even touring while on vacation. More comfortable, more feminine and more put together than jeans. I love this skirt. I ordered my usual size and the fit is great. For reference, I'm 5'7 and weigh between 130 and 135lbs. I normally wear a size 4 or 6 jeans, depending on the brand. I hope this was .

So I was in a pinch to order shorts for my upcoming road trip honeymoon, and I made the mistake of ordering 3 of these shorts in different colors.I'm 5'6", 122 lbs, waist 26", hips 34".. small fits comfortably. The shorts are definitely stretchy and in my honest opinion, overly-frayed.. look good from the back and the side. However, the front is just sub-par. It's almost as it the shorts were cut out from men's jeans.. too much extra fabric by the crotch. Therefore, when you take a step, they bunch up in a very unflattering way. It makes it look like I have external bits, if you know what I'm saying.!Seeing as they're still comfortable and don't look toooo hideous and I simply just don't have enough time to experiment with other jeans right now, I'll keep them. But I definitely won't purchase these again.. and in fact, it's scarring me from making future Amazon clothing purchases again.

In all I bought 4 pair not even 10 minutes after receiving my first pair. The first two pairs were the white and dark medium. They both fit as expected. For reference I bought medium normal size 8, 5'4, 138 pounds. The black and light blue were smaller than the previous 2 pairs. Beware the material % is not all the same nor is the sizing. I ordered medium and the last 2 pairs fit like a small. I posted a picture of 3 of the pairs, all mediums and you can see where they differ in the size. I was refunded for 2 of the smaller pairs. Would love to reorder but feel like the same outcome will happen again. One other thing I don't like is the shorts are on the shorter side and will ride up your butt. I would still recommend these shorts. They are super cute on.

I have large hips/butt and thighs,, but a small waist. Because of this, I can't remember the last time I found denim shorts that fit... until today. These fit almost perfectly! There's even room to spare in the hips! My hips widest point (with feet together) is 41". Waist 2 finger-widths below belly button is 33". Smallest section of waist is 29". I'm 5' 10" and 155lbs. My thighs are a bigger than most (25") - I'm tall, so everything is bigger. I ordered the "A Blue #5" in size large. They fit almost perfectly, and with no gap! Not even a little! It's a freaking miracle! I'm toying with the idea of ordering my next pair in medium, because these do ride up. I'm not sure if that might be because the waist isn't tight enough? I'll be purchasing more as soon as I can. Will update if I get medium.

I initially ordered this product in a small but it was tight. I’m weigh 138, size 4/6, and have an athletic build with larger thighs. Medium worked much better. Soft. The right amount of stretch. A little shorter than I’m used to, but not obscene and only wearing at home. Gave it a 4 in some areas due to a weird crotch issue and because when I put my hands in the pockets to adjust the lining properly, it almost went through the ripped section so I have to be cautious. A great buy and glad I purchased. I’ll keep the small as I’m losing weight and it will fit great in another 5-10 lbs.

I have recently gained some weight so needed a few basics for winter until that New Year’s resolution rolls around…anyhoo I started with this long sleeved, scoop neck tee. I have a really pretty skirt that needed exactly this so when I saw the price, I had to grab it. It is impressive for the $7.99 price tag; it is very soft, low wrinkle and is sewn well. I am 5’5”, 193 lbs and a bust of 36DDD. I would say it runs large. It is plenty long and has a fair amount of stretch in the black, rib knit fabric. There is a cropped version by Biditanle, which you can easily find on their store page. I was contemplating a return, simply due to the longer length but realized this would be perfect for my low waisted pants. It is not see thru and is warm but not bulky. It will make a great layering piece. I tend to like a thicker, ‘supportive’ fabric as I like to wear them tightly and have them not bunched up on my waist. I would suggest a smooth fitting, full coverage T-shirt bra if you wear it snuggly like I do. Size down for that look! There is a wide version of colors which is also nice. Great gifts for the holidays, too.This top is thin and sleek enough (mid-weight not see thru) to wear under a jacket and not add extra bulk while still being warm enough. Notice in the photo the lack of wrinkles. It came out of the bag that way so it will definitely travel well!An average scoop neck, full length T-shirt with a GREAT PRICE…

I’ve purchased several brands of ribbed long sleeved shirts online and this is the softest. It’s longer (so you can wear tucked) but know that it is a form-fitting style. Check the size chart. Im 5’3, 130lbs, wearing a Medium.

I love these pants! Trying to not wear jeans so much and these are a great alternative. The material is very soft and comfortable. I haven’t washed them yet, but I wash anything like these pants in cold water, gentle cycle and hang to dry, so I don’t expect a problem. I’m 5’5” and 138 lbs. The medium fits perfectly.
I love these! They are so cute and comfy! Great alternative to uncomfortable jeans! I got an XL. I’m 5’3” about 200lbs. Fits great! I will have to get more colors!

5’6” 150 lbs. M fit nice, if you want a little less cling, try a size up. Not too thin: don’t see my underwear line, I think the pockets help with that. Nice for the price and I’ll probably hang them to dry
